items.description: Reshape an array to a new shape with the same total element count.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `new_shape` вҖ” `&[usize]`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Returns a new array with data copied from the original.

items.name: transpose_2d
items.description: Transpose a 2D array (swap rows and columns).  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: fill
items.description: Fill all elements of an array with a value (in-place).  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&mut NdArray`. - `val` вҖ” `f64`.

items.name: clone_array
items.description: Clone an array (convenience wrapper). Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `NdArray`.

items.name: bitwise_and
items.description: Bitwise AND of two Int32 arrays. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `b` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_or
items.description: Bitwise OR of two Int32 arrays. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `b` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_xor
items.description: Bitwise XOR of two Int32 arrays. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `b` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_not
items.description: Bitwise NOT of an Int32 array. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_lshift
items.description: Bitwise left shift of an Int32 array by `amount` bits.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `amount` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

items.name: bitwise_rshift
items.description: Bitwise right shift (arithmetic) of an Int32 array by `amount` bits.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `amount` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.

compute::spatial.module_doc: 2D spatial operations and linear algebra for NdArray.  This module is part of Luna2D's `compute` subsystem and provides the implementation details for spatial-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `convolve2d()`, `dilate()`, `erode()`, `flood_fill()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: convolve2d
items.description: 2D convolution with zero-padding (same-size output).  # Parameters - `input` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `kernel` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  The kernel center is at `(kR/2, kC/2)`. Both input and kernel must be 2D arrays. Output shape equals input shape.

items.name: dilate
items.description: Morphological dilation with a Manhattan-diamond structuring element.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `radius` вҖ” `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  For each cell, output is 1.0 if any cell within Manhattan distance `radius` is non-zero in the input, 0.0 otherwise. Input must be 2D.

items.name: erode
items.description: Morphological erosion with a Manhattan-diamond structuring element.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `radius` вҖ” `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  For each cell, output is 1.0 only if all cells within Manhattan distance `radius` are non-zero in the input, 0.0 otherwise. Input must be 2D.

items.name: flood_fill
items.description: Flood fill using BFS with 4-connectivity.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `row` вҖ” `usize`. - `col` вҖ” `usize`. - `val` вҖ” `f64`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Starting from `(row, col)`, fills all connected cells that have the same value as the starting cell with `val`. Returns a new array (does not mutate the original). Indices are 0-based.

items.name: get_region
items.description: Extract a rectangular sub-region from a 2D array.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `row` вҖ” `usize`. - `col` вҖ” `usize`. - `sub_rows` вҖ” `usize`. - `sub_cols` вҖ” `usize`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Returns a new 2D array of shape `(sub_rows, sub_cols)` starting at `(row, col)`. All indices are 0-based.

items.name: set_region
items.description: Copy a source 2D array into a target 2D array at position `(row, col)`.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&mut NdArray`. - `row` вҖ” `usize`. - `col` вҖ” `usize`. - `src` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<(), String>`.  Modifies `a` in-place. All indices are 0-based.

items.name: matmul
items.description: Matrix multiplication of two 2D arrays: (m,k) Г— (k,n) вҶ’ (m,n).  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `b` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<NdArray, String>`.  Uses a naive triple loop. Both arrays must be 2D with compatible inner dimensions.

items.name: dot
items.description: Dot product of two 1D arrays (same length). Returns a scalar.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `&NdArray`. - `b` вҖ” `&NdArray`.  # Returns `Result<f64, String>`.

data::bin_pack.module_doc: Luna2D Binary Pack Format вҖ” format-string based binary serialization.  Provides `write`, `read`, and `measure_size` for the `luna.data` module. Format strings use space-separated named type tokens.  # Format string tokens  | Token  | Type                       | Byte size  | |--------|----------------------------|------------| | `le`   | switch to little-endian    | (modifier) | | `be`   | switch to big-endian       | (modifier) | | `u8`   | unsigned 8-bit integer     | 1          | | `u16`  | unsigned 16-bit integer    | 2          | | `u32`  | unsigned 32-bit integer    | 4          | | `u64`  | unsigned 64-bit integer    | 8          | | `i8`   | signed 8-bit integer       | 1          | | `i16`  | signed 16-bit integer      | 2          | | `i32`  | signed 32-bit integer      | 4          | | `i64`  | signed 64-bit integer      | 8          | | `f32`  | 32-bit float               | 4          | | `f64`  | 64-bit float               | 8          | | `bool` | boolean (u8: 0=false)      | 1          | | `str`  | u32-length-prefixed UTF-8  | 4 + len    | | `cstr` | null-terminated UTF-8      | len + 1    | | `pad`  | zero padding byte          | 1          |

items.name: BinValue
items.description: A Luna2D serializable binary value.  # Variants - `U8` вҖ” Unsigned 8-bit integer. - `U16` вҖ” Unsigned 16-bit integer. - `U32` вҖ” Unsigned 32-bit integer. - `U64` вҖ” Unsigned 64-bit integer. - `I8` вҖ” Signed 8-bit integer. - `I16` вҖ” Signed 16-bit integer. - `I32` вҖ” Signed 32-bit integer. - `I64` вҖ” Signed 64-bit integer. - `F32` вҖ” 32-bit float. - `F64` вҖ” 64-bit float. - `Bool` вҖ” Boolean. - `Str` вҖ” UTF-8 string. - `Bytes` вҖ” Raw byte vector.

items.name: write
items.description: Write values into a binary buffer according to a Luna2D format string.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `&str`. Space-separated format token string (e.g. `"u32 f32 str"`). - `values` вҖ” `&[BinValue]`. Values to write, one per non-`pad` token in order.  # Returns `Result<ByteData, String>`.

items.name: read
items.description: Read values from a binary buffer according to a Luna2D format string.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `&str`. Space-separated format token string. - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`. Source byte buffer. - `offset` вҖ” `usize`. Starting byte offset (0-based).  # Returns `Result<(Vec<BinValue>, usize), String>` вҖ” decoded values and next byte position.

items.name: measure_size
items.description: Compute the total byte size that `write` would produce for the given format string.  Returns an error if the format contains `str` or `cstr` tokens since their encoded size depends on the string content.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `&str`. Space-separated format token string.  # Returns `Result<usize, String>`.

data::byte_data.module_doc: Contiguous byte buffer accessible from Lua.  This module is part of Luna2D's `data` subsystem and provides the implementation details for byte data-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ByteData`. Primary functions: `new()`, `from_bytes()`, `from_string()`, `len()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ByteData
items.description: Contiguous byte buffer for binary data manipulation.  Wraps a `Vec<u8>` with indexed get/set operations and string conversion.  # Fields - `data` вҖ” `Vec<u8>`.

data::compress.module_doc: Data compression and decompression using deflate, gzip, zlib, and LZ4.  This module is part of Luna2D's `data` subsystem and provides the implementation details for compress-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `CompressFormat`. Primary functions: `parse_str()`, `compress()`, `decompress()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: CompressFormat
items.description: Supported compression formats. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Deflate` вҖ” Deflate variant. - `Gzip` вҖ” Gzip variant. - `Lz4` вҖ” Lz4 variant. - `Zlib` вҖ” Zlib variant.

items.name: compress
items.description: Compress data using the specified format and compression level (0-9).  # Parameters - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`. - `format` вҖ” `CompressFormat`. - `level` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Result<Vec<u8>, String>`.

items.name: decompress
items.description: Decompress data using the specified format.  # Parameters - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`. - `format` вҖ” `CompressFormat`.  # Returns `Result<Vec<u8>, String>`.

data::dataview.module_doc: Read-only windowed view into a shared byte buffer.  `DataView` provides typed accessor methods over a slice of a `Vec<u8>` without copying the underlying data. All reads are little-endian. Bounds are checked on every access; out-of-range indices return an error.

items.name: DataView
items.description: A windowed, read-only view into a shared byte buffer.  Uses `Arc<Vec<u8>>` for shared ownership so that multiple views can reference the same buffer without copying.  # Fields - `data` вҖ” `Arc<Vec<u8>>`. The shared backing buffer. - `offset` вҖ” `usize`. The byte offset in `data` where this view starts. - `size` вҖ” `usize`. The number of bytes in this view.

data::encode.module_doc: Base64 and hex encoding/decoding for data serialization.  This module is part of Luna2D's `data` subsystem and provides the implementation details for encode-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `EncodeFormat`. Primary functions: `parse_str()`, `encode()`, `decode()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: EncodeFormat
items.description: Supported encoding formats. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Base64` вҖ” Base64 variant. - `Hex` вҖ” Hex variant.

items.name: encode
items.description: Encode bytes into a string using the specified format.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `EncodeFormat`. - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`.  # Returns `String`.

items.name: decode
items.description: Decode a string back into bytes using the specified format.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `EncodeFormat`. - `text` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<Vec<u8>, String>`.

data::hash.module_doc: Cryptographic hash functions for data integrity verification.  This module is part of Luna2D's `data` subsystem and provides the implementation details for hash-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `HashAlgorithm`. Primary functions: `parse_str()`, `hash()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: HashAlgorithm
items.description: Supported hash algorithms. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Md5` вҖ” Md5 variant. - `Sha1` вҖ” Sha1 variant. - `Sha256` вҖ” Sha256 variant. - `Sha512` вҖ” Sha512 variant.

items.name: hash
items.description: Compute the hash of data using the specified algorithm, returned as a hex string.  # Parameters - `algorithm` вҖ” `HashAlgorithm`. - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`.  # Returns `String`.

data.module_doc: Binary data manipulation, compression, hashing, encoding, and pack/unpack.  Provides `ByteData` for raw byte buffers, plus compression (deflate/gzip/lz4/zlib), cryptographic hashing (MD5/SHA family), encoding (base64/hex), TOML parsing, binary pack/unpack (`pack` module), and a windowed byte-buffer view (`dataview` module).

items.name: bin_pack
items.description: Luna2D Binary Pack Format вҖ” space-separated type-token serialization.

items.name: byte_data
items.description: Raw byte buffer for binary data manipulation.

items.name: compress
items.description: Deflate, gzip, lz4, and zlib compression/decompression.

items.name: dataview
items.description: Windowed read-only byte-buffer view.

items.name: encode
items.description: Base64 and hex encoding/decoding.

items.name: hash
items.description: Cryptographic hash functions (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512).

items.name: pack
items.description: Binary pack/unpack utilities compatible with the LГ–VE2D `data.pack` API.

items.name: toml_convert
items.description: TOML parsing and encoding.

data::pack.module_doc: Binary pack/unpack utilities compatible with LГ–VE2D's `data.pack` API.  Provides format-string based binary serialization for the `luna.data` module. Supports little-endian and big-endian byte order via `<` and `>` prefixes.

items.name: PackValue
items.description: A Rust-side value that can be packed into or unpacked from a binary buffer.  # Variants - `Int` вҖ” Signed integer (i64). - `UInt` вҖ” Unsigned integer (u64). - `Float` вҖ” 32-bit float (f32). - `Double` вҖ” 64-bit float (f64). - `Str` вҖ” UTF-8 string. - `Bytes` вҖ” Raw byte vector.

items.name: pack
items.description: Packs values according to a format string into a `ByteData` buffer.  Format characters: - `<` вҖ” switch to little-endian (default) - `>` вҖ” switch to big-endian - `b`/`B` вҖ” i8 / u8 - `h`/`H` вҖ” i16 / u16 - `i`/`I` вҖ” i32 / u32 - `l`/`L` вҖ” i64 / u64 - `f` вҖ” f32 - `d`/`n` вҖ” f64 - `s` вҖ” length-prefixed string (u32 len + bytes) - `z` вҖ” null-terminated string - `x` вҖ” padding byte (zero)  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `&str`. Format string (e.g., `"<fI"`). - `values` вҖ” `&[PackValue]`. Values to pack in order.  # Returns `Result<ByteData, String>`.

items.name: unpack
items.description: Unpacks values from a byte buffer according to a format string.  Returns all decoded values followed by the next unread byte offset.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `&str`. Format string. - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`. Source bytes. - `offset` вҖ” `usize`. Starting byte offset (0-based).  # Returns `Result<(Vec<PackValue>, usize), String>` вҖ” decoded values and next byte position.

items.name: get_packed_size
items.description: Computes the total byte size that `pack` would produce for the given format and values.  For fixed-width types (`b`/`B`/`h`/`H`/`i`/`I`/`l`/`L`/`f`/`d`/`n`) the values are not accessed, so an empty slice may be passed. String types (`s`/`z`) require the corresponding `PackValue::Str` entry to compute the size.  # Parameters - `format` вҖ” `&str`. Format string. - `values` вҖ” `&[PackValue]`. Values (only needed for `s` and `z` entries).  # Returns `Result<usize, String>`.

data::toml_convert.module_doc: TOML parsing and encoding for Luna2D.  Converts between TOML strings and Lua tables. Supports the full TOML spec via the `toml` crate, mapping types to their Lua equivalents.  This module is part of Luna2D's `data` subsystem and provides the implementation details for toml convert-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `parse_toml()`, `encode_toml()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: parse_toml
items.description: Parse a TOML string into a `toml::Value`.  # Parameters - `input` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<toml::Value, String>`.

items.name: encode_toml
items.description: Encode a `toml::Value` into a TOML string.  # Parameters - `value` вҖ” `&toml::Value`.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

dataframe::frame.module_doc: Core DataFrame and Database types with CellValue cells.  This module is part of Luna2D's `dataframe` subsystem and provides the implementation details for frame-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `CellValue`, `ColRef`, `DataFrame`, `Database`. Primary functions: `is_nil()`, `as_number()`, `as_text()`, `as_bool()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: CellValue
items.description: A single cell value in a DataFrame column.  # Variants - `Nil` вҖ” Nil variant. - `Number` вҖ” Number variant. - `Text` вҖ” Text variant. - `Bool` вҖ” Bool variant.

items.name: ColRef
items.description: Column reference: string name or 1-based integer index.  # Variants - `Name` вҖ” Name variant. - `Index` вҖ” Index variant.  When constructed from Lua, `Index` holds the raw 1-based value. `DataFrame::resolve_col` converts it to a 0-based column index.

items.name: DataFrame
items.description: In-memory column-major tabular data. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `column_names` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `data` вҖ” `Vec<Vec<CellValue>>`.  Data is stored as `data[col_index][row_index]`. All public methods that accept row indices use **0-based** indexing; the Lua binding layer is responsible for converting from 1-based.

items.name: Database
items.description: Named catalog of DataFrames. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `tables` вҖ” `HashMap<String`.

dataframe.module_doc: In-memory column-major tabular data with query, analytics, and SQL.  Provides `DataFrame` for named-column data tables and `Database` for multi-table catalogs. Used by the `luna.dataframe` Lua module.

items.name: frame
items.description: Column-major DataFrame and Database types.

items.name: query
items.description: Filter, sort, group-by, and aggregate query engine.

items.name: serial
items.description: CSV and JSON serialization/deserialization for DataFrames.

items.name: sql
items.description: SQL query parser and executor for in-memory databases.

dataframe::query.module_doc: DataFrame query, filter, sort, join, analytics, and mutation.  This module is part of Luna2D's `dataframe` subsystem and provides the implementation details for query-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `filter()`, `sort()`, `head()`, `tail()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

dataframe::serial.module_doc: CSV, JSON, and LVDF binary serialization for DataFrame.  This module is part of Luna2D's `dataframe` subsystem and provides the implementation details for serial-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `from_csv()`, `to_csv()`, `from_json()`, `to_json()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: from_csv
items.description: Parse a CSV string into a DataFrame. Returns a fully initialised instance with all fields set to their initial values.  # Parameters - `s` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<DataFrame, String>`.  Handles quoted fields (embedded commas, newlines, escaped quotes `""`). Auto-detects column types: tries f64 вҶ’ bool вҶ’ keeps as Text.

items.name: from_json
items.description: Parse JSON (array-of-objects) into a DataFrame.  # Parameters - `s` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<DataFrame, String>`.  Expects format: `[{"col1": val1, "col2": val2}, ...]` Maps JSON types: numberвҶ’Number, stringвҶ’Text, true/falseвҶ’Bool, nullвҶ’Nil.

items.name: from_binary
items.description: Deserialize a DataFrame from LVDF binary format.  # Parameters - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`.  # Returns `Result<DataFrame, String>`.

dataframe::sql.module_doc: SQL-like query parser and executor for DataFrame.  Supports a subset of SQL: SELECT, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, LIMIT/OFFSET, aggregate functions, and JOIN (for Database queries).  This module is part of Luna2D's `dataframe` subsystem and provides the implementation details for sql-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `query_sql()`, `query_sql_database()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: query_sql
items.description: Execute a SQL query on a single DataFrame.  # Parameters - `df` вҖ” `&DataFrame`. - `sql` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<DataFrame, String>`.  Supports: SELECT, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, LIMIT/OFFSET, and aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX).

items.name: query_sql_database
items.description: Execute a SQL query on a Database (supports FROM and JOIN).  # Parameters - `db` вҖ” `&Database`. - `sql` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<DataFrame, String>`.

engine::app.module_doc: Uses a `winit` event loop with `GpuRenderer` for hardware-accelerated rendering. The game loop structure (callbacks, SharedState, Lua VM) follows the standard pattern.

items.name: App
items.description: Entry point for the Luna2D engine. Owns the game loop, GPU renderer, and Lua VM lifecycle.  # Fields - `config` вҖ” `Config`. - `conf_error` вҖ” `Option<String>`.

engine::app_winit.module_doc: Luna2D application lifecycle using winit 0.30 + wgpu GPU rendering.  # DEAD FILE This file is NOT declared in `src/engine/mod.rs` and is not compiled. Preserved for reference only. Message catalog migration was NOT applied here. Replaces the minifb-based `App` with a `winit` event loop and `GpuRenderer`. The game loop structure (callbacks, SharedState, Lua VM) is preserved; only the rendering and windowing backends change.

items.name: App
items.description: Entry point for the Luna2D engine. Owns the game loop, GPU renderer, and Lua VM lifecycle.

engine::config.module_doc: When the engine starts it looks for a `conf.lua` file in the game directory. If found, it is executed in a minimal Lua VM and the result is read into a [`Config`] struct.  Missing fields fall back to built-in defaults so authors only need to specify the settings they actually want to change.  # Structure  [`Config`] is the top-level container and contains five nested structs: - [`WindowConfig`] вҖ” window geometry, title, display placement, and decoration options. - [`GraphicsConfig`] вҖ” GPU backend selection and power preference, resolved at startup. - [`ModulesConfig`] вҖ” boolean feature-flags for optional engine subsystems (audio,   physics, graphics, etc.).  Disabling a module avoids the startup cost and prevents   the matching `luna.*` API calls from being registered. - [`PerformanceConfig`] вҖ” target frame-rate cap (`fps_cap`).  The `identity` field sets the name of the per-user save directory returned by `luna.filesystem.getSaveDirectory()`.  If unset, the engine uses the game directory name as a fallback.  # Example `conf.lua`  ```lua function luna.conf(t)     t.window.title   = "My Game"     t.window.width   = 1280     t.window.height  = 720     t.window.vsync   = true     -- GPU backend: "auto" | "dx12" | "vulkan" | "metal"     t.graphics.backend          = "auto"     -- Power preference: "high" | "low" | "none"     t.graphics.power_preference = "high" end ```

items.name: Config
items.description: Top-level engine configuration. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Can be populated from `conf.lua` or constructed with defaults.  # Fields - `window` вҖ” Window dimensions, title, vsync, fullscreen, and resize settings. - `graphics` вҖ” GPU backend selection and power preference (resolved at engine startup). - `modules` вҖ” Flags enabling optional subsystems (audio, physics, graphics, etc.). - `performance` вҖ” Frame rate cap. - `identity` вҖ” Save directory name (used for persistent game data). - `version` вҖ” Target engine version string. - `log_file` вҖ” Path to the log file, relative to the game directory. - `log_append` вҖ” If `true`, appends to an existing log file instead of truncating it. - `log_level` вҖ” Minimum log level written to both stderr and the log file (`"error"`, `"warn"`, `"info"`, `"debug"`, `"trace"`). Overrides the build-mode default when set.

items.name: GraphicsConfig
items.description: GPU backend and power-preference settings resolved once at engine startup.  These values are read from `t.graphics` in `conf.lua` and translate directly into [`wgpu::Backends`] and [`wgpu::PowerPreference`] passed to [`wgpu::Instance::new`] and [`wgpu::Instance::request_adapter`] respectively.  Changing these fields after the GPU has been initialised has no effect.  # Fields - `backend` вҖ” Which graphics API to use. `"auto"` lets wgpu choose the best available   backend for the current platform (DX12 on Windows, Metal on macOS, Vulkan on Linux).   Valid values: `"auto"`, `"dx12"`, `"vulkan"`, `"metal"`. - `power_preference` вҖ” Hint for which physical adapter to prefer when multiple GPUs are   present. `"high"` requests the discrete GPU, `"low"` requests the integrated GPU,   `"none"` expresses no preference and lets the driver decide.   Valid values: `"high"`, `"low"`, `"none"`.

items.name: WindowConfig
items.description: Window dimensions, title, vsync, fullscreen, and resize settings.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” Window width in pixels. - `height` вҖ” Window height in pixels. - `title` вҖ” Title bar string. - `vsync` вҖ” Enable vertical sync. - `fullscreen` вҖ” Launch in fullscreen mode. - `resizable` вҖ” Allow the user to resize the window. - `min_width` вҖ” Minimum window width (optional). - `min_height` вҖ” Minimum window height (optional). - `borderless` вҖ” Remove window decorations (title bar, borders). - `icon` вҖ” Path to a window icon image, resolved relative to the game directory and applied during startup. - `display_index` вҖ” Monitor index for window placement (0 = primary). - `scale_mode` вҖ” Viewport scaling mode: `"none"`, `"letterbox"`, `"stretch"`, or `"pixel"`. Default: `"none"`. - `game_width` вҖ” Logical game resolution width in virtual pixels. `None` means match window width. - `game_height` вҖ” Logical game resolution height in virtual pixels. `None` means match window height. - `maximized` вҖ” Start the window maximized. Default: `false`.

items.name: ModulesConfig
items.description: Flags to enable or disable optional engine subsystems.  All flags default to `true` (all systems on) except `debug`, which defaults to `true` only in debug builds.  Set a flag to `false` in `conf.lua` to skip registering the matching `luna.*` namespace entirely.  # Fields - `audio` вҖ” rodio audio subsystem (`luna.audio`). - `physics` вҖ” rapier2d physics world (`luna.physics`). - `graphics` вҖ” GPU render pipeline (`luna.graphics`, `luna.font`, `luna.sprite`). - `input` вҖ” keyboard / mouse / gamepad input (`luna.input`). - `timer` вҖ” frame timer and scheduled callbacks (`luna.timer`). - `filesystem` вҖ” sandboxed game filesystem (`luna.filesystem`). - `window` вҖ” window state queries (`luna.window`). - `particle` вҖ” 2D particle emitters (`luna.particle`). - `image` вҖ” CPU-side image manipulation (`luna.image`). - `gui` вҖ” retained-mode GUI widgets (`luna.gui`). - `overlay` вҖ” fullscreen overlay and post-processing effects (`luna.overlay`, `luna.postfx`). - `tilemap` вҖ” tile maps, tile sets, and map generation (`luna.tilemap`). - `scene` вҖ” scene stack and transition management (`luna.scene`). - `savegame` вҖ” save/load orchestration and schema versioning (`luna.savegame`). - `entity` вҖ” lightweight ECS primitives (`luna.entity`). - `ai` вҖ” FSMs, behaviour trees, and steering (`luna.ai`, `luna.steering`). - `pathfinding` вҖ” Aвҳ… and flow-field navigation grids (`luna.pathfinding`). - `thread` вҖ” background Rust threads and `Channel` objects (`luna.thread`). - `graph` вҖ” directed graphs and flow simulation (`luna.graph`). - `data` вҖ” binary data helpers, encoding/compression, and serial (`luna.data`, `luna.serial`). - `compute` вҖ” dense numerical arrays and `DataFrame` (`luna.compute`, `luna.dataframe`). - `minimap` вҖ” minimap extraction and FOV masking (`luna.minimap`). - `modding` вҖ” mod discovery and load ordering (`luna.modding`). - `pipeline` вҖ” data transformation pipelines and pattern helpers (`luna.pipeline`, `luna.patterns`). - `system` вҖ” system information queries (`luna.system`). - `localization` вҖ” string localisation tables (`luna.localization`). - `debug` вҖ” debug bridge, doc server, and automation helpers (`luna.debug`, `luna.debugbridge`, `luna.docs`, `luna.automation`).

items.name: PerformanceConfig
items.description: Frame rate cap and other performance tuning options.  # Fields - `target_fps` вҖ” Desired frames per second for the game loop.

engine::debug_overlay.module_doc: Debug overlay for displaying FPS and draw call statistics.  Renders green text on a semi-transparent background in the top-right corner of the screen. Can be toggled at runtime via F12 or `luna.system.setDebugOverlay()`.

items.name: DebugOverlay
items.description: Debug overlay showing FPS and render statistics.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`.  Generates `DrawCommand` sequences for rendering performance info in the top-right corner of the screen.

engine::error.module_doc: Structured error types and result alias for the Luna2D engine.  All engine-level error conditions are expressed as variants of [`EngineError`], a `thiserror`-derived enum that carries a human-readable description and belongs to one of the six [`ErrorCategory`] groups: `Init`, `Runtime`, `Resource`, `Script`, `System`, or `Filesystem`.  This module also provides [`EngineResult<T>`], a type alias for `Result<T, EngineError>`, which should be used in public APIs that can fail.  # Design goal  Every error variant has a stable four-digit numeric code (e.g. `E1001`) that can be displayed in the error screen and referenced in documentation without changing across releases.  This makes it straightforward for users to search for a specific error online or in the Luna2D issue tracker.  Lua errors are wrapped as [`EngineError::LuaError`] so they can flow through the same result type and be presented by [`crate::engine::error_screen::ErrorScreen`].

items.name: ErrorCategory
items.description: Error category for grouping related engine errors.  # Variants - `Init` вҖ” Init variant. - `Runtime` вҖ” Runtime variant. - `Resource` вҖ” Resource variant. - `Script` вҖ” Script variant. - `System` вҖ” System variant.

items.name: EngineError
items.description: All possible error conditions that can occur in the Luna2D engine.  Each variant carries a stable error code (`E1001`вҖ“`E1012`) and belongs to an [`ErrorCategory`] for structured error reporting.  # Variants - `InitializationError` вҖ” Engine startup failure (window, renderer, Lua VM). - `RenderError` вҖ” Error during frame rendering. - `InputError` вҖ” Error reading or processing input state. - `AudioError` вҖ” Failure in the audio subsystem. - `PhysicsError` вҖ” Error in the physics world or body simulation. - `FileSystemError` вҖ” Sandboxed I/O failure. - `LuaError` вҖ” Lua script execution or binding error. - `WindowError` вҖ” Window creation or event loop failure. - `ConfigError` вҖ” `conf.lua` parse error. - `ResourceNotFound` вҖ” A requested asset was not found in the game directory. - `ResourceNotLoaded` вҖ” A resource was released but a stale handle was accessed. - `IoError` вҖ” Low-level I/O error (from `std::io::Error`).

items.name: EngineResult
items.description: Convenience alias for `Result<T, EngineError>` used throughout the engine.  # Returns Wraps any value type `T` in a `Result` that carries an `EngineError` on failure.

engine::error_screen.module_doc: Visual error screen for displaying Lua and engine errors to the user.  Generates `DrawCommand` sequences that render a Love2D-style blue error screen, including the error title, message, traceback, and instructions to quit or restart.

items.name: ErrorScreen
items.description: Visual error screen that generates `DrawCommand` sequences for the GPU renderer.  Stores a pre-processed error title, message lines, and traceback so that `draw_commands()` can emit a frame without any game assets loaded.

items.name: wrap_text
items.description: Wraps a text string at word boundaries to fit within `max_chars` columns.  # Parameters - `text` вҖ” `&str`. - `max_chars` вҖ” `usize`.  # Returns `Vec<String>`.  Returns one `String` per output line. Empty input produces an empty `Vec`.

items.name: format_traceback
items.description: Cleans up a Lua traceback string for display.  # Parameters - `traceback` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Vec<String>`.  - Removes `[string "..."]` wrappers, showing just `filename:line`. - Strips the "stack traceback:" header line. - Prefixes each line with two spaces of indentation.

engine::log_messages.module_doc: Structured logging with stable message IDs for the Luna2D engine.  Every engine log message has a stable ID (`L001`..`L099`) so that external tools and Lua scripts can filter or match on them.  Use the [`log_msg!`] macro instead of bare `log::info!` / `log::warn!` / `log::error!` calls.

items.name: set_log_level
items.description: Sets the global log level at runtime (called from `luna.system.setLogLevel`).  # Parameters - `level` вҖ” `&str`.

items.name: get_log_level
items.description: Returns the current log level name. This accessor incurs no allocation; call it freely in hot paths.  # Returns `&'static str`.

engine::messages.module_doc: TOML-backed message catalog for stable, human-readable engine log messages.  The catalog is embedded at compile time from `src/engine/cfg/messages.toml` and loaded once into a process-wide [`OnceLock`] on first access.  Use [`get_message`] inside the [`log_msg!`](crate::log_msg) macro вҖ” do not call it directly in performance-critical paths.  # Usage  ```rust,ignore use crate::engine::log_messages::L003_GAME_LOADED;  // Simple вҖ” catalog text only: log_msg!(info, L003_GAME_LOADED);  // With dynamic detail appended: log_msg!(info, L003_GAME_LOADED, "path: {}", main_lua.display()); ```

items.name: MessageCatalog
items.description: Immutable map from stable message ID (e.g. `"L001"`) to human-readable text.  Loaded once via [`init`] or lazily on the first [`get_message`] call.

items.name: init
items.description: Initialise the global message catalog from the embedded TOML.  Safe to call multiple times вҖ” only the first call takes effect. Called automatically by [`App::new`](crate::engine::App) during engine startup.

items.name: get_message
items.description: Resolve a stable message ID to its human-readable text.  Returns the catalog text if the ID is registered, or the raw ID string if the catalog has not been initialised or the ID is not present.  # Parameters - `id` вҖ” `&'static str`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

items.name: catalog
items.description: Returns a reference to the global [`MessageCatalog`], or `None` if [`init`] has not been called yet.  # Returns `Option<&'static MessageCatalog>`.

engine.module_doc: Core engine lifecycle: application loop, configuration, error handling, and diagnostics.  This module owns the highest-level runtime structures in the Luna2D engine. [`App`] drives the winit event loop, fires `luna.load/update/draw/keypressed/вҖҰ` Lua callbacks at the right moments, and holds every domain subsystem inside a `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>` that is shared with the Lua VM.  # Module overview  - [`app`] вҖ” [`App`] struct that implements `winit::ApplicationHandler`.  It creates the   window and renderer, initialises every subsystem, executes `main.lua`, and routes   window events to their corresponding Lua callbacks. - [`config`] вҖ” [`Config`], [`WindowConfig`], [`ModulesConfig`], and [`PerformanceConfig`]   structs loaded from `conf.lua` at startup. - [`debug_overlay`] вҖ” on-screen FPS and draw-call counter drawn in the bottom-right   corner of the window during development. - [`error`] вҖ” [`EngineError`] enum and [`EngineResult`] type alias used throughout the   codebase for structured, recoverable error propagation. - [`error_screen`] вҖ” fallback render path that displays a human-readable Lua or engine   error on screen instead of crashing silently. - [`log_messages`] вҖ” structured, stable-ID log message constants used by `log::info!`   macros across the engine so message IDs remain consistent between builds. - [`resource_keys`] вҖ” typed newtype keys (`SoundKey`, `TextureKey`, `ShaderId`, вҖҰ) for   all slot-map resource pools in the engine.

items.name: app
items.description: Entry point for the Luna2D engine lifecycle and game loop.

items.name: config
items.description: Engine and window configuration structs (Config, WindowConfig, etc.).

items.name: debug_overlay
items.description: Debug overlay for FPS and draw-call statistics.

items.name: error
items.description: EngineError enum and EngineResult type alias for engine-level errors.

items.name: error_screen
items.description: Visual error screen for Lua and engine errors.

items.name: log_messages
items.description: Structured logging with stable message IDs.

items.name: messages
items.description: TOML-backed human-readable message catalog for all engine log messages.

items.name: resource_keys
items.description: Typed resource keys for generational ID-based resource pools.

items.name: shared_state
items.description: Central shared runtime state: SharedState, WindowState, FullscreenType, ErrorInfo.

engine::resource_keys.module_doc: Typed resource keys for generational ID-based resource pools.  Each key type maps to a specific resource category, preventing accidental cross-pool lookups at compile time.  This module is part of Luna2D's `engine` subsystem and provides the implementation details for resource keys-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TextureKey`, `FontKey`, `CanvasKey`, `SoundKey`, `ParticleKey`, `ShapeKey`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

engine::shared_state.module_doc: Central shared runtime state for the Luna2D engine.  [`SharedState`] is the hub that connects every subsystem to every API call. It is created once at startup, wrapped in `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>`, and cloned into every Lua API closure. The engine event loop also holds a clone.  Also defines [`WindowState`], [`FullscreenType`], and [`ErrorInfo`] which are window-management types used by both `engine` and `lua_api`.

items.name: FullscreenType
items.description: Fullscreen mode type for window management.  # Variants - `Desktop` вҖ” Desktop variant. - `Exclusive` вҖ” Exclusive variant.

items.name: WindowState
items.description: Tracks window state and queues window operations for the event loop.  # Fields - `focused` вҖ” `bool`. - `mouse_focused` вҖ” `bool`. - `minimized` вҖ” `bool`. - `maximized` вҖ” `bool`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. - `dpi_scale` вҖ” `f64`. - `position_x` вҖ” `i32`. - `position_y` вҖ” `i32`. - `pending_title` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `pending_fullscreen` вҖ” `Option<bool>`. - `pending_fullscreen_type` вҖ” `FullscreenType`. - `pending_position` вҖ” `Option<(i32, i32)>`. - `pending_size` вҖ” `Option<(u32, u32)>`. - `pending_minimize` вҖ” `bool`. - `pending_maximize` вҖ” `bool`. - `pending_restore` вҖ” `bool`. - `pending_close` вҖ” `bool`. - `pending_attention` вҖ” `bool`. - `pending_icon_path` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `vsync_mode` вҖ” `i32`. - `pending_vsync` вҖ” `Option<i32>`. - `fullscreen` вҖ” `bool`. - `fullscreen_type` вҖ” `FullscreenType`. - `game_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `game_height` вҖ” `f32`. - `scale_mode_str` вҖ” `String`. - `viewport_scale_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `viewport_scale_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `viewport_offset_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `viewport_offset_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `pending_scale_mode` вҖ” `Option<String>`.  Query fields are written by `app.rs` from window events and read by Lua. Pending fields are written by Lua closures and consumed by `app.rs`.

items.name: ErrorInfo
items.description: Structured error information for the last engine error.  # Fields - `message` вҖ” `String`. - `code` вҖ” `String`. - `category` вҖ” `String`. - `hint` вҖ” `Option<String>`.

items.name: ScreenshotRequest
items.description: Pending request to save the next rendered screen frame as a PNG.  # Fields - `path` вҖ” `String`.

items.name: SharedState
items.description: Shared mutable state passed via `Rc<RefCell<SharedState>>` to all Lua API closures and the engine loop.  # Fields - `draw_commands` вҖ” Queue of pending `DrawCommand` values, flushed each frame. - `current_color` вҖ” Active RGBA draw color `[r, g, b, a]`. - `background_color` вҖ” Screen clear color set by `luna.graphics.setBackgroundColor`. - `textures` вҖ” Loaded texture pixel data, indexed by `Texture::id`. - `keys_down` вҖ” Set of currently held key name strings. - `mouse` вҖ” Mouse cursor position, button state, scroll, and cursor settings. - `delta_time` вҖ” Elapsed time for the current frame in seconds. - `total_time` вҖ” Accumulated time since engine start in seconds. - `fps` вҖ” Rolling frames-per-second measurement. - `window_width` вҖ” Current window width in pixels. - `window_height` вҖ” Current window height in pixels. - `window_title` вҖ” Current window title string. - `mixer` вҖ” The rodio audio mixer managing all sound sources. - `game_dir` вҖ” Absolute path to the game directory. - `quit_requested` вҖ” Set to `true` by `luna.event.quit()` to end the game loop. - `exit_code` вҖ” Exit code to return when `quit_requested` is `true`. - `restart_requested` вҖ” Set to `true` by `luna.event.restart()` to trigger engine restart. - `line_width` вҖ” Current stroke width for outline draw commands. - `blend_mode` вҖ” Current blend mode for draw operations. - `fonts` вҖ” Loaded TTF fonts for text rendering. - `active_font` вҖ” Index of the currently active font (`None` = use bitmap fallback). - `canvases` вҖ” Off-screen render targets (canvases) for compositing. - `gamepads` вҖ” Connected gamepad state instances. - `fs` вҖ” Persistent sandboxed `GameFS` instance with mount layer support. - `shapes` вҖ” Stores all compound shape instances.  Shared mutable state accessible by both the engine loop and Lua closures.

items.name: RendererStats
items.description: Snapshot of renderer statistics for a single frame.  # Fields - `draw_calls` вҖ” `usize`. - `textures` вҖ” `usize`. - `fonts` вҖ” `usize`. - `canvases` вҖ” `usize`. - `texture_memory` вҖ” `usize`. RGBA byte estimate.

entity.module_doc: Entity/ECS module вҖ” lightweight entity-component-system with ID recycling, bitmap tags, layers, blueprints, and system dispatch.  This module is part of Luna2D's `entity` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: relationships
items.description: Generic relationship system for entity pairs.

items.name: universe
items.description: Universe вҖ” entity management with ID recycling, components, tags, and systems.

entity::relationships.module_doc: Generic relationship system for entities.  Stores symmetric numeric relations and named-state levels between entity pairs. Entity IDs are `u32` to match [`Universe`](super::Universe).  This module is part of Luna2D's `entity` subsystem and provides the implementation details for relationships-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `RelationType`, `Relationship`, `RelationshipManager`. Primary functions: `new()`, `has_level()`, `new()`, `define_type()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: RelationType
items.description: Definition of a named relation type with a fixed set of valid level strings.  # Example ```text define_type("diplomacy", &["war","neutral","alliance"], "neutral") ```  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `levels` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `default_level` вҖ” `String`.

items.name: Relationship
items.description: A relationship between two entities: numeric value plus per-type named levels.  The relationship is keyed as `(min(a, b), max(a, b))` so that `AвҶ”B` and `BвҶ”A` share the same record. Code using [`RelationshipManager`] does not need to sort the IDs.  # Fields - `from_id` вҖ” `u32`. - `to_id` вҖ” `u32`. - `value` вҖ” `f64`. - `s` вҖ” ``type_name вҶ’ level_string`.`. - `type_levels` вҖ” `HashMap<String`.

items.name: RelationshipManager
items.description: Manages all relation types and the per-pair relationship records.  # Fields - `types` вҖ” `HashMap<String`. - `relations` вҖ” `HashMap<(u32`.

entity::universe.module_doc: Universe вҖ” a self-contained ECS world. Entities are u32 IDs starting at 1.  This module is part of Luna2D's `entity` subsystem and provides the implementation details for universe-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Universe`. Primary functions: `new()`, `get_system_store()`, `spawn()`, `kill()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Universe
items.description: A self-contained ECS world. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Manages entities (u32 IDs with recycling), components (stored in Lua registry tables), string and bitmap tags, layers, blueprints, and systems.  # Fields - `next_id` вҖ” `u32`. - `free_list` вҖ” `Vec<u32>`. - `alive` вҖ” `HashSet<u32>`. - `string_tags` вҖ” `HashMap<u32`. - `bitmap_tag_names` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `bitmap_masks` вҖ” `HashMap<u32`. - `layers` вҖ” `HashMap<u32`. - `generations` вҖ” `HashMap<u32, u8>`: per-slot generation counter. - `tag_index` вҖ” `HashMap<String, Vec<u32>>`: inverted tag вҶ’ packed entity IDs. - `parents` вҖ” `HashMap<u32, u32>`: child slot вҶ’ parent slot. - `children` вҖ” `HashMap<u32, Vec<u32>>`: parent slot вҶ’ child slots. - `component_store` вҖ” `Option<RegistryKey>`. - `blueprint_store` вҖ” `Option<RegistryKey>`. - `system_store` вҖ” `Option<RegistryKey>`.

items.name: deep_copy_table
items.description: Deep-copies a Lua table recursively. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `lua` вҖ” `&'lua Lua`. - `t` вҖ” `&Table<'lua>`.  # Returns `LuaResult<Table<'lua>>`.

event.module_doc: Event queue for polling system and custom events.  Provides an alternative to the callback model where game code can poll events from a FIFO queue. Also contains the `Signal` pub-sub type for handle-based event dispatching.

items.name: EventArg
items.description: Argument values that can be attached to events.  # Variants - `Str` вҖ” Str variant. - `Num` вҖ” Num variant. - `Bool` вҖ” Bool variant. - `Nil` вҖ” Nil variant.

items.name: Event
items.description: A single event in the event queue. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `args` вҖ” `Vec<EventArg>`.

items.name: EventQueue
items.description: FIFO event queue for system and custom events.  # Fields - `events` вҖ” `VecDeque<Event>`.

items.name: event_to_lua_multi
items.description: Converts an [`Event`] into a Lua multi-value (name followed by args).  The first value is the event name as a Lua string; subsequent values are each [`EventArg`] converted to its Lua equivalent.  # Parameters - `lua` вҖ” `&Lua`. The Lua VM. - `event` вҖ” `&Event`. The event to convert.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaMultiValue>`.

event::signal.module_doc: Handle-based pub-sub signal system.  `Signal` provides a lightweight event bus where listeners subscribe by name and receive a monotonically increasing handle ID for later removal. Callbacks fire in registration order.

items.name: Subscription
items.description: A single subscription entry in a [`Signal`].  # Fields - `handle` вҖ” `u64`. - `name` вҖ” `String`.

items.name: Signal
items.description: Handle-based pub-sub signal dispatcher. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Listeners register for named events and receive a unique handle ID. When an event is emitted, all matching callbacks fire in registration order. The actual callback functions are stored externally (e.g. in the Lua registry); this struct tracks only the subscription metadata.  # Fields - `next_handle` вҖ” `u64`. - `subscriptions` вҖ” `HashMap<String`. - `handle_to_name` вҖ” `HashMap<u64`.

filesystem::async_loader.module_doc: Background asset-loading worker that reads files off the main thread.  The loader owns a single OS thread that processes read requests from a bounded channel.  Results are polled from the main thread each frame вҖ” no `unsafe`, no shared mutable state beyond an `Arc<Mutex<>>` result map.

items.name: LoadHandle
items.description: Opaque handle returned to callers (and to Lua) that identifies a pending load.

items.name: LoadResult
items.description: Outcome of a completed load request. Returns an error if the source data is malformed or missing.  # Variants - `Ready` вҖ” Ready variant. - `Error` вҖ” Error variant.

items.name: LoadStatus
items.description: Status returned by [`AsyncLoader::poll`].  # Variants - `Pending` вҖ” Pending variant. - `Done` вҖ” Done variant.

items.name: AsyncLoader
items.description: A single-threaded background file reader.  Create one per engine session; drop it to join the worker thread.  # Fields - `next_id` вҖ” `AtomicU64`. - `tx` вҖ” `Option<mpsc::SyncSender<LoadRequest>>`. - `results` вҖ” `Arc<Mutex<HashMap<u64`. - `worker` вҖ” `Option<thread::JoinHandle<()>>`.

filesystem::file_data.module_doc: Raw file data buffer loaded from the VFS.

items.name: FileData
items.description: Raw bytes loaded from the virtual filesystem.  # Fields - `path` вҖ” `String`. The virtual path this data was loaded from. - `bytes` вҖ” `Vec<u8>`. Raw file content.

filesystem::file_handle.module_doc: File handle with buffered read/write and sandboxed path resolution.  This module is part of Luna2D's `filesystem` subsystem and provides the implementation details for file handle-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `FileMode`, `FileHandle`. Primary functions: `parse_mode()`, `as_str()`, `open()`, `read()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: FileMode
items.description: File access mode. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Read` вҖ” open for reading; file must exist - `Write` вҖ” open for writing; creates or truncates - `Append` вҖ” open for appending; creates if needed - `Closed` вҖ” handle is not open

items.name: FileHandle
items.description: A sandboxed file handle for reading or writing game files.  # Fields - `mode` вҖ” `FileMode`. - `path` вҖ” `PathBuf`. - `logical_path` вҖ” `String`. - `reader` вҖ” `Option<BufReader<std::fs::File>>`. - `writer` вҖ” `Option<BufWriter<std::fs::File>>`. - `size` вҖ” `u64`.

filesystem.module_doc: Mod implementation for the `filesystem` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `filesystem` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: vfs
items.description: Sandboxed virtual filesystem that restricts I/O to the game directory.

items.name: file_handle
items.description: File handle with buffered read/write and sandboxed path resolution.

items.name: async_loader
items.description: Background asset-loading worker that reads files off the main thread.

items.name: file_data
items.description: Raw file data buffer loaded from the VFS.

filesystem::vfs.module_doc: Vfs implementation for the `filesystem` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `filesystem` subsystem and provides the implementation details for vfs-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `FileInfo`, `FileType`, `GameFS`. Primary functions: `new()`, `base_dir()`, `read_string()`, `read_bytes()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: FileInfo
items.description: File metadata returned by `get_info()`. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `file_type` вҖ” whether the entry is a file, directory, symlink, or other - `size` вҖ” size in bytes - `modified_time` вҖ” last modification time as a UNIX timestamp, if available - `readonly` вҖ” whether the entry is marked read-only

items.name: FileType
items.description: File type classification for `FileInfo`.  # Variants - `File` вҖ” regular file - `Directory` вҖ” directory - `Symlink` вҖ” symbolic link - `Other` вҖ” unknown or special entry

items.name: MountLayer
items.description: A virtual filesystem mount layer overlaid on top of the game directory.  # Fields - `source` вҖ” `PathBuf`. The host-OS directory being mounted. - `mountpoint` вҖ” `String`. The virtual path prefix this layer serves.

items.name: GameFS
items.description: Sandboxed filesystem rooted at the game directory; prevents path-traversal attacks.  # Fields - `base_dir` вҖ” `PathBuf`. - `identity` вҖ” `String`. - `mounts` вҖ” `Vec<MountLayer>`.

fx::ambient.module_doc: Ambient lighting state with time-of-day colour cycling.  Provides [`AmbientState`] for gradual ambient colour transitions driven by an in-game clock.

items.name: AmbientState
items.description: Ambient lighting state with time-of-day colour cycling.  When `enabled` is `true`, `Overlay::update` calls `compute_color_from_time()` each frame and stores the result back into `color`. This means any manual writes to `color` will be overwritten on the next update. To set the tint manually without cycling, set `enabled = false` and write directly to `color`.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether time-of-day colour cycling is active. - `color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]` вҖ” Current ambient tint (RGBA); auto-computed when enabled. - `time_of_day` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Time of day in hours (0.0вҖ“24.0); drives `compute_color_from_time`.

fx::atmosphere.module_doc: Atmospheric visual effects data models.  Contains data-only structs for clouds, fog, vignette, lightning, film grain, and heat haze вҖ” all consumed by the overlay renderer.

items.name: CloudState
items.description: Cloud shadow overlay state.  Renders `count` soft shadow blobs that drift horizontally across the screen. The `offset` field is an internal scroll accumulator that advances by `speed * dt` each frame; the renderer uses it as a UV shift and the value is allowed to grow without bound (the renderer wraps it modulo screen width). Adjust `scale` to change blob size and `opacity` to control how dark the shadows appear.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether cloud shadows are active. - `count` вҖ” `u32` вҖ” Number of cloud shadow blobs rendered each frame. - `speed` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Horizontal scroll speed in pixels per second. - `scale` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Relative blob size (1.0 = default). - `opacity` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Shadow overlay opacity (0.0вҖ“1.0). - `offset` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Internal accumulator; used by the renderer as a UV offset.

items.name: FogState
items.description: Atmospheric fog state.  Renders a uniform translucent colour rectangle over the entire scene, simulating thick atmospheric haze. The `density` field maps linearly to the overlay opacity вҖ” a `density` of 1.0 fills the screen with the solid `color`; 0.0 is invisible. This is a simple per-frame alpha blend and does not perform any distance-based depth fog calculation. For a subtler ground-level effect, pair it with a low opacity and a grey-blue tint.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether fog is active. - `density` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Fog opacity (0.0вҖ“1.0); maps directly to blend alpha. - `color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]` вҖ” Fog tint colour (RGBA).

items.name: HeatHazeState
items.description: Heat haze distortion state.  Provides a UV-space shimmer distortion driven by a slowly-evolving sine wave pattern. The `intensity` field scales the peak UV offset in screen-space pixels вҖ” values in the 0.2вҖ“2.0 range produce a subtle desert mirage effect; higher values are suitable for extreme heat or magical distortion. The GPU layer in `lua_api` animates the sine phase using the current game time, so no per-frame Lua call is needed.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether heat haze distortion is active. - `intensity` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Peak UV displacement in pixels (typical range 0.0вҖ“5.0).

items.name: VignetteState
items.description: Vignette screen-edge darkening state.  Applies a smooth radial darkening that grades from transparent at the screen centre to opaque black at the corners. The `strength` value controls how aggressively the edges are darkened: 0.0 produces no visible effect; 0.5 is a gentle film-like border; 1.0 crushes the corners to near-black. The vignette is useful for directing the player's eye toward the centre of the screen and for conveying low-health or tense atmospheric states.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether vignette darkening is active. - `strength` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Edge darkening intensity (0.0вҖ“1.0; default 0.5).

items.name: FilmGrainState
items.description: Film grain noise overlay state.  Adds randomised per-pixel luminance noise over the rendered scene, simulating the grain of analog film stock. The noise pattern is regenerated every frame by the GPU layer so it does not repeat or flicker in a regular pattern. `intensity` scales the peak noise amplitude: 0.1вҖ“0.3 gives a subtle cinematic look; values above 0.5 produce a heavy grain that can obscure fine detail.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether film grain noise is active. - `intensity` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Grain amplitude (0.0вҖ“1.0; default 0.3).

items.name: LightningState
items.description: Lightning flash state.  A single-shot full-screen hard flash distinct from `FlashState`: lightning uses a very short default duration (0.15 s) and is designed to simulate the brief, intense luminosity of a lightning bolt. Trigger it with `Overlay::trigger_lightning`; the flash is non-repeating and deactivates automatically once `elapsed >= duration`. For sustained ambient flicker, chain repeated `trigger_lightning` calls from Lua with randomised delays.  # Fields - `active` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether a lightning flash is in progress. - `color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]` вҖ” Flash colour (RGBA); defaults to pale blue-white. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Time elapsed since the last trigger (seconds). - `duration` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Total flash duration in seconds (default 0.15).

fx::effect.module_doc: Post-processing effect data model.  Defines [`PostFxEffect`] вҖ” a single named effect with typed parameters for the post-processing pipeline.

items.name: PostFxEffect
items.description: A single post-processing effect with named float parameters.  Acts as a parameter bag describing one shader pass вҖ” it does NOT hold any GPU resource. Parameters are stored in a `HashMap<String, f32>` so that new shader uniforms can be added without changing the struct layout. Use `PostFxEffectType::default_params` to pre-populate sensible defaults, then call `set_parameter` to override individual values. The `enabled` flag lets you temporarily skip the pass within the stack without removing it.  # Fields - `effect_type` вҖ” `PostFxEffectType` вҖ” Which shader pass to run. - `params` вҖ” Named float parameters controlling the shader (e.g., `"threshold"`, `"intensity"`). - `enabled` вҖ” Whether this effect is active within its parent stack. - `shader_id` вҖ” Optional custom shader handle; only used for `Custom` effects.

fx::effect_type.module_doc: Built-in post-processing effect type definitions.  Enumerates all shader passes recognised by the engine's post-processing pipeline and provides parameter presets for each.

items.name: PostFxEffectType
items.description: Built-in effect types for the post-processing pipeline.  Each variant maps to a distinct full-screen shader pass implemented in `lua_api`. The `Custom` variant defers to an external shader ID supplied via `PostFxEffect::new_custom`. Use `PostFxEffectType::from_name` to parse effect names from Lua strings, and `PostFxEffect::default_params` to retrieve the canonical starting parameters for each type.  # Variants - `Bloom` вҖ” HDR bloom with threshold and intensity parameters. - `Blur` вҖ” Gaussian blur with configurable radius and strength. - `Crt` вҖ” CRT monitor simulation with scanline strength. - `Godrays` вҖ” Light ray / god ray screen-space effect with intensity. - `Vignette` вҖ” Screen edge darkening with configurable strength. - `ColourGrade` вҖ” Colour grading with brightness, contrast, and saturation. - `Chromatic` вҖ” Chromatic aberration with pixel offset. - `Pixelate` вҖ” Block pixelation effect with configurable block size. - `Sepia` вҖ” Warm sepia tone mapping with configurable strength. - `Grayscale` вҖ” Desaturate to greyscale with configurable strength. - `Invert` вҖ” Colour inversion with configurable strength. - `Scanlines` вҖ” Horizontal scanline bars (CRT-free) with strength and spacing. - `EdgeDetect` вҖ” Sobel edge detection outline with configurable strength. - `HueShift` вҖ” Hue rotation in degrees. - `Noise` вҖ” Random per-pixel noise with configurable strength. - `Custom` вҖ” User-provided shader pass created via `newPass()`.

fx::image_effect.module_doc: `ImageEffect` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ an ordered chain of `PostFxEffect` passes for per-image draw calls.  [`ImageEffect`] groups one or more [`PostFxEffect`] entries and converts them to lightweight [`crate::graphics::ShaderPassDescriptor`] values via [`ImageEffect::to_passes`]. This module lives in **Tier 2** and is permitted to import from `crate::graphics` (Tier 1).

items.name: ImageEffect
items.description: An ordered shader-effect chain to apply when drawing a single image.  Can be attached to a `luna.graphics.draw` call via the options-table overload (`effect` key). Effects are applied in insertion order through each enabled pass. `to_passes` converts the chain to the lightweight Tier-1 type embedded into `DrawCommand` variants.  Each effect entry is stored as a shared `Rc<RefCell<PostFxEffect>>` so that Lua-side handles returned by `addEffect` or `getEffect` reflect mutations made through the chain itself.  # Fields - `effects` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ `Vec<Rc<RefCell<PostFxEffect>>>` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Ordered list of shader passes. - `name` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ `String` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Optional human-readable label for the chain.

fx.module_doc: Composable visual effects layer вҖ” Tier 2 Engine Extension.  The `fx` module provides two families of visual effects as pure CPU data models. GPU application is handled in `lua_api`. All files are flat in this folder.  ## Post-processing effects (image-space pipeline)  | File | Types | |---|---| | `effect_type` | `PostFxEffectType` вҖ” enum of all built-in effect kinds | | `effect` | `PostFxEffect` вҖ” per-effect parameter bag | | `stack` | `PostFxStack` вҖ” ordered pipeline of post-processing passes | | `image_effect` | `ImageEffect` вҖ” lightweight per-image effect chain |  ## Screen overlays (world-simulation effects)  | File | Types | |---|---| | `ambient` | `AmbientState` вҖ” time-of-day colour cycling | | `atmosphere` | `CloudState`, `FogState`, `HeatHazeState`, `VignetteState`, `FilmGrainState`, `LightningState` | | `screen_effects` | `FlashState`, `ShakeState`, `FadeState` вҖ” one-shot screen effects | | `overlay` | `Overlay` вҖ” main struct combining all screen subsystems | | `weather` | `WeatherState`, `WeatherParticle`, `WeatherType` вҖ” weather simulation |  ## Tier  `fx` is a **Tier 2 вҖ” Engine Extension** module. It may import `math`, `engine`, and any Tier 1 module. It must not import other Tier 2 modules.

items.name: effect
items.description: Per-effect parameter bag with builder helpers.

items.name: effect_type
items.description: Enum of all built-in post-processing effect kinds (bloom, blur, CRT, colour grading, вҖҰ).

items.name: image_effect
items.description: Lightweight per-image effect chain (subset of the full pipeline).

items.name: stack
items.description: Ordered pipeline of post-processing passes applied to the rendered scene.

items.name: ambient
items.description: Ambient lighting state with time-of-day colour cycling (night вҶ’ dawn вҶ’ day вҶ’ dusk).

items.name: atmosphere
items.description: Atmospheric effects: clouds, fog, heat haze, vignette, film grain, lightning.

items.name: overlay
items.description: Main `Overlay` struct combining all screen-effect subsystems into one per-frame controller.

items.name: screen_effects
items.description: One-shot screen effects: flash (colour burst), shake (jitter), fade (alpha transition).

items.name: weather
items.description: Weather simulation: rain, snow, hail, dust, leaves, ash, pollen particles.

fx::overlay.module_doc: Composable per-frame overlay system.  [`Overlay`] aggregates ambient, atmospheric, weather, and screen effect sub-states and ticks them each frame.

items.name: Overlay
items.description: Composable per-frame screen-effect overlay managing multiple visual subsystems.  All subsystems start inactive and can be enabled independently. Each frame the game loop should call `update(dt)` to advance the simulation, then use `get_shake_offset`, `get_flash_alpha`, etc. (or the Lua API methods) to read back values for the renderer. No GPU objects are stored here; this struct is a pure data model.  Typical Lua usage: ```lua local o = luna.overlay.newOverlay(800, 600) o:setWeather("rain")   -- enable rain o:setWeatherEnabled(true) o:flash(1, 1, 1)        -- quick white flash -- in luna.update(dt): o:update(dt) ```  # Fields - `width` Г”ГҮГ¶ `u32` Г”ГҮГ¶ Overlay canvas width in pixels. - `height` Г”ГҮГ¶ `u32` Г”ГҮГ¶ Overlay canvas height in pixels. - `weather` Г”ГҮГ¶ Weather particle subsystem. - `ambient` Г”ГҮГ¶ Ambient lighting with time-of-day colour cycling. - `flash` Г”ГҮГ¶ One-shot screen flash effect. - `shake` Г”ГҮГ¶ Camera-shake effect with decaying PRNG offsets. - `fade` Г”ГҮГ¶ Full-screen colour fade effect. - `clouds` Г”ГҮГ¶ Scrolling cloud shadow overlay. - `fog` Г”ГҮГ¶ Uniform atmospheric fog tint. - `heat_haze` Г”ГҮГ¶ Distortion strength for shimmer effects. - `vignette` Г”ГҮГ¶ Screen-edge darkening. - `film_grain` Г”ГҮГ¶ Per-frame random noise overlay. - `lightning` Г”ГҮГ¶ Hard single-frame lightning flash.

fx::screen_effects.module_doc: Full-screen transient effects.  Provides [`FlashState`], [`FadeState`], and [`ShakeState`] for brief screen-wide visual feedback.

items.name: FlashState
items.description: Flash screen effect state.  A flash overlays an opaque colour rectangle on the screen for a short duration. The alpha fades linearly from `color[3]` (at `elapsed = 0`) down to zero (at `elapsed = duration`); the fade is computed on read by `Overlay::get_flash_alpha`. Calling `Overlay::trigger_flash` while a flash is already active restarts it from the beginning with the new colour and duration.  # Fields - `active` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether a flash is in progress. - `color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]` вҖ” Flash colour (RGBA); `color[3]` is the initial alpha. - `duration` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Total flash duration in seconds. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Time elapsed since flash start.

items.name: ShakeState
items.description: Shake screen effect state.  The shake applies a per-frame pixel offset to the camera or draw origin. Offsets are generated by a simple xorshift PRNG seeded at struct creation, giving a deterministic-ish sequence every run вҖ” no heap allocation is needed. The intensity decays linearly with the fraction of elapsed time so the shake diminishes smoothly. Calling `Overlay::trigger_shake` while a shake is already active resets elapsed time to zero and applies the new parameters immediately.  # Fields - `active` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether a shake is in progress. - `intensity` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Peak shake magnitude in pixels. - `duration` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Total shake duration in seconds. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Time elapsed since shake start. - `offset_x` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Current horizontal pixel offset (read by the renderer). - `offset_y` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Current vertical pixel offset (read by the renderer).

items.name: FadeState
items.description: Fade screen effect state.  A fade interpolates `color[3]` (the alpha channel of the fill colour) from `start_alpha` to `target_alpha` over `duration` seconds. `start_alpha` is captured automatically from the current `color[3]` when `Overlay::trigger_fade` is called, so a fade-out can seamlessly continue from wherever the previous fade-in stopped. Calling `trigger_fade` while a fade is already in progress restarts it with the new target and duration from the current alpha.  # Fields - `active` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether a fade is in progress. - `color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]` вҖ” Fill colour (RGB) with the animated alpha in `[3]`. - `target_alpha` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Alpha value to reach at the end of the fade (0.0вҖ“1.0). - `duration` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Total fade duration in seconds. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Time elapsed since fade start. - `start_alpha` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Alpha at the moment the fade was triggered.

fx::stack.module_doc: Post-processing effect stack.  [`PostFxStack`] manages an ordered chain of effects that captures and processes the rendered scene each frame.

items.name: PostFxStack
items.description: An ordered chain of effects that captures and processes the rendered scene.  The full lifecycle every draw frame is: 1. Call `beginCapture()` (Lua) вҖ” the GPU redirects draw calls to an    internal canvas. 2. Draw the scene normally. 3. Call `endCapture()` (Lua) вҖ” stops capture. 4. Call `apply()` (Lua) вҖ” runs each enabled effect in insertion order    through ping-pong canvases and blits the final result to the screen.  Effects are referenced by numeric index into the local `effects` vector in `LuaPostFxStack`. The `PostFxStack` itself is agnostic to the concrete effect objects and only tracks indices, enabled states, and canvas dimensions.  # Fields - `effects` вҖ” Ordered list of effect indices (referencing external storage). - `enabled` вҖ” Per-effect enable flag, parallel to `effects`. - `width` вҖ” Internal canvas width in pixels. - `height` вҖ” Internal canvas height in pixels.  The stack manages ping-pong canvases internally for multi-pass rendering. During `luna.draw`, the user calls `beginCapture()` вҶ’ draws scene вҶ’ `endCapture()` вҶ’ `apply()` to render the post-processed result.

fx::weather.module_doc: Weather particle system data.  Defines [`WeatherType`], [`WeatherParticle`], and [`WeatherState`] for rain, snow, and dust particle overlays.

items.name: WeatherType
items.description: Weather particle types supported by the overlay system.  Each variant changes the visual character of spawned particles: their fall speed, size, and opacity are all tuned per type inside `Overlay::spawn_particle`. The `None` variant disables the spawner without clearing existing particles вҖ” call `Overlay::clear` to purge everything at once.  # Variants - `None` вҖ” No weather active; spawner is disabled. - `Rain` вҖ” Fast, narrow, semi-transparent streaks. - `Snow` вҖ” Slow, large, high-opacity dots with gentle drift. - `Hail` вҖ” Very fast, opaque, medium-sized pellets. - `Dust` вҖ” Very slow, small, low-opacity motes. - `Leaves` вҖ” Medium speed, large, high-opacity irregular blobs. - `Ash` вҖ” Slow, small, medium-opacity flakes. - `Pollen` вҖ” Extremely slow, tiny, low-opacity specks.

items.name: WeatherParticle
items.description: A single weather particle in the overlay's weather system.  Particles are created by `Overlay::spawn_particle` and stored in `WeatherState::particles`. Each frame `Overlay::update_weather` moves every particle by its velocity plus the current wind contribution, then removes any that have scrolled off the screen bounds. Particles are never explicitly recycled вҖ” they are simply dropped and re-spawned at the top edge.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Horizontal position in screen pixels. - `y` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Vertical position in screen pixels. - `vx` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Horizontal velocity in pixels per second (base, before wind). - `vy` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Vertical velocity in pixels per second (base, before wind). - `size` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Particle radius/size in pixels. - `alpha` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Particle opacity (0.0вҖ“1.0).

items.name: WeatherState
items.description: Weather subsystem state.  Controls the full particle simulation for the current weather. The `intensity` value (0.0вҖ“1.0) scales both the maximum live particle count (up to 200) and the spawn rate. `spawn_timer` is an internal accumulator that should not be written to from outside the module вҖ” it is managed entirely by `Overlay::update_weather`. Wind affects all particles equally by adding a global velocity offset each frame.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool` вҖ” Whether the weather spawner is active. - `weather_type` вҖ” `WeatherType` вҖ” Determines particle appearance and base velocity. - `intensity` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Particle density (0.0вҖ“1.0); scales max count and spawn rate. - `wind_direction` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Wind angle in radians; 0 = right, ПҖ/2 = down. - `wind_speed` вҖ” `f32` вҖ” Wind speed in pixels per second. - `particles` вҖ” `Vec<WeatherParticle>` вҖ” Currently live particles.

graph::algorithms.module_doc: Graph algorithms вҖ” connected components, cycle detection, topological sort.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graph` subsystem and provides the implementation details for algorithms-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `get_components()`, `has_cycle()`, `topological_sort()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

graph::core.module_doc: Top-level directed graph container with node, edge, and item management.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graph` subsystem and provides the implementation details for core-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `GraphStats`, `Graph`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add_node()`, `remove_node()`, `has_node()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: GraphStats
items.description: Statistics snapshot of the graph state. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `nodes` вҖ” `usize`. - `edges` вҖ” `usize`. - `items` вҖ” `usize`. - `active_nodes` вҖ” `usize`. - `active_edges` вҖ” `usize`. - `items_in_transit` вҖ” `usize`. - `items_on_nodes` вҖ” `usize`. - `total_demand` вҖ” `i32`. - `total_supply` вҖ” `i32`. - `queued_items` вҖ” `usize`.

items.name: Graph
items.description: A directed graph with typed nodes, edges, and flowing items.  # Fields - `nodes` вҖ” `HashMap<u64, Node>`. - `edges` вҖ” `HashMap<u64, Edge>`. - `items` вҖ” `HashMap<u64, GraphItem>`.

graph::edge.module_doc: Graph edge вҖ” a directed connection between nodes.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graph` subsystem and provides the implementation details for edge-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Edge`. Primary functions: `new()`, `get_type()`, `set_type()`, `is_on_cooldown()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Edge
items.description: A directed connection between two nodes in the graph.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `u64`. - `edge_type` вҖ” `String`. - `from_node` вҖ” `u64`. - `to_node` вҖ” `u64`. - `capacity` вҖ” `i32`. - `throughput` вҖ” `f64`. - `travel_time` вҖ” `f64`. - `weight` вҖ” `f64`. - `speed_modifier` вҖ” `f64`. - `cooldown` вҖ” `f64`. - `cooldown_timer` вҖ” `f64`. - `bidirectional` вҖ” `bool`. - `active` вҖ” `bool`. - `allowed_types` вҖ” `HashSet<String>`. - `items_in_transit` вҖ” `Vec<u64>`.

graph::graph.module_doc: Top-level directed graph container with node, edge, and item management.

items.name: GraphStats
items.description: Statistics snapshot of the graph state.  # Fields - `nodes` вҖ” `usize`. Total number of nodes. - `edges` вҖ” `usize`. Total number of edges. - `items` вҖ” `usize`. Total number of items. - `active_nodes` вҖ” `usize`. Number of active nodes. - `active_edges` вҖ” `usize`. Number of active edges. - `items_in_transit` вҖ” `usize`. Items currently in transit on edges. - `items_on_nodes` вҖ” `usize`. Items currently sitting on nodes. - `total_demand` вҖ” `i32`. Sum of all demand quantities. - `total_supply` вҖ” `i32`. Sum of all supply quantities. - `queued_items` вҖ” `usize`. Total items in all queues.

items.name: Graph
items.description: A directed graph with typed nodes, edges, and flowing items.  # Fields - `nodes` вҖ” `HashMap<u64, Node>`. All nodes keyed by ID. - `edges` вҖ” `HashMap<u64, Edge>`. All edges keyed by ID. - `items` вҖ” `HashMap<u64, GraphItem>`. All items keyed by ID.

graph::item.module_doc: Graph item вҖ” a typed entity that flows through the network.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graph` subsystem and provides the implementation details for item-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ItemPosition`, `GraphItem`. Primary functions: `new()`, `kill()`, `is_alive()`, `get_type()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ItemPosition

items.name: GraphItem
items.description: A typed entity that flows through the graph network.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `u64`. - `item_type` вҖ” `String`. - `decay_time` вҖ” `f64`. - `remaining_life` вҖ” `f64`. - `alive` вҖ” `bool`. - `priority` вҖ” `i32`. - `position` вҖ” `ItemPosition`.

graph.module_doc: Directed graph with item flow simulation for Luna2D.  Provides nodes, edges, typed items, simulation (decay, transit, push/pull flow, conversions, queues), Dijkstra pathfinding, graph algorithms (components, cycles, topological sort), and supply/demand processing.

items.name: algorithms
items.description: Graph algorithms: connected components, topological sort, cycle detection.

items.name: simulation

items.name: supply_demand
