graph::node.module_doc: Graph node вҖ” a vertex with capacity, flow control, conversion rules, and queuing.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graph` subsystem and provides the implementation details for node-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `OverflowPolicy`, `FlowMode`, `ConversionRule`, `Supply`, `Demand`. Primary functions: `to_str()`, `to_str()`, `new()`, `get_type()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: OverflowPolicy
items.description: What happens when items arrive at a full node.  # Variants - `Reject` вҖ” Reject variant. - `Destroy` вҖ” Destroy variant. - `Queue` вҖ” Queue variant.

items.name: FlowMode
items.description: How a node participates in automatic item flow.  # Variants - `Passive` вҖ” Passive variant. - `Push` вҖ” Push variant. - `Pull` вҖ” Pull variant. - `Both` вҖ” Both variant.

items.name: ConversionRule
items.description: A rule that converts N input items of one type into M output items of another.  # Fields - `in_type` вҖ” `String`. - `out_type` вҖ” `String`. - `in_count` вҖ” `u32`. - `out_count` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: Supply
items.description: A supply declaration on a node. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `item_type` вҖ” `String`. - `quantity` вҖ” `i32`.

items.name: Demand
items.description: A demand declaration on a node. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `item_type` вҖ” `String`. - `quantity` вҖ” `i32`. - `priority` вҖ” `i32`.

items.name: Node
items.description: A vertex in the graph with capacity, flow control, conversion, and queuing.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `u64`. - `node_type` вҖ” `String`. - `capacity` вҖ” `i32`. - `active` вҖ” `bool`. - `overflow_policy` вҖ” `OverflowPolicy`. - `flow_mode` вҖ” `FlowMode`. - `push_rate` вҖ” `f64`. - `pull_rate` вҖ” `f64`. - `push_filter` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `pull_filter` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `process_time` вҖ” `f64`. - `queue_enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `queue_capacity` вҖ” `i32`. - `queue` вҖ” `VecDeque<u64>`. - `items` вҖ” `Vec<u64>`. - `conversions` вҖ” `HashMap<String, ConversionRule>`. - `demands` вҖ” `Vec<Demand>`. - `supplies` вҖ” `Vec<Supply>`. - `tags` вҖ” `HashSet<String>`. - `push_timer` вҖ” `f64`. - `pull_timer` вҖ” `f64`. - `process_accumulator` вҖ” `f64`.

graph::pathfinding.module_doc: Dijkstra pathfinding and reachability queries on the graph.

graphics::canvas.module_doc: Off-screen render targets (canvases) for deferred compositing.  A `Canvas` stores the logical dimensions of an off-screen texture that the GPU renderer can draw to.  After rendering, the canvas can be composited to the screen (or another canvas) as a regular image.

items.name: Canvas
items.description: An off-screen render target with a fixed pixel resolution.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`.  The GPU-side texture is managed by `GpuRenderer`; this struct holds only the logical metadata exposed to the Lua API.

items.name: Color
items.description: RGBA color stored as `f32` components in the range `[0.0, 1.0]`.  Used everywhere the API accepts a color: `luna.graphics.setColor`, sprite tints, background color, etc.  # Fields - `r` вҖ” Red channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `g` вҖ” Green channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `b` вҖ” Blue channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `a` вҖ” Alpha channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`; `1.0` = fully opaque.

graphics::decal_surface.module_doc: Persistent surface for stamping decal textures.  A simple data container describing an off-screen render target onto which decals can be composited.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for decal surface-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `DecalSurface`. Primary functions: `new()`, `get_dimensions()`, `get_width()`, `get_height()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: DecalSurface
items.description: Persistent render target for stamping decals.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`.  Stores the target dimensions. Actual pixel data and GPU resources are managed separately by the renderer.

graphics::draw_layer.module_doc: Z-ordered draw layer for controlling render order.  Entries are queued with a z-order value and flushed in ascending order so that lower z-values are drawn first (back-to-front).  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for draw layer-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `LayerEntry`, `DrawLayer`. Primary functions: `new()`, `queue()`, `flush()`, `clear()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: LayerEntry
items.description: A queued draw entry with its z-order. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `z_order` вҖ” `f64`. - `callback_id` вҖ” `usize`.

items.name: DrawLayer
items.description: Z-ordered draw callback queue. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Collects entries during the draw phase and flushes them in z-order so that rendering happens back-to-front.  # Fields - `entries` вҖ” `Vec<LayerEntry>`. - `next_id` вҖ” `usize`.

graphics::font.module_doc: TTF/OTF font loading, glyph rasterization, and atlas packing for GPU text rendering.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for font-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Font`, `GlyphInfo`. Primary functions: `from_bytes()`, `ensure_glyph()`, `text_width()`, `line_height()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Font
items.description: A loaded TTF/OTF font with a glyph atlas for GPU rendering.  Wraps a `fontdue::Font` for parsing and rasterization, caches rasterized glyphs in a HashMap, and packs them into a row-based RGBA atlas bitmap.  # Fields - `inner` вҖ” `fontdue::Font`. - `size` вҖ” `f32`. - `glyphs` вҖ” `HashMap<char`. - `atlas_bitmap` вҖ” `Vec<u8>`. - `atlas_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `atlas_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `cursor_x` вҖ” `u32`. - `cursor_y` вҖ” `u32`. - `row_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `line_height` вҖ” `f32`. - `ascent` вҖ” `f32`. - `descent` вҖ” `f32`. - `dirty` вҖ” `bool`.

items.name: GlyphInfo
items.description: Information about a single rasterized glyph in the atlas.  # Fields - `uv_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `uv_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `uv_w` вҖ” `f32`. - `uv_h` вҖ” `f32`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `advance_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_y` вҖ” `f32`.  Contains UV coordinates for sampling the atlas texture and metric data for text layout (advance width, baseline offset).

graphics::gpu_renderer.module_doc: GPU-accelerated 2D renderer for Luna2D, backed by wgpu.  Processes a `DrawCommand` queue each frame, tessellates geometry on the CPU, uploads vertex / index data to GPU buffers, and issues a single render pass.  # Design - Two render pipelines: *color* (solid + gradient shapes) and *texture* (images, sprites). - Transform stack maintained during command processing вҖ” identical to Love2D `push/pop`. - Colored geometry is batched in one draw call; each distinct texture source is one draw call. - Draw order: colored shapes first (in submission order), textured images second.

items.name: RenderStats
items.description: Per-frame rendering statistics.  # Fields - `draw_calls` вҖ” `u32`. - `texture_switches` вҖ” `u32`. - `canvas_switches` вҖ” `u32`. - `shader_switches` вҖ” `u32`. - `batched_draws` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: GpuRenderer
items.description: GPU-accelerated renderer that processes `DrawCommand` queues via wgpu.  # Fields - `device` вҖ” `wgpu::Device`. - `queue` вҖ” `wgpu::Queue`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `render_stats` вҖ” `RenderStats`.  Owns the wgpu `Device` and `Queue`. The caller (App) owns the `Surface` and configuration; `render_frame` receives a shared reference each frame.

graphics::image_effect.module_doc: Lightweight per-image shader-effect pass data ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Tier 1 graphics layer.  [`ShaderPassDescriptor`] describes one shader pass in a per-image effect chain. This type lives in Tier 1 and has **no imports from `src/postfx/`**.  The bridge layer (`lua_api`) creates `Vec<ShaderPassDescriptor>` values (via `ImageEffect::to_passes()`) and embeds them into `DrawCommand` variants before the frame is submitted to the GPU renderer.

items.name: ShaderPassDescriptor
items.description: One shader pass in a per-image effect chain.  Carries the built-in effect name, a flat float-parameter map, and an enabled flag. The GPU renderer matches `effect_name` against known built-in effect names to select the correct shader pass. Passes with `enabled = false` are skipped during rendering.  # Fields - `effect_name` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ `String` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Built-in effect name (e.g. `"blur"`, `"vignette"`). - `params` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ `HashMap<String, f32>` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ Named float parameters for the shader. - `enabled` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ `bool` ГўвӮ¬вҖқ When `false` this pass is skipped by the renderer.

graphics::mesh.module_doc: Mesh API for custom geometry rendering.  Meshes allow Lua scripts to define arbitrary vertex data for rendering textured or colored geometry.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mesh-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `MeshDrawMode`, `MeshVertex`, `Mesh`. Primary functions: `new()`, `from_vertices()`, `set_vertex()`, `get_vertex()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: MeshDrawMode
items.description: Drawing mode for mesh geometry. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Triangles` вҖ” Triangles variant. - `Fan` вҖ” Fan variant. - `Strip` вҖ” Strip variant.

items.name: MeshVertex
items.description: A single vertex in a mesh. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `u` вҖ” `f32`. - `v` вҖ” `f32`. - `r` вҖ” `f32`. - `g` вҖ” `f32`. - `b` вҖ” `f32`. - `a` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: Mesh
items.description: Custom geometry mesh with per-vertex position, UV, and color data.  # Fields - `vertices` вҖ” `Vec<MeshVertex>`. - `indices` вҖ” `Option<Vec<u32>>`. - `texture` вҖ” `Option<TextureKey>`. - `draw_mode` вҖ” `MeshDrawMode`.

graphics.module_doc: Mod implementation for the `graphics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: canvas
items.description: Off-screen render targets (canvases) for deferred compositing.

items.name: decal_surface
items.description: Persistent surface for stamping decal textures.

items.name: draw_layer
items.description: Z-ordered draw layer for controlling render order.

items.name: font
items.description: TTF/OTF font loading, glyph rasterization, and atlas packing.

items.name: gpu_renderer
items.description: GPU-accelerated renderer backed by wgpu (primary runtime renderer).

items.name: image_effect
items.description: Lightweight per-image shader-effect pass data for the draw command pipeline.

items.name: mesh
items.description: Custom geometry mesh with per-vertex position, UV, and color data.

items.name: nine_slice
items.description: Nine-slice (9-patch) image rendering for scalable UI elements.

items.name: renderer
items.description: DrawCommand queue, draw enums, and texture data types.

items.name: shader
items.description: Custom WGSL shader support with uniform variables.

items.name: shape
items.description: Compound shape builder that accumulates vector primitives for batched drawing.

items.name: sprite
items.description: Sprite struct combining a texture, transform, and tint color.

items.name: sprite_batch
items.description: Sprite batching for efficient rendering of many sprites sharing one texture.

items.name: sprite_sheet
items.description: Grid-based sprite sheet with directional support and named groups.

items.name: texture
items.description: Texture loading and TextureData storage for the renderer.

items.name: texture_atlas
items.description: CPU-side bin-packing texture atlas using shelf algorithm.

graphics::nine_slice.module_doc: Nine-slice (9-patch) image rendering for scalable UI elements.  A nine-slice divides a texture into 9 regions using 4 border insets: corners are drawn at fixed size, edges stretch in one axis, and the center stretches in both axes. This preserves visual quality when scaling UI panels, buttons, and dialog boxes.

items.name: Patch
items.description: A single patch rectangle: `(src_x, src_y, src_w, src_h, dst_x, dst_y, dst_w, dst_h)`.

items.name: NineSlice
items.description: A nine-slice image definition: a texture plus border insets.  The four insets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) define pixel distances from each edge of the source texture. These divide the texture into 9 regions:  ```text в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ TL    в”Ӯ    Top Edge      в”Ӯ  TR   в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө в”Ӯ Left  в”Ӯ    Center        в”Ӯ Right в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Edge  в”Ӯ   (stretches)    в”Ӯ Edge  в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө в”Ӯ BL    в”Ӯ   Bottom Edge    в”Ӯ  BR   в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ ```  Corners maintain their original pixel dimensions. Edges stretch along one axis. The center stretches in both axes.  # Fields - `e` вҖ” `:textures` for the source image.`. - `texture_key` вҖ” `TextureKey`. - `top` вҖ” `f32`. - `right` вҖ” `f32`. - `bottom` вҖ” `f32`. - `left` вҖ” `f32`. - `tex_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `tex_height` вҖ” `f32`.

graphics::renderer.module_doc: This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for renderer-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `CompareMode`, `StencilAction`, `TextAlign`, `DrawMode`, `BlendMode`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: CompareMode
items.description: Stencil comparison mode for `luna.graphics.setStencilTest`.  # Variants - `Equal` вҖ” Equal variant. - `NotEqual` вҖ” NotEqual variant. - `Less` вҖ” Less variant. - `LessEqual` вҖ” LessEqual variant. - `Greater` вҖ” Greater variant. - `GreaterEqual` вҖ” GreaterEqual variant. - `Always` вҖ” Always variant. - `Never` вҖ” Never variant.

items.name: StencilAction
items.description: Stencil write action for `luna.graphics.stencil` and `luna.graphics.setStencilMode`.  # Variants - `Keep` вҖ” Keep variant. - `Zero` вҖ” Zero variant. - `Replace` вҖ” Replace variant. - `Increment` вҖ” Increment variant. - `Decrement` вҖ” Decrement variant. - `IncrementWrap` вҖ” IncrementWrap variant. - `DecrementWrap` вҖ” DecrementWrap variant. - `Invert` вҖ” Invert variant.

items.name: StencilMode
items.description: Combined stencil rendering mode stored in `SharedState`.  Controls what happens to stencil buffer values when draws occur (`action`), the test that subsequent draws must pass (`compare`), and the reference value (`value`) used by the comparison.  The GPU applies this lazily when the pipeline is rebuilt.  # Fields - `action` вҖ” `StencilAction`. - `compare` вҖ” `CompareMode`. - `value` вҖ” `u8`.

items.name: DepthMode
items.description: Depth test comparison mode for `luna.graphics.setDepthMode`.  # Variants - `Always` вҖ” Always variant. - `Never` вҖ” Never variant. - `Less` вҖ” Less variant. - `LessEqual` вҖ” LessEqual variant. - `Equal` вҖ” Equal variant. - `NotEqual` вҖ” NotEqual variant. - `Greater` вҖ” Greater variant. - `GreaterEqual` вҖ” GreaterEqual variant.

items.name: TextAlign
items.description: Text alignment mode for formatted text printing.  # Variants - `Left` вҖ” Left variant. - `Center` вҖ” Center variant. - `Right` вҖ” Right variant. - `Justify` вҖ” Justify variant.

items.name: DrawMode
items.description: Whether a shape is drawn filled or as an outline.  # Variants - `Fill` вҖ” Draw shapes as solid filled areas. - `Line` вҖ” Draw shapes as outlines using the current line width.

items.name: BlendMode
items.description: Blending mode for draw operations. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Alpha` вҖ” Alpha variant. - `Add` вҖ” Add variant. - `Multiply` вҖ” Multiply variant. - `Replace` вҖ” Replace variant. - `Screen` вҖ” Screen variant.

items.name: DrawCommand
items.description: A single deferred draw operation queued during `luna.draw()` and executed by `GpuRenderer`.  # Variants - `SetColor(r, g, b, a)` вҖ” Sets the active draw color; affects all subsequent draw calls. - `Rectangle` вҖ” Draws an axis-aligned rectangle. - `RoundedRectangle` вҖ” Draws a rectangle with rounded corners. - `Circle` вҖ” Draws a circle at center `(x, y)` with radius `r`. - `Ellipse` вҖ” Draws an ellipse with semi-axes `rx`, `ry`. - `Triangle` вҖ” Draws a triangle with three explicit vertices. - `Polygon` вҖ” Draws an arbitrary polygon from a flat `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]` vertex list. - `Line` вҖ” Draws a line segment from `(x1, y1)` to `(x2, y2)`. - `Polyline` вҖ” Draws a connected sequence of line segments through a flat `[x0,y0,x1,y1,вҖҰ]` list. - `DrawImage` вҖ” Blits a texture (by index) at position `(x, y)`. - `DrawImageEx` вҖ” Draws a texture with full transform (rotation, scale, origin offset). - `DrawQuad` вҖ” Draws a sub-region (quad) of a texture with full transform. - `Print` вҖ” Renders text using the built-in bitmap font. - `SetLineWidth` вҖ” Sets the stroke width for subsequent outline draws. - `PushTransform` вҖ” Saves the current transform onto the stack. - `PopTransform` вҖ” Restores the previous transform from the stack. - `Translate` вҖ” Applies a translation to the current transform. - `Rotate` вҖ” Applies a rotation (in radians) to the current transform. - `Scale` вҖ” Applies a scale to the current transform. - `Arc` вҖ” Draws an arc (sector or outline) of a circle. - `DrawShape` вҖ” Draw all primitives in a compound shape with a unified affine transform. - `DrawParticleSystem` вҖ” Renders all particles in a single batched command, dispatching per-shape geometry helpers internally. - `BeginPostFx` вҖ” Start capturing the framebuffer for post-processing; subsequent draw calls render into the capture target. - `EndPostFx` вҖ” Stop capturing; resume rendering to the previous target. - `ApplyPostFx` вҖ” Apply the registered post-processing effects from the named stack and composite the result onto the current target.

items.name: TextureData
items.description: Raw RGBA pixel data for a loaded texture, stored in the renderer's texture atlas.  # Fields - `pixels` вҖ” Raw RGBA byte data in RGBA format. - `width` вҖ” Texture width in pixels. - `height` вҖ” Texture height in pixels.

items.name: ParticleRenderShape
items.description: Geometric shape used when rendering a single untextured particle via `DrawParticleSystem`.  This mirrors `particle::ParticleShape` one-for-one. The particle module converts `ParticleShape` -> `ParticleRenderShape` when building the `DrawParticleSystem` command, keeping the Tier-1 `graphics` module free of an upward Tier-2 import.  # Variants - `Square` вҖ” Square variant. - `Circle` вҖ” Circle variant. - `Triangle` вҖ” Triangle variant. - `Spark` вҖ” Spark variant. - `Diamond` вҖ” Diamond variant.

items.name: ParticleInstance
items.description: Per-particle render data for a single frame.  Produced by `ParticleSystem::draw_commands()` and consumed by the GPU renderer's `DrawParticleSystem` arm. All positions are in world space.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `r` вҖ” `f32`. - `g` вҖ” `f32`. - `b` вҖ” `f32`. - `a` вҖ” `f32`. - `rotation` вҖ” `f32`. - `size` вҖ” `f32`. - `shape` вҖ” `ParticleRenderShape`. - `texture_key` вҖ” `Option<TextureKey>`. - `quad` вҖ” `Option<[f32; 4]>`. - `quad_tex_dims` вҖ” `Option<(f32, f32)>`.

items.name: DrawableKind
items.description: Type discriminator for resources that can be passed to luna.graphics.draw.  Used to dispatch the polymorphic draw(drawable, ...) Lua API to the correct DrawCommand variant based on resource type.  # Variants - Image(TextureKey) вҖ” A loaded texture (Image). - Canvas(CanvasKey) вҖ” An off-screen render target. - SpriteBatch(SpriteBatchKey) вҖ” A batched sprite collection. - Mesh(MeshKey) вҖ” A custom geometry mesh.

graphics::shader.module_doc: Custom WGSL shader support for Luna2D.  Allows Lua scripts to create and apply custom fragment shaders with uniform variables.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for shader-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ShaderFragmentInput`, `Shader`, `UniformValue`. Primary functions: `new()`, `send()`, `has_uniform()`, `ordered_uniforms()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ShaderFragmentInput
items.description: Which fragment shader input the user's entry point expects.  # Variants - `Color` вҖ” Color variant. - `Uv` вҖ” Uv variant.

items.name: Shader
items.description: Represents a compiled custom shader with its uniform values.  # Fields - `source` вҖ” `String`. - `wrapper_source` вҖ” `String`. - `fragment_entry_name` вҖ” `String`. - `fragment_inputs` вҖ” `Vec<ShaderFragmentInput>`. - `uniforms` вҖ” `HashMap<String, UniformValue>`.  Currently stores shader source and uniforms. The GPU pipeline is created lazily when the shader is first used during rendering.

items.name: UniformValue
items.description: A uniform value that can be sent to a shader from Lua.  # Variants - `Float` вҖ” Float variant. - `Vec2` вҖ” Vec2 variant. - `Vec3` вҖ” Vec3 variant. - `Vec4` вҖ” Vec4 variant. - `Int` вҖ” Int variant. - `Bool` вҖ” Bool variant.

graphics::shape.module_doc: Compound shape builder for multi-primitive vector drawing.  This module provides [`CompoundShape`] and [`ShapeCommand`], which together implement an object-space command buffer for vector primitives.  A Lua script builds up a [`CompoundShape`] once, then replays it any number of times via [`crate::graphics::DrawCommand::DrawShape`] with a per-call affine transform.  **Tier position**: Tier 1 (`graphics`). Imports only from within the `graphics` crate-module (`super::renderer`). No imports from Tier 2вҖ“3 modules or from `lua_api`.

items.name: ShapeCommand
items.description: A single drawing command stored inside a [`CompoundShape`] command queue.  This is a restricted subset of [`crate::graphics::DrawCommand`] вҖ” only primitive draw commands and rendering-state setters are included. Transform commands (`PushTransform`, `Translate`, etc.) are intentionally excluded: the shape's transform is applied once at draw time by the `DrawCommand::DrawShape` wrapper.  # Variants - `SetColor` вҖ” changes the active color for following commands. - `SetLineWidth` вҖ” changes the line width for following outlined commands. - `Rectangle` вҖ” draws an axis-aligned rectangle, filled or outlined. - `RoundedRectangle` вҖ” draws a rectangle with per-axis corner radii. - `Circle` вҖ” draws a circle at centre `(x, y)` with radius `r`. - `Ellipse` вҖ” draws an ellipse with semi-axes `rx`, `ry`. - `Triangle` вҖ” draws a triangle with three explicit vertices. - `Polygon` вҖ” draws an arbitrary polygon from a flat vertex list. - `Line` вҖ” draws a single line segment. - `Polyline` вҖ” draws a connected sequence of line segments. - `Arc` вҖ” draws an arc (sector or outline) of a circle.

items.name: CompoundShape
items.description: A compound shape that accumulates draw primitives in local (object-space) coordinates and replays them as a unified entity via [`crate::graphics::DrawCommand::DrawShape`].  Build up commands with [`push_command`](CompoundShape::push_command), then submit a [`crate::graphics::DrawCommand::DrawShape`] each frame to draw with an affine transform.  # Fields - `commands` вҖ” `Vec<ShapeCommand>`. The ordered list of drawing commands. - `current_color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Last color set via a `SetColor` command (retained for reference). - `current_line_width` вҖ” `f32`. Last line width set via a `SetLineWidth` command.

graphics::sprite.module_doc: Sprite implementation for the `graphics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for sprite-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Sprite`. Primary functions: `new()`, `set_position()`, `set_scale()`, `set_rotation()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Sprite
items.description: A textured game object with position, scale, rotation, and tint color.  `Sprite` acts as a transform + tint wrapper around a `Texture`. It does not own the texture; it references it by id in the renderer's texture atlas.  # Fields - `texture_id` вҖ” Index into the renderer's texture atlas. - `position` вҖ” World-space position of the sprite's origin. - `scale` вҖ” Per-axis scale factor; `Vec2::ONE` = original size. - `rotation` вҖ” Rotation in radians. - `color` вҖ” Multiplicative tint applied to the texture; `Color::WHITE` = no tint.

graphics::sprite_batch.module_doc: Sprite batching for efficient rendering of many sprites sharing one texture.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for sprite batch-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SpriteBatch`, `BatchEntry`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add()`, `clear()`, `texture_key()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SpriteBatch
items.description: A batch of sprites sharing a single texture, drawn in one GPU call.  Created via `luna.graphics.newSpriteBatch(image_id, max_sprites?)`. Use `add()` to queue sprites and the engine draws them all at once.  # Fields - `e` вҖ” `:textures`.`. - `texture_key` вҖ” `TextureKey`. - `entries` вҖ” `Vec<BatchEntry>`. - `max_entries` вҖ” `usize`.

items.name: BatchEntry
items.description: A single sprite in a batch, describing position, region, and transform.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `quad_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `quad_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `quad_w` вҖ” `f32`. - `quad_h` вҖ” `f32`. - `rotation` вҖ” `f32`. - `sx` вҖ” `f32`. - `sy` вҖ” `f32`. - `ox` вҖ” `f32`. - `oy` вҖ” `f32`.

graphics::sprite_sheet.module_doc: Grid-based sprite sheet with directional support and named groups.  Splits a texture into a uniform grid of frames and provides indexed, row/column, named-group, and directional access to the resulting quads.

items.name: FrameGroup
items.description: Named frame group within the sprite sheet.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `start_frame` вҖ” `usize`. - `count` вҖ” `usize`.

items.name: DirectionLayout
items.description: Directional layout for sprite sets. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Rows` вҖ” Rows variant. - `Columns` вҖ” Columns variant.

items.name: SpriteSheet
items.description: Grid-based sprite sheet with directional support and named groups.  # Fields - `frame_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `frame_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `columns` вҖ” `u32`. - `rows` вҖ” `u32`. - `texture_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `texture_height` вҖ” `u32`.  Divides a texture into equal-sized cells and pre-computes UV quads for every frame. Supports named groups and 4/8-directional layouts.

graphics::texture.module_doc: Texture implementation for the `graphics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for texture-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Texture`. Primary functions: `load()`, `from_rgba()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Texture
items.description: A loaded image asset referenced by its index into the renderer's texture list.  `Texture` is a lightweight handle; the actual pixel data lives in `TextureData` inside `Renderer::textures` (or `SharedState::textures`).  # Fields - `id` вҖ” Index into the renderer's `TextureData` list. - `width` вҖ” Image width in pixels. - `height` вҖ” Image height in pixels.

graphics::texture_atlas.module_doc: CPU-side bin-packing texture atlas using a shelf algorithm.  Packs named rectangular regions into a fixed-size atlas without any GPU interaction. Useful for building sprite-sheet layouts at load time.

items.name: AtlasRegion
items.description: A named rectangular region packed into the atlas.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `x` вҖ” `u32`. - `y` вҖ” `u32`. - `w` вҖ” `u32`. - `h` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: TextureAtlas
items.description: CPU-side bin-packing atlas for sprite regions.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `padding` вҖ” `u32`.  Uses a shelf-packing algorithm: regions are placed left-to-right on horizontal shelves. When a region does not fit on any existing shelf a new shelf is opened below the last one.

gui::containers.module_doc: Container and layout widgets: Panel, Layout, ScrollPanel, NinePatch.  Containers hold child widgets and optionally apply layout rules to them. [`Panel`] is the simplest container.  [`Layout`] adds flexbox-inspired positioning (horizontal, vertical, grid).  [`ScrollPanel`] provides a scrollable viewport.  [`NinePatch`] computes nine-slice rectangles for scalable panel borders.

items.name: Panel
items.description: Generic container widget that holds an ordered list of children.  The invisible root of the GUI widget tree is a `Panel`.  Panels can have a title and optional scrolling behaviour.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `children` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Indices of child widgets in the context's pool. - `title` вҖ” `String`. Optional panel title. - `scrollable` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the panel clips and scrolls overflow.

items.name: LayoutDirection
items.description: Direction in which a [`Layout`] positions its children.  # Variants - `Vertical` вҖ” Children stack top-to-bottom. - `Horizontal` вҖ” Children flow left-to-right. - `Grid` вҖ” Children placed in a fixed-column grid.

items.name: Layout
items.description: Flexbox-inspired layout container.  Arranges child widgets along a main axis (vertical, horizontal, or grid) with configurable spacing, wrapping, cross-axis alignment, and main-axis justification.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `children` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Child widget indices. - `direction` вҖ” `LayoutDirection`. Main axis direction. - `spacing` вҖ” `f32`. Gap between children in pixels. - `columns` вҖ” `usize`. Column count for grid mode. - `wrap` вҖ” `bool`. Whether children wrap to the next line. - `align` вҖ” `String`. Cross-axis alignment: `"start"`, `"center"`, `"end"`, `"stretch"`. - `justify` вҖ” `String`. Main-axis justification: `"start"`, `"center"`, `"end"`, `"space-between"`, `"space-around"`, `"space-evenly"`.

items.name: ScrollPanel
items.description: Scrollable viewport container.  Clips child content to its own bounds and offsets children by a scroll position.  Scroll speed controls how fast wheel events translate into scroll offset changes.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `children` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Child widget indices. - `content_width` вҖ” `f32`. Total scrollable content width. - `content_height` вҖ” `f32`. Total scrollable content height. - `scroll_x` вҖ” `f32`. Current horizontal scroll offset. - `scroll_y` вҖ” `f32`. Current vertical scroll offset. - `scroll_speed` вҖ” `f32`. Wheel-to-scroll multiplier.

items.name: NineSlice
items.description: A nine-slice rectangle: `(sx, sy, sw, sh, dx, dy, dw, dh)`.

items.name: NinePatch
items.description: Nine-slice data for scalable panel border rendering.  Given the source image dimensions and inset offsets from each edge, the `get_slices` method computes nine `(sx, sy, sw, sh, dx, dy, dw, dh)` rectangles that tile the source image onto the destination widget bounds without distorting corners.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `inset_left` вҖ” `u32`. Left inset in source image pixels. - `inset_top` вҖ” `u32`. Top inset in source image pixels. - `inset_right` вҖ” `u32`. Right inset in source image pixels. - `inset_bottom` вҖ” `u32`. Bottom inset in source image pixels. - `image_width` вҖ” `u32`. Source image width. - `image_height` вҖ” `u32`. Source image height.

items.name: GUIWindow
items.description: A draggable, closeable window container.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `title` вҖ” `String`. Window title bar text. - `closeable` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the close button is shown. - `draggable` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the window can be dragged. - `resizable` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the window can be resized. - `children` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Child widget indices.

items.name: SplitPanel
items.description: A resizable split panel with two child regions.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `orientation` вҖ” `String`. `"horizontal"` or `"vertical"`. - `split_position` вҖ” `f32`. Split ratio 0.0вҖ“1.0. - `min_panel_size` вҖ” `f32`. Minimum panel size in pixels. - `first_child` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. First panel widget index. - `second_child` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Second panel widget index.

items.name: DockPanel
items.description: A dock-based layout container with left/right/top/bottom/center regions.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `docked` вҖ” `Vec<(usize, String)>`. Pairs of `(widget_index, side)`. - `split_sizes` вҖ” `Vec<(String, f32)>`. Per-side size overrides.

gui::context.module_doc: Root widget tree, focus tracking, toast queue, and input routing.  [`GuiContext`] is the central coordinator for the GUI system.  It owns a flat pool of type-erased widgets (stored as [`WidgetKind`] enum variants), tracks which widget has keyboard focus, manages a queue of active [`Toast`](super::Toast) notifications, and optionally holds a [`Theme`] for styled rendering.  Widgets are identified by a `usize` index into the internal `widgets` vector.  The root panel is always at index `0`.  Container widgets (`Panel`, `Layout`, `ScrollPanel`) store their children's indices.  Input events are forwarded from Lua callbacks and dispatched to the widget tree by hit-testing against widget bounds.

items.name: GuiEvent
items.description: A single interaction event emitted by the GUI widget tree.  Accumulated by [`GuiContext`] in `pending_events` and drained by the Lua bridge each frame to dispatch registered Lua callbacks.  # Variants - `Click(usize)` вҖ” A button-family widget at the given index was activated. - `Change(usize)` вҖ” A value-bearing widget changed its value. - `Close(usize)` вҖ” A dismissible widget was closed. - `Select(usize, usize)` вҖ” An item in a list/tab widget was selected; (widget_idx, item_idx).

items.name: WidgetKind
items.description: Type-erased widget storage.  Each variant wraps a concrete widget type so that the [`GuiContext`] can store all widgets in a single `Vec<WidgetKind>`.  # Variants - `Button` вҖ” Wraps a [`Button`]. - `Label` вҖ” Wraps a [`Label`]. - `TextInput` вҖ” Wraps a [`TextInput`]. - `CheckBox` вҖ” Wraps a [`CheckBox`]. - `Slider` вҖ” Wraps a [`Slider`]. - `ProgressBar` вҖ” Wraps a [`ProgressBar`]. - `ComboBox` вҖ” Wraps a [`ComboBox`]. - `ListBox` вҖ” Wraps a [`ListBox`]. - `Panel` вҖ” Wraps a [`Panel`]. - `Layout` вҖ” Wraps a [`Layout`]. - `ScrollPanel` вҖ” Wraps a [`ScrollPanel`]. - `NinePatch` вҖ” Wraps a [`NinePatch`]. - `TabBar` вҖ” Wraps a [`TabBar`]. - `Toast` вҖ” Wraps a [`Toast`]. - `Separator` вҖ” Wraps a [`Separator`]. - `Spacer` вҖ” Wraps a [`Spacer`]. - `TreeView` вҖ” Wraps a [`TreeView`]. - `RadioButton` вҖ” Wraps a [`RadioButton`]. - `ScrollBar` вҖ” Wraps a [`ScrollBar`]. - `GUIWindow` вҖ” Wraps a [`GUIWindow`]. - `SplitPanel` вҖ” Wraps a [`SplitPanel`]. - `DockPanel` вҖ” Wraps a [`DockPanel`]. - `Toolbar` вҖ” Wraps a [`Toolbar`]. - `MenuBar` вҖ” Wraps a [`MenuBar`]. - `MenuItem` вҖ” Wraps a [`MenuItem`]. - `Dialog` вҖ” Wraps a [`Dialog`]. - `StatusBar` вҖ” Wraps a [`StatusBar`]. - `Accordion` вҖ” Wraps a [`Accordion`]. - `TooltipPanel` вҖ” Wraps a [`TooltipPanel`]. - `ColorPicker` вҖ” Wraps a [`ColorPicker`]. - `GUITable` вҖ” Wraps a [`GUITable`]. - `ImageWidget` вҖ” Wraps an [`ImageWidget`].

items.name: GuiContext
items.description: Central GUI coordinator: widget pool, focus, toasts, and theme.  The context owns a flat `Vec<WidgetKind>` indexed by widget ID.  Index `0` is always the invisible root `Panel`.  New widgets are appended with `add_*` methods that return the widget's index.  Focus is tracked by `focused_widget: Option<usize>`.  The toast queue is a separate `Vec<Toast>` that is updated and drained by `update(dt)`.  # Fields - `widgets` вҖ” `Vec<WidgetKind>`. Flat pool of all widgets. - `focused_widget` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Index of the focused widget. - `toasts` вҖ” `Vec<Toast>`. Active toast notifications. - `theme` вҖ” `Option<Theme>`. Active theme. - `pending_events` вҖ” `Vec<GuiEvent>`. Interaction events queued since the last drain.

gui::controls.module_doc: Interactive and display control widgets.  This sub-module provides the leaf-level widgets that users interact with or read information from: [`Button`], [`Label`], [`TextInput`], [`CheckBox`], [`Slider`], [`ProgressBar`], [`ComboBox`], [`ListBox`], and [`TabBar`].  Each widget embeds a [`WidgetBase`](super::WidgetBase) for shared properties and adds type-specific data fields.

items.name: Button
items.description: Clickable button widget.  Displays a text label and triggers an `onClick` callback (registered via Lua) when the mouse is pressed and released within its bounds.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Button label text.

items.name: Label
items.description: Static text label widget.  Displays read-only text.  Does not receive focus or respond to input events.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Display text.

items.name: TextInput
items.description: Editable single-line text input field.  Supports placeholder text, maximum character length, cursor position tracking, and an `onChange` callback triggered when the text mutates.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Current input value. - `placeholder` вҖ” `String`. Greyed-out hint when empty. - `max_length` вҖ” `usize`. Maximum character count (0 = unlimited). - `cursor_pos` вҖ” `usize`. Byte offset of the editing cursor. - `focused` вҖ” `bool`. Whether this input has keyboard focus.

items.name: CheckBox
items.description: Toggle check-box widget with an associated label.  Fires an `onChange` callback (via Lua) when toggled.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Label text shown beside the check box. - `checked` вҖ” `bool`. Current toggle state.

items.name: Slider
items.description: Numeric value slider widget.  Allows the user to drag a thumb along a track to select a value between `min` and `max`, snapped to `step` increments.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `value` вҖ” `f64`. Current value. - `min` вҖ” `f64`. Minimum allowed value. - `max` вҖ” `f64`. Maximum allowed value. - `step` вҖ” `f64`. Snap increment (0 = continuous).

items.name: ProgressBar
items.description: Read-only progress indicator widget.  Displays a filled bar proportional to `value` within `[min, max]`.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `value` вҖ” `f64`. Current value. - `min` вҖ” `f64`. Minimum value. - `max` вҖ” `f64`. Maximum value.

items.name: ComboBox
items.description: Drop-down selection widget.  Maintains a list of string items and a 0-based selected index.  The Lua API exposes 1-based indices for Lua convention.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `items` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. Item list. - `selected_index` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. 0-based selected index. - `open` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the dropdown is expanded.

items.name: ListBox
items.description: Scrollable list of selectable items.  Maintains a list of string items, a 0-based selected index, and a configurable per-item height.  The Lua API exposes 1-based indices.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `items` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. Item list. - `selected_index` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. 0-based selected index. - `item_height` вҖ” `f32`. Height of each item row in pixels.

items.name: TabBar
items.description: Tabbed page selector widget.  Manages a list of tab labels and tracks the currently active tab (0-based internally, 1-based in Lua).  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `tabs` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. Tab label list. - `active_tab` вҖ” `usize`. 0-based index of the active tab.

items.name: RadioButton
items.description: A grouped radio button with mutually exclusive selection.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Display label next to the radio circle. - `selected` вҖ” `bool`. Whether this radio button is currently selected. - `group` вҖ” `String`. Group name вҖ” only one radio in a group can be selected.

items.name: ScrollBar
items.description: A scroll bar for scrollable content areas.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `position` вҖ” `f32`. Current scroll position. - `content_size` вҖ” `f32`. Total content size. - `view_size` вҖ” `f32`. Visible viewport size. - `vertical` вҖ” `bool`. Orientation.

gui::data_graph_renderer.module_doc: Mathematical function graph and chart renderer.  [`GraphRenderer`] manages multiple named data series (line, scatter, bar) and provides viewport вҶ” world coordinate mapping for rendering charts in a Luna2D game. The renderer stores pure data вҖ” actual draw calls are issued by the Lua wrapper in `graphics_api.rs`.

items.name: GraphSeries
items.description: A data series that can be added to a [`GraphRenderer`].  # Variants - `Line` вҖ” Line variant. - `Scatter` вҖ” Scatter variant. - `Bar` вҖ” Bar variant.

items.name: GraphRenderer
items.description: Mathematical function graph / chart renderer.  # Fields - `viewport` вҖ” `(f32, f32, f32, f32)`. - `range` вҖ” `(f64, f64, f64, f64)`. - `show_grid` вҖ” `bool`. - `show_axes` вҖ” `bool`. - `show_labels` вҖ” `bool`. - `grid_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `axis_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `bg_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `title` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `x_label` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `y_label` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `cursor` вҖ” `Option<(f64, f64)>`.  Stores data series and rendering options. Use [`world_to_screen`](Self::world_to_screen) and [`screen_to_world`](Self::screen_to_world) for coordinate mapping.

gui::extras.module_doc: Utility widgets: toast notifications, separators, spacers, and tree views.  These widgets serve auxiliary UI roles вҖ” visual dividers, empty spacing, auto-expiring notification overlays, and collapsible hierarchical trees.

items.name: Toast
items.description: Auto-expiring notification overlay.  A toast displays a message for a configurable duration, tracking elapsed time so that the GUI context can remove it once it expires.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `message` вҖ” `String`. Notification text. - `duration` вҖ” `f32`. Total display time in seconds. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32`. Time elapsed since display started.

items.name: Separator
items.description: Visual divider line widget.  Draws a horizontal or vertical line with a configurable thickness.  Does not accept input or hold children.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `vertical` вҖ” `bool`. `true` for a vertical line, `false` for horizontal. - `thickness` вҖ” `f32`. Line thickness in pixels.

items.name: Spacer
items.description: Empty layout filler widget.  Takes up space in a layout without rendering anything.  Useful for pushing other widgets apart.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties.

items.name: TreeNode
items.description: A single node in a [`TreeView`] hierarchy.  # Fields - `text` вҖ” `String`. Display label. - `children` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Indices of child nodes in the tree's flat pool. - `expanded` вҖ” `bool`. Whether child nodes are visible. - `parent` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Index of the parent node (None for root-level).

items.name: TreeView
items.description: Collapsible hierarchical tree widget.  Stores nodes in a flat `Vec` with parent/child index links. Root-level nodes have `parent = None`.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `nodes` вҖ” `Vec<TreeNode>`. Flat pool of all tree nodes. - `root_nodes` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Indices of root-level nodes. - `selected_node` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Index of the currently selected node.

items.name: ToolbarButton
items.description: A named action button entry in a [`Toolbar`].  Toolbar buttons are identified by a string `id`, allowing scripts to reference them by name after creation.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `String`. Unique identifier within the toolbar. - `tooltip` вҖ” `String`. Tooltip text shown on hover. - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the button can be interacted with. - `toggled` вҖ” `bool`. Latched pressed/toggled state.

items.name: Toolbar
items.description: A toolbar container for buttons and separators.  Named [`ToolbarButton`] entries are tracked in `buttons`; generic child widgets created through [`GuiContext`] APIs are tracked in `children`.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `orientation` вҖ” `String`. `"horizontal"` or `"vertical"`. - `children` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Generic child widget indices. - `buttons` вҖ” `Vec<ToolbarButton>`. Named toolbar button entries.

items.name: MenuBar
items.description: A horizontal menu bar.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `menus` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Top-level menu item widget indices.

items.name: MenuItem
items.description: A menu item usable in menus and context menus.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Item display text. - `shortcut` вҖ” `String`. Keyboard shortcut display text. - `checked` вҖ” `bool`. Check mark state. - `items` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`. Sub-item widget indices.

items.name: Dialog
items.description: A modal dialog window.  Dialogs display a title bar, an optional content widget, and a row of footer buttons.  The `open` flag drives visibility; Lua scripts toggle it via the `open()` and `close()` methods.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `title` вҖ” `String`. Dialog title. - `modal` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the dialog blocks background input. - `open` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the dialog is currently displayed. - `content_idx` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Index of the body content widget, if any. - `footer_buttons` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. Labels for footer action buttons.

items.name: StatusBar
items.description: A status bar with named sections.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `sections` вҖ” `Vec<(String, f32)>`. `(text, width)` pairs.

items.name: AccordionSection
items.description: A single section in an [`Accordion`].  # Fields - `title` вҖ” `String`. Section header text. - `content_idx` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Content widget index. - `expanded` вҖ” `bool`. Whether section is expanded.

items.name: Accordion
items.description: A collapsible accordion container with named sections.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `sections` вҖ” `Vec<AccordionSection>`. Accordion sections. - `exclusive` вҖ” `bool`. If true, only one section can be expanded at a time.

items.name: TooltipPanel
items.description: A rich tooltip panel attached to a target widget.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `text` вҖ” `String`. Tooltip text. - `delay` вҖ” `f32`. Hover delay in seconds before showing. - `target_idx` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Target widget index.

items.name: ColorPicker
items.description: A color picker widget with RGB/HSV/HSL modes.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `r` вҖ” `f32`. Red component 0.0вҖ“1.0. - `g` вҖ” `f32`. Green component 0.0вҖ“1.0. - `b` вҖ” `f32`. Blue component 0.0вҖ“1.0. - `a` вҖ” `f32`. Alpha component 0.0вҖ“1.0. - `show_alpha` вҖ” `bool`. Whether to show the alpha slider. - `color_mode` вҖ” `String`. `"rgb"`, `"hsv"`, or `"hsl"`.

items.name: TableColumn
items.description: A single column in a [`GUITable`].  # Fields - `header` вҖ” `String`. Column header text. - `width` вҖ” `f32`. Column width in pixels.

items.name: GUITable
items.description: A data table widget with sortable columns and selectable rows.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `columns` вҖ” `Vec<TableColumn>`. Column definitions. - `rows` вҖ” `Vec<Vec<String>>`. Row data. - `selected_row` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Currently selected row (0-based). - `sortable` вҖ” `bool`. Whether columns can be sorted by clicking headers.

items.name: ImageWidget
items.description: An image display widget.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared widget properties. - `scale_mode` вҖ” `String`. `"fit"`, `"fill"`, `"stretch"`, or `"none"`. - `tint` вҖ” `(f32, f32, f32, f32)`. RGBA tint colour.

gui.module_doc: Retained-mode 2D widget system for building in-game menus, HUDs, dialog boxes, and inventory screens.  The `gui` module provides a tree of widgets rooted at an invisible `Panel` returned by `getRoot()`.  Concrete widget types вҖ” `Button`, `Label`, `TextInput`, `CheckBox`, `Slider`, `ProgressBar`, `ListBox`, `ComboBox`, `ScrollPanel`, `NinePatch`, `Panel`, `Toast`, and many more вҖ” inherit shared base properties (position, size, visibility, padding, margin, z-order, anchor constraints, and flexbox layout settings) from `WidgetBase`.  A `Theme` maps `(WidgetType, WidgetState)` pairs to `WidgetStyle` records containing colours, font size, border width, and corner radius.  Input events are forwarded manually from `luna.mousepressed` / `luna.keypressed` etc., giving scripts full control over which GUI instance is active.  ## Sub-modules  | Sub-module | Exported types | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `widget` | [`WidgetBase`], [`WidgetState`], [`WidgetType`] | Shared base fields, state enum, type tag | | `theme` | [`Theme`], [`WidgetStyle`] | Per-widget-type per-state styling | | `containers` | [`Panel`], [`Layout`], [`ScrollPanel`], [`NinePatch`] | Layout containers and nine-patch slicer | | `controls` | [`Button`], [`Label`], [`TextInput`], [`CheckBox`], [`Slider`], [`ProgressBar`], [`ComboBox`], [`ListBox`], [`TabBar`] | Interactive and display controls | | `extras` | [`Toast`], [`Separator`], [`Spacer`], [`TreeNode`], [`TreeView`] | Utility widgets: notifications, separators, tree views | | `context` | [`GuiContext`] | Root widget tree, focus tracking, toast queue, input routing |  ## Tier  `gui` is a **Tier 2 вҖ” Engine Extension** module.  It may import `math`, `engine`, and all Tier 1 modules.  It must not import other Tier 2 modules.

items.name: containers
items.description: Layout primitives: panels, dock, scroll, window, nine-slice, split, and grid containers.

items.name: context
items.description: Root widget tree, focus management, input routing, and toast queue.

items.name: controls
items.description: Interactive leaf widgets: buttons, checkboxes, sliders, combo-boxes, text inputs, and more.

items.name: extras
items.description: Extended widgets: accordion, color picker, dialog, menu bar, status bar, tree view, and table.

items.name: theme
items.description: Visual theme tokens (colors, fonts, spacing) used across all widgets.

items.name: widget
items.description: Core widget trait and shared widget-state types.

items.name: data_graph_renderer
items.description: Mathematical function graph and chart renderer (data series, viewport mapping).

gui::theme.module_doc: Per-widget-type, per-state styling system.  A [`Theme`] maps `(WidgetType, WidgetState)` pairs to [`WidgetStyle`] records.  When the GUI context draws a widget it looks up the style for the widget's type and current state, falling back to the `Normal` state style if no state-specific entry exists, and finally to a hard-coded default if the type has no theme entry at all.  The Lua API exposes `luna.gui.newTheme()`, `theme:setStyle()`, and `luna.gui.setTheme()` so game scripts can fully customise appearance without touching Rust.

items.name: WidgetStyle
items.description: Visual style record applied to a specific widget type in a specific state.  All colour values are RGBA in `[0.0, 1.0]`.  Font size is in pixels. Border width and corner radius are in pixels.  # Fields - `bg_color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Background fill colour (RGBA). - `fg_color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Foreground / text colour (RGBA). - `border_color` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Border stroke colour (RGBA). - `border_width` вҖ” `f32`. Border stroke width in pixels. - `corner_radius` вҖ” `f32`. Rounded corner radius in pixels. - `font_size` вҖ” `f32`. Text size in pixels.

items.name: Theme
items.description: Theme registry that maps `(WidgetType, WidgetState)` pairs to [`WidgetStyle`].  The lookup order when resolving a style for a widget: 1. Exact `(type, state)` entry. 2. Fallback to `(type, Normal)`. 3. Fallback to `WidgetStyle::default()`.  # Fields - `styles` вҖ” `HashMap<(WidgetType, WidgetState), WidgetStyle>`. Style store.

gui::widget.module_doc: Shared widget base fields, state enum, and type tag.  Every concrete widget embeds a [`WidgetBase`] that provides position, size, visibility, enable state, padding, margin, z-order, min/max size constraints, anchor edges, and flexbox layout properties.  The [`WidgetState`] enum models the five visual states a widget can be in (normal, hovered, pressed, focused, disabled), and [`WidgetType`] tags each concrete kind so the theme system can key its style lookup.

items.name: WidgetState
items.description: Visual interaction state of a widget.  The GUI system transitions a widget through these states in response to input events forwarded from the Lua game loop.  The theme uses the current state to select the appropriate [`WidgetStyle`](super::WidgetStyle).  # Variants - `Normal` вҖ” Default idle appearance. - `Hovered` вҖ” Mouse cursor is inside the widget bounds. - `Pressed` вҖ” Mouse button is held down on the widget. - `Focused` вҖ” Widget has keyboard focus (tab navigation). - `Disabled` вҖ” Widget is inactive; input events are ignored.

items.name: WidgetType
items.description: Type tag identifying a concrete widget kind.  Used as a key (together with [`WidgetState`]) in the theme system so that each widget type can have its own styled appearance per state.  # Variants - `Button` вҖ” Clickable button. - `Label` вҖ” Static text label. - `TextInput` вҖ” Editable single-line text field. - `CheckBox` вҖ” Toggle check box. - `Slider` вҖ” Numeric value slider. - `ProgressBar` вҖ” Read-only progress indicator. - `ComboBox` вҖ” Drop-down selection. - `ListBox` вҖ” Scrollable list of selectable items. - `Panel` вҖ” Generic container. - `Layout` вҖ” Flexbox layout container. - `ScrollPanel` вҖ” Scrollable viewport. - `NinePatch` вҖ” Nine-slice scalable panel. - `TabBar` вҖ” Tabbed page selector. - `Toast` вҖ” Auto-expiring notification. - `Separator` вҖ” Visual divider line. - `Spacer` вҖ” Empty spacing filler. - `TreeView` вҖ” Collapsible tree of nodes. - `RadioButton` вҖ” Grouped radio button. - `ScrollBar` вҖ” Scroll bar for scrollable areas. - `GUIWindow` вҖ” Draggable/closeable window. - `SplitPanel` вҖ” Resizable split panel. - `DockPanel` вҖ” Dock-based layout. - `Toolbar` вҖ” Toolbar container. - `MenuBar` вҖ” Horizontal menu bar. - `MenuItem` вҖ” Menu item. - `Dialog` вҖ” Modal dialog. - `StatusBar` вҖ” Status bar with sections. - `Accordion` вҖ” Collapsible accordion. - `TooltipPanel` вҖ” Rich tooltip panel. - `ColorPicker` вҖ” Color picker. - `GUITable` вҖ” Data table. - `ImageWidget` вҖ” Image display widget.

items.name: WidgetBase
items.description: Shared base properties embedded by every concrete widget.  `WidgetBase` does not carry rendering or input-handling logic вҖ” it stores the common fields that the Lua API and the layout engine operate on. Concrete widgets (e.g. `Button`, `Label`) embed a `WidgetBase` and add type-specific data.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `String`. Optional identifier for `findById` lookup. - `widget_type` вҖ” `WidgetType`. Discriminator for theme key lookup. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. Horizontal position relative to parent. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. Vertical position relative to parent. - `width` вҖ” `f32`. Widget width in pixels. - `height` вҖ” `f32`. Widget height in pixels. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the widget is drawn and receives events. - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the widget accepts input. - `state` вҖ” `WidgetState`. Current visual/interaction state. - `tooltip` вҖ” `String`. Tooltip text (displayed externally). - `z_order` вҖ” `i32`. Draw layer; higher values draw on top. - `padding` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Inner padding `[top, right, bottom, left]`. - `margin` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Outer margin `[top, right, bottom, left]`. - `min_width` вҖ” `f32`. Minimum width constraint. - `min_height` вҖ” `f32`. Minimum height constraint. - `max_width` вҖ” `f32`. Maximum width constraint (`f32::INFINITY` = none). - `max_height` вҖ” `f32`. Maximum height constraint (`f32::INFINITY` = none). - `anchor_left` вҖ” `Option<f32>`. Left anchor edge offset. - `anchor_top` вҖ” `Option<f32>`. Top anchor edge offset. - `anchor_right` вҖ” `Option<f32>`. Right anchor edge offset. - `anchor_bottom` вҖ” `Option<f32>`. Bottom anchor edge offset. - `anchor_center_x` вҖ” `Option<f32>`. Horizontal centre anchor. - `anchor_center_y` вҖ” `Option<f32>`. Vertical centre anchor. - `flex_grow` вҖ” `f32`. Flexbox grow factor. - `flex_shrink` вҖ” `f32`. Flexbox shrink factor.

image::compressed.module_doc: Compressed GPU texture data from DDS/DXT files.  Provides [`CompressedImageData`] for loading DXT1/DXT3/DXT5/BC7 textures from DDS files without CPU-side decompression.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: CompressedFormat
items.description: GPU-compressed texture format identifier.  # Variants - `Dxt1` вҖ” Dxt1 variant. - `Dxt3` вҖ” Dxt3 variant. - `Dxt5` вҖ” Dxt5 variant. - `Bc7` вҖ” Bc7 variant. - `Etc1` вҖ” Etc1 variant. - `Etc2Rgb` вҖ” Etc2Rgb variant. - `Etc2Rgba` вҖ” Etc2Rgba variant. - `Unknown` вҖ” Unknown or unsupported format.

items.name: CompressedImageData
items.description: CPU-side holder for GPU-compressed texture data loaded from a DDS file.  Does NOT decompress pixels вҖ” the raw compressed bytes are for direct GPU upload. The `mipmaps` vector holds the raw data for the base level (index 0) and any additional mip levels. Upper mip levels beyond level 0 are stored as empty slices in this lightweight holder.  # Fields - `format` вҖ” `CompressedFormat`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `mipmaps` вҖ” `Vec<Vec<u8>>`.

image::image_data.module_doc: CPU-side RGBA8 pixel buffer for image manipulation.  This module is part of Luna2D's `image` subsystem and provides the implementation details for image data-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ImageData`. Primary functions: `new()`, `from_file()`, `from_bytes()`, `width()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ImageData
items.description: CPU-side pixel buffer in RGBA8 format. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Stores pixel data in row-major order, 4 bytes per pixel (R, G, B, A). Can be created empty, from a file, or from raw bytes.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `pixels` вҖ” `Vec<u8>`.

image.module_doc: CPU-side pixel-level image manipulation.  Provides `ImageData` for reading and writing individual pixels in RGBA8 format, and `CompressedImageData` for holding DDS/DXT compressed GPU texture data.  This module is part of Luna2D's `image` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: image_data
items.description: RGBA8 pixel buffer with per-pixel read/write access.

items.name: compressed
items.description: DDS/DXT compressed GPU texture data, loaded without CPU decompression.

items.name: palette_lut
items.description: Color palette lookup table mapping source colors to target colors for shader-based palette swapping.

image::palette_lut.module_doc: Color palette lookup table for shader-based palette swapping.  Maps source colors to target colors, allowing sprites to be re-colored at render time without modifying the original texture.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for palette lut-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `PaletteLUT`. Primary functions: `new()`, `get_color_count()`, `set_color()`, `get_from_color()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: PaletteLUT
items.description: Color palette lookup table mapping source colors to target colors.  # Fields - `from_colors` вҖ” `Vec<Color>`. - `to_colors` вҖ” `Vec<Color>`.  Each entry pairs a "from" color with a "to" color. At render time a shader can replace pixels matching a source color with the corresponding target color.

input::gamepad.module_doc: Gamepad implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for gamepad-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `GamepadState`, `GamepadMappings`. Primary functions: `new()`, `update_button()`, `update_axis()`, `is_button_pressed()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: GamepadState
items.description: Holds the current button and axis state for a single gamepad identified by its id.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” Platform-assigned controller identifier. - `name` вҖ” Human-readable controller name (e.g. "Xbox Controller"). - `connected` вҖ” Whether the gamepad is currently connected. - `buttons` вҖ” *(private)* Map of button index to pressed state. - `axes` вҖ” *(private)* Map of axis index to value in `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: gilrs_button_to_string
items.description: Converts a `gilrs::Button` to a engine-compatible string name.  # Parameters - `button` вҖ” `gilrs::Button`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

items.name: gilrs_axis_to_string
items.description: Converts a `gilrs::Axis` to a engine-compatible string name.  # Parameters - `axis` вҖ” `gilrs::Axis`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

items.name: GamepadMappings
items.description: Stores SDL2 GameControllerDB-format mapping strings keyed by GUID.  Each entry is a single line in the `guid,name,mappings` format used by SDL's game controller database. Call `load_from_file` to populate from disk and `save_to_file` to persist accumulated mappings.  # Fields - `map` вҖ” GUID вҶ’ mapping-string dictionary.

input::keyboard.module_doc: Keyboard implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for keyboard-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `KeyboardState`. Primary functions: `new()`, `begin_frame()`, `press_scancode()`, `release_scancode()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: MOD_SHIFT
items.description: Bitmask constant for the Shift modifier key.

items.name: MOD_CTRL
items.description: Bitmask constant for the Control modifier key.

items.name: MOD_ALT
items.description: Bitmask constant for the Alt/Option modifier key.

items.name: MOD_META
items.description: Bitmask constant for the Super/Meta modifier key.

items.name: KeyboardState
items.description: Tracks which keyboard keys are currently down, just pressed, or just released.  Also tracks physical scancodes, key repeat, and text input state.  # Fields - `keys_down` вҖ” `HashSet<String>`. - `keys_pressed` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `keys_released` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `scancodes_down` вҖ” `HashSet<String>`. - `scancodes_pressed` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `scancodes_released` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `key_repeat_enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `text_input_enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `text_input_buffer` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `modifiers` вҖ” `u8`.

items.name: get_scancode_from_key
items.description: Resolves a logical Luna key name to the closest physical scancode string.  # Parameters - `crate` вҖ” parameter.  # Returns `Option<String>`.

items.name: get_key_from_scancode
items.description: Resolves a physical scancode string to the closest logical Luna key name.  # Parameters - `crate` вҖ” parameter.  # Returns `Option<String>`.

items.name: winit_key_to_string
items.description: Converts a `winit 0.30` logical `Key` to the lowercase string name used by the `luna.*` API.  Returns `Some(name)` for recognised keys, `None` for keys without a mapping (which the engine skips silently).  # Parameters - `key` вҖ” A reference to the `winit::keyboard::Key` from a `WindowEvent::KeyboardInput`.  # Returns `Option<String>` вҖ” Lowercase key name (e.g. `"space"`, `"escape"`, `"a"`) or `None`.

items.name: winit_scancode_to_string
items.description: Converts a `winit 0.30` physical `KeyCode` to a engine-compatible scancode string.  Scancodes represent physical key positions and are layout-independent.  # Parameters - `code` вҖ” A `winit::keyboard::KeyCode` from the physical key field.  # Returns `Option<&'static str>` вҖ” Scancode string or `None` for unmapped keys.

input.module_doc: Mod implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: gamepad
items.description: Gamepad button and axis state for a single controller.

items.name: keyboard
items.description: Keyboard state tracking pressed, held, and released keys.

items.name: mouse
items.description: Mouse position and button state management.

items.name: touch
items.description: Touch input state tracking for touchscreens.

input::mouse.module_doc: Mouse implementation for the `input` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mouse-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SystemCursor`, `MouseState`. Primary functions: `from_name()`, `as_str()`, `new()`, `begin_frame()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SystemCursor
items.description: Standard system cursor shapes. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Arrow` вҖ” Arrow variant. - `IBeam` вҖ” IBeam variant. - `Wait` вҖ” Wait variant. - `Crosshair` вҖ” Crosshair variant. - `Hand` вҖ” Hand variant. - `SizeNWSE` вҖ” SizeNWSE variant. - `SizeNESW` вҖ” SizeNESW variant. - `SizeWE` вҖ” SizeWE variant. - `SizeNS` вҖ” SizeNS variant. - `SizeAll` вҖ” SizeAll variant. - `No` вҖ” No variant.

items.name: MouseState
items.description: Tracks mouse cursor position and per-button pressed/down/released state.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `buttons` вҖ” `[bool; 5]`. - `buttons_pressed` вҖ” `[bool; 5]`. - `buttons_released` вҖ” `[bool; 5]`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. - `grabbed` вҖ” `bool`. - `relative_mode` вҖ” `bool`. - `scroll_x` вҖ” `f64`. - `scroll_y` вҖ” `f64`. - `cursor_type` вҖ” `SystemCursor`.  Supports five buttons (indices 0 = left, 1 = right, 2 = middle, 3 = back/button4, 4 = forward/button5).

items.name: CursorKind
items.description: The cursor type вҖ” either a named system icon or user-supplied pixel data.  # Variants - `System` вҖ” A named OS cursor from the `SystemCursor` variants. - `Custom` вҖ” A custom cursor created from RGBA pixel data.

items.name: CursorHandle
items.description: A held cursor вҖ” either a system cursor icon or custom pixel-data cursor.  # Fields - `kind` вҖ” `CursorKind`. The underlying cursor type.

items.name: is_cursor_supported
items.description: Returns whether cursor customisation is supported on this platform.  # Returns `bool` вҖ” always `true` on desktop.

input::touch.module_doc: Touch input state tracking for Luna2D.  This module is part of Luna2D's `input` subsystem and provides the implementation details for touch-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TouchPoint`, `TouchState`. Primary functions: `new()`, `touch_start()`, `touch_move()`, `touch_end()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TouchPoint
items.description: Information about a single touch point. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `u64`. - `x` вҖ” `f64`. - `y` вҖ” `f64`. - `pressure` вҖ” `f64`.

items.name: TouchState
items.description: Tracks active touch points. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `touches` вҖ” `HashMap<u64`.

root.module_doc: Luna2D engine binary entry point.  This crate is a thin wrapper around the `luna2d` library crate. Its only job is to call [`luna2d::luna_run`], which installs the panic hook, parses the command-line game-directory argument, loads `conf.lua`, and enters the main engine loop.  Two binary crates share the same source via `Cargo.toml` feature flags:   - `luna`   вҖ” console-attached binary (default, used during development)   - `lunec`  вҖ” no-console binary (Windows release build, no terminal window)  To run a game pass the path to the game directory as the first CLI argument:  ```text luna path/to/my_game ```  If no argument is given, the engine looks for `main.lua` in the current working directory and falls back to the splash screen if none is found.

light::attenuation.module_doc: Custom attenuation coefficients for light falloff curves.

items.name: Attenuation
items.description: Custom attenuation coefficients controlling light intensity decay.  # Fields - `constant` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear` вҖ” `f32`. - `quadratic` вҖ” `f32`.  The effective intensity at distance `d` is: `intensity / (constant + linear * d + quadratic * d * d)`. Defaults: constant = 1.0, linear = 0.0, quadratic = 0.0 (no custom attenuation).

light::blend_mode.module_doc: Light blend mode enum for controlling how light color mixes with the scene.

items.name: LightBlendMode
items.description: How light color mixes with the scene.  # Variants - `Add` вҖ” Add variant. - `Sub` вҖ” Sub variant. - `Mix` вҖ” Mix variant.

light::falloff.module_doc: Falloff mode enum for controlling how light intensity decays over distance.

items.name: FalloffMode
items.description: How light intensity decays from center to edge.  # Variants - `Linear` вҖ” Linear variant. - `Smooth` вҖ” Smooth variant. - `Constant` вҖ” Constant variant.

light::flicker.module_doc: Built-in flicker effect configuration for lights.

items.name: FlickerConfig
items.description: Built-in flicker effect that modulates light intensity over time.  # Fields - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `speed` вҖ” `f32`. - `strength` вҖ” `f32`. - `phase` вҖ” `f32`.  When enabled, the engine modulates `intensity` each frame by: `intensity * (1.0 + strength * sin(phase))`. The `phase` field auto-advances by `speed * dt` each tick.

light::light2d.module_doc: 2D point light data container for lighting systems.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for light2d-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Light2D`. Primary functions: `new()`, `set_position()`, `get_position()`, `set_radius()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Light2D
items.description: 2D point light with position, radius, color, intensity, and shadow settings.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `radius` вҖ” `f32`. - `color` вҖ” `Color`. - `intensity` вҖ” `f32`. - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `energy` вҖ” `f32`. - `blend_mode` вҖ” `LightBlendMode`. - `falloff` вҖ” `FalloffMode`. - `shadow_enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `shadow_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `shadow_filter` вҖ” `ShadowFilter`. - `shadow_smooth` вҖ” `f32`. - `light_mask` вҖ” `u16`. - `shadow_mask` вҖ” `u16`. - `light_type` вҖ” `LightType`. - `direction` вҖ” `f32`. - `inner_angle` вҖ” `f32`. - `outer_angle` вҖ” `f32`. - `attenuation` вҖ” `Attenuation`. - `flicker` вҖ” `FlickerConfig`. - `group_id` вҖ” `u16`. - `volumetric` вҖ” `bool`.  Stores all parameters needed to describe a circular light source in 2D space: position, reach, tint, brightness, and on/off state, plus energy scaling, blend mode, falloff curve, shadow settings, light geometry type, custom attenuation, flicker effects, and grouping.

light::light_type.module_doc: Light type enum for point, directional, and spot lights.

items.name: LightType
items.description: The geometric shape of a light source.  # Variants - `Point` вҖ” Point variant. - `Directional` вҖ” Directional variant. - `Spot` вҖ” Spot variant.  `Point` emits equally in all directions from a single position. `Directional` casts parallel rays in a given direction with no positional falloff. `Spot` emits a cone of light defined by inner and outer angles.

light::light_world.module_doc: Resource pool and state for the 2D lighting system.

items.name: LightWorld
items.description: Resource pool and state for the 2D lighting system.  # Fields - `lights` вҖ” `SlotMap<LightKey, Light2D>`. - `occluders` вҖ” `SlotMap<OccluderKey, Occluder>`. - `ambient` вҖ” `Color`. - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `max_lights` вҖ” `u16`.  Owns all light sources and shadow occluders. The renderer reads this each frame to produce the lighting pass. `enabled` auto-activates when the first light is added.

light.module_doc: 2D point-light data model вҖ” Tier 1 core engine module.  Provides `Light2D`, a pure data container describing a circular point light source in 2D space (position, radius, color, intensity, enabled flag). No GPU resources are stored here; the renderer receives light data via `DrawCommand` variants and performs all GPU work after the Lua callback returns.  Also provides `LightBlendMode`, `FalloffMode`, `ShadowFilter` enums, `LightType` (Point/Directional/Spot), `Attenuation` coefficients, `FlickerConfig` built-in flicker effects, `Occluder` polygon shadow casters, and `LightWorld` resource pool.  ## Tier  `light` is a **Tier 1 вҖ” Core Engine** module. It may import only `math` and `engine`. It must not import other Tier 1 modules.

items.name: attenuation
items.description: Custom attenuation coefficients for distance falloff.

items.name: blend_mode
items.description: How light color mixes with the scene.

items.name: falloff
items.description: How light intensity decays from center to edge.

items.name: flicker
items.description: Built-in flicker effect configuration.

items.name: light2d
items.description: 2D point-light data container: position, radius, color, and intensity.

items.name: light_type
items.description: Geometric light type: point, directional, or spot.

items.name: light_world
items.description: Resource pool and state for the 2D lighting system.

items.name: occluder
items.description: Polygon shadow caster that blocks light.

items.name: shadow
items.description: Edge quality for shadow boundaries.

light::occluder.module_doc: Polygon shadow caster for the 2D lighting system.

items.name: Occluder
items.description: Polygon shadow caster that blocks light.  # Fields - `vertices` вҖ” `Vec<Vec2>`. - `position` вҖ” `Vec2`. - `opacity` вҖ” `f32`. - `light_mask` вҖ” `u16`. - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`.  An occluder defines a convex or concave polygon that casts shadows when placed between a light source and the scene. The `light_mask` field controls which lights this occluder interacts with.

light::shadow.module_doc: Shadow filter enum for controlling edge quality of shadow boundaries.

items.name: ShadowFilter
items.description: Edge quality for shadow boundaries.  # Variants - `None` вҖ” None variant. - `Pcf5` вҖ” Pcf5 variant. - `Pcf13` вҖ” Pcf13 variant.

math::bezier.module_doc: Bezier curve evaluation using De Casteljau's algorithm.  Supports arbitrary-degree curves with control point manipulation, rendering to polylines, derivative computation, and geometric transforms.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem and provides the implementation details for bezier-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `BezierCurve`. Primary functions: `new()`, `evaluate()`, `render()`, `render_segment()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: BezierCurve
items.description: A Bezier curve defined by control points.  Uses De Casteljau's algorithm for evaluation. Minimum 2 control points required.  # Fields - `control_points` вҖ” `Vec<Vec2>`.

math::color.module_doc: RGBA color value type for the Luna2D math layer.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem (Baseline layer). `Color` is a pure value type with no engine dependencies. Key types exported from this module: `Color`. Primary functions: `from_u8()`, `to_u8()`, `to_rgb_u32()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Color
items.description: RGBA color stored as `f32` components in the range `[0.0, 1.0]`.
