Used everywhere the API accepts a color: `luna.graphics.setColor`, sprite tints, background color, etc.  # Fields - `r` вҖ” Red channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `g` вҖ” Green channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `b` вҖ” Blue channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`. - `a` вҖ” Alpha channel, `[0.0, 1.0]`; `1.0` = fully opaque.

items.name: gamma_to_linear
items.description: Convert a single sRGB gamma-space color component to linear space.  Input and output in `[0.0, 1.0]`. Uses the standard IEC 61966-2-1 sRGB transfer function.  # Parameters - `c` вҖ” gamma-encoded sRGB channel value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Linear-light value in `[0.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: linear_to_gamma
items.description: Convert a single linear-space color component to sRGB gamma space.  Input and output in `[0.0, 1.0]`. Uses the standard IEC 61966-2-1 sRGB inverse transfer function.  # Parameters - `c` вҖ” linear-light channel value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Gamma-encoded sRGB value in `[0.0, 1.0]`.

math::easing.module_doc: Standard easing functions for smooth animation and interpolation.  Each function takes a normalised progress value `t` in `[0.0, 1.0]` and returns the eased value, also in `[0.0, 1.0]` for most curves.

items.name: linear
items.description: Linear interpolation вҖ” no easing. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_quad
items.description: Quadratic ease-in вҖ” starts slow, accelerates.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_quad
items.description: Quadratic ease-out вҖ” starts fast, decelerates.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_quad
items.description: Quadratic ease-in-out вҖ” slow start and end, fast middle.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_cubic
items.description: Cubic ease-in вҖ” starts slow, accelerates sharply.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_cubic
items.description: Cubic ease-out вҖ” starts fast, decelerates sharply.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_cubic
items.description: Cubic ease-in-out вҖ” smooth S-curve. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_quart
items.description: Quartic ease-in вҖ” very slow start. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_quart
items.description: Quartic ease-out вҖ” very slow end. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_quart
items.description: Quartic ease-in-out вҖ” pronounced S-curve.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_sine
items.description: Sinusoidal ease-in вҖ” gentle sine-based acceleration.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_sine
items.description: Sinusoidal ease-out вҖ” gentle sine-based deceleration.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_sine
items.description: Sinusoidal ease-in-out вҖ” gentle S-curve.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_expo
items.description: Exponential ease-in вҖ” very slow start, rapid acceleration.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_expo
items.description: Exponential ease-out вҖ” rapid start, very slow end.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_out_expo
items.description: Exponential ease-in-out вҖ” sharp S-curve with exponential tails.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_elastic
items.description: Elastic ease-in вҖ” spring-like overshoot at the start.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_elastic
items.description: Elastic ease-out вҖ” spring-like overshoot at the end.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_bounce
items.description: Bounce ease-out вҖ” simulates a bouncing ball landing.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_bounce
items.description: Bounce ease-in вҖ” simulates a bouncing ball launching.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_in_back
items.description: Back ease-in вҖ” pulls back before accelerating past the start.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: ease_out_back
items.description: Back ease-out вҖ” overshoots the target then settles back.  # Parameters - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns Eased output; typically in `[0.0, 1.0]` (may overshoot for elastic, back, and bounce curves).

items.name: apply
items.description: Looks up an easing function by name and applies it to progress value `t`.  Supported names (case-insensitive): `"linear"`, `"inQuad"`, `"outQuad"`, `"inOutQuad"`, `"inCubic"`, `"outCubic"`, `"inOutCubic"`, `"inQuart"`, `"outQuart"`, `"inOutQuart"`, `"inSine"`, `"outSine"`, `"inOutSine"`, `"inExpo"`, `"outExpo"`, `"inOutExpo"`, `"inElastic"`, `"outElastic"`, `"outBounce"`, `"inBounce"`, `"inBack"`, `"outBack"`.  # Parameters - `name` вҖ” easing name string (case-insensitive) - `t` вҖ” normalised progress value in `[0.0, 1.0]`  # Returns `Some(f32)` with the eased value, or `None` if the name is unrecognised.

math::geometry.module_doc: 2D geometry utility functions.  Free functions for common geometric computations: intersections, containment, polygon operations, and rasterization.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem and provides the implementation details for geometry-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `angle_between()`, `circle_contains_point()`, `circle_intersects_circle()`, `circle_intersects_line()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: angle_between
items.description: Returns the angle in radians from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2).  # Parameters - `x1` вҖ” `f32`. - `y1` вҖ” `f32`. - `x2` вҖ” `f32`. - `y2` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: circle_contains_point
items.description: Returns true if the point (px, py) is inside the circle centered at (cx, cy) with radius r.  # Parameters - `cx` вҖ” `f32`. - `cy` вҖ” `f32`. - `r` вҖ” `f32`. - `px` вҖ” `f32`. - `py` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: circle_intersects_circle
items.description: Returns true if two circles overlap. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `x1` вҖ” `f32`. - `y1` вҖ” `f32`. - `r1` вҖ” `f32`. - `x2` вҖ” `f32`. - `y2` вҖ” `f32`. - `r2` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: circle_intersects_line
items.description: Line-circle intersection. Returns (intersects, hit1, hit2).  # Parameters - `cx` вҖ” `f32`. - `cy` вҖ” `f32`. - `r` вҖ” `f32`. - `lx1` вҖ” `f32`. - `ly1` вҖ” `f32`. - `lx2` вҖ” `f32`. - `ly2` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(bool, Option<(f32, f32)>, Option<(f32, f32)>)`.  Points are along the infinite line through (lx1,ly1)-(lx2,ly2).

items.name: circle_intersects_segment
items.description: Segment-circle intersection. Same as line-circle but clamped to the segment.  # Parameters - `cx` вҖ” `f32`. - `cy` вҖ” `f32`. - `r` вҖ” `f32`. - `sx1` вҖ” `f32`. - `sy1` вҖ” `f32`. - `sx2` вҖ” `f32`. - `sy2` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(bool, Option<(f32, f32)>, Option<(f32, f32)>)`.

items.name: polygon_area
items.description: Computes the signed area of a polygon using the Shoelace formula.  # Parameters - `vertices` вҖ” `&[f32]`.  # Returns `f32`.  `vertices` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: polygon_centroid
items.description: Computes the centroid of a polygon. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `vertices` вҖ” `&[f32]`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.  `vertices` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: segment_intersects_segment
items.description: Tests if two line segments intersect. Returns (intersects, intersection_point).  # Parameters - `x1` вҖ” `f32`. - `y1` вҖ” `f32`. - `x2` вҖ” `f32`. - `y2` вҖ” `f32`. - `x3` вҖ” `f32`. - `y3` вҖ” `f32`. - `x4` вҖ” `f32`. - `y4` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(bool, Option<(f32, f32)>)`.

items.name: closest_point_on_segment
items.description: Returns the closest point on a line segment to a given point.  # Parameters - `px` вҖ” `f32`. - `py` вҖ” `f32`. - `x1` вҖ” `f32`. - `y1` вҖ” `f32`. - `x2` вҖ” `f32`. - `y2` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.

items.name: point_in_polygon
items.description: Tests if a point is inside a polygon using the ray casting algorithm.  # Parameters - `vertices` вҖ” `&[f32]`. - `px` вҖ” `f32`. - `py` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `bool`.  `vertices` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

items.name: line_intersect
items.description: Infinite line intersection. Returns the intersection point if lines are not parallel.  # Parameters - `x1` вҖ” `f32`. - `y1` вҖ” `f32`. - `x2` вҖ” `f32`. - `y2` вҖ” `f32`. - `x3` вҖ” `f32`. - `y3` вҖ” `f32`. - `x4` вҖ” `f32`. - `y4` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `Option<(f32, f32)>`.

items.name: bresenham
items.description: Bresenham line rasterization from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2).  # Parameters - `x1` вҖ” `i32`. - `y1` вҖ” `i32`. - `x2` вҖ” `i32`. - `y2` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.  Returns all integer grid cells the line passes through.

items.name: convex_hull
items.description: Computes the convex hull of a set of 2D points using Andrew's monotone chain algorithm.  # Parameters - `points` вҖ” `&[f32]`.  # Returns `Vec<f32>`.  `points` is a flat array `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`. Returns flat `[x0, y0, ...]`.

items.name: delaunay_triangulate
items.description: Delaunay triangulation using the Bowyer-Watson algorithm.  # Parameters - `points` вҖ” `&[(f64, f64)]`.  # Returns `Vec<[f64; 6]>`.  Returns a list of triangles, each as `[x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3]`.

math::mat3.module_doc: Mat3 implementation for the `math` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mat3-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Mat3`. Primary functions: `identity()`, `from_row_major()`, `from_translation()`, `from_rotation()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Mat3
items.description: A 3Г—3 column-major matrix used for 2D affine transforms (translation, rotation, scale).  Used by `Camera::view_matrix` to combine position, rotation, and zoom into a single transform that maps world coordinates to screen coordinates.  # Fields - `m` вҖ” 3Г—3 array of `f32` in row-major layout (`m[row][col]`).

math.module_doc: Foundational math and value types for the Luna2D Baseline layer.  This module is part of Luna2D's Baseline (`math` subsystem) вҖ” the leaf of the dependency graph with no Luna2D dependencies of its own.  [`Color`] (`sRGB [f32; 4]`) lives here as a pure value type. It was moved from `src/graphics/srgb.rs` during the graphics-module-split session and is now the canonical color type for the entire engine.  All public items are documented. See `docs/architecture.md` for tier context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: bezier
items.description: Bezier curve evaluation using De Casteljau's algorithm.

items.name: color
items.description: RGBA color value type: named constants, `f32` and `u8` construction, packed `u32` output.

items.name: easing
items.description: Standard easing functions for smooth animation and interpolation.

items.name: geometry
items.description: 2D geometry utility functions: intersections, containment, polygon ops, rasterization.

items.name: mat3
items.description: 3x3 column-major matrix for 2D transforms (translate, rotate, scale).

items.name: noise_functions
items.description: Standalone noise functions: `perlin2d`, `perlin3d`, `perlin4d`, `simplex2d`, `simplex_noise_2d`, `simplex_noise_3d`, `fbm`.

items.name: noise_generator
items.description: Configurable noise generator: `NoiseGenerator`, `NoiseKind`, `FractalType`, `DistType`, `MapGenOptions`.

items.name: polygon
items.description: Polygon utilities: ear-clipping triangulation and convexity testing.

items.name: random
items.description: Seedable random number generator for reproducible sequences.

items.name: rect
items.description: Axis-aligned rectangle with intersection and containment queries.

items.name: spatial_hash
items.description: Spatial hash for efficient broad-phase AABB collision queries.

items.name: transform
items.description: 2D affine transform with chainable methods wrapping Mat3.

items.name: tween
items.description: Value interpolator with easing curves.

items.name: vec2
items.description: 2D floating-point vector with arithmetic operators and common helpers.

math::noise_functions.module_doc: Standalone noise functions: Perlin, Simplex, and fractal FBM.  All return values in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`. Private helpers (`fade`, `lerp`, `hash*`, `grad*`, `simplex_contribution`) live below the public functions.

items.name: perlin2d
items.description: Generates 2D Perlin noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space - `seed` вҖ” numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: simplex2d
items.description: Generates 2D Simplex noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space - `seed` вҖ” numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: simplex_noise_2d
items.description: Returns 2D simplex noise for the given coordinates using seed 0.  Convenience wrapper around [`simplex2d`] with a fixed seed of `0`. Use [`NoiseGenerator::simplexNoise`] for seeded output.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space. - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space.  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: simplex_noise_3d
items.description: Returns 3D simplex noise for the given coordinates using seed 0.  Delegates to [`NoiseGenerator::simplex_3d`] with seed `0`.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space. - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space. - `z` вҖ” Z coordinate in noise space.  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: fbm
items.description: Generates fractal Brownian motion noise by layering multiple octaves of Perlin noise.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space - `seed` вҖ” numeric seed - `octaves` вҖ” number of noise layers (1вҖ“8 typical) - `lacunarity` вҖ” frequency multiplier per octave (typical: 2.0) - `gain` вҖ” amplitude multiplier per octave (typical: 0.5)  # Returns A value centred around `0.0` in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: perlin3d
items.description: Generates 3D Perlin noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space - `z` вҖ” Z coordinate in noise space - `seed` вҖ” numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

items.name: perlin4d
items.description: Generates 4D Perlin noise at the given coordinates.  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” X coordinate in noise space - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate in noise space - `z` вҖ” Z coordinate in noise space - `w_coord` вҖ” W coordinate in noise space - `seed` вҖ” numeric seed to vary the noise pattern  # Returns A value in approximately `[-1.0, 1.0]`.

math::noise_generator.module_doc: Seeded procedural noise generator (`NoiseGenerator`).  Provides Perlin, Simplex, Worley noise in 1DвҖ“4D, plus fractal combinators (FBM, ridged, turbulence), domain warping, and 2D map generation (`MapGenOptions`).  Enums: `DistType`, `NoiseKind`, `FractalType`. Primary struct: `NoiseGenerator`.

items.name: DistType
items.description: Distance metric for Worley noise. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Euclidean` вҖ” Euclidean variant. - `Manhattan` вҖ” Manhattan variant. - `Chebyshev` вҖ” Chebyshev variant.

items.name: NoiseKind
items.description: Noise algorithm kind used by fractal combinators.  # Variants - `Perlin` вҖ” Perlin variant. - `Simplex` вҖ” Simplex variant.

items.name: FractalType
items.description: Fractal type for multi-octave noise. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Fbm` вҖ” Fbm variant. - `Ridged` вҖ” Ridged variant. - `Turbulence` вҖ” Turbulence variant.

items.name: MapGenOptions
items.description: Options for 2D noise map generation. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `scale_x` вҖ” `f64`. - `scale_y` вҖ” `f64`. - `octaves` вҖ” `u32`. - `lacunarity` вҖ” `f64`. - `persistence` вҖ” `f64`. - `kind` вҖ” `NoiseKind`. - `fractal` вҖ” `FractalType`. - `offset_x` вҖ” `f64`. - `offset_y` вҖ” `f64`.

items.name: NoiseGenerator
items.description: Seeded procedural noise generator. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Holds a 512-entry permutation table derived deterministically from the seed.  All noise methods are pure functions of the seed and coordinates вҖ” the same inputs always produce the same output.  # Fields - `seed` вҖ” `u64`. - `perm` вҖ” `[u8; 512]`.

math::polygon.module_doc: Polygon utilities: ear-clipping triangulation and convexity testing.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem and provides the implementation details for polygon-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `triangulate()`, `is_convex()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: triangulate
items.description: Triangulate a simple polygon using the ear-clipping algorithm.  # Parameters - `polygon` вҖ” slice of `Vec2` vertices forming a simple (non-self-intersecting) polygon;   must have at least 3 vertices  # Returns `Ok(triangles)` вҖ” a `Vec` of `[Vec2; 3]` triangle triples covering the polygon area. `Err(String)` вҖ” if triangulation fails (e.g. self-intersecting or degenerate input).

items.name: is_convex
items.description: Check if a polygon is convex. This accessor incurs no allocation; call it freely in hot paths.  Uses cross-product sign consistency at each vertex to determine convexity.  # Parameters - `polygon` вҖ” slice of `Vec2` vertices  # Returns `true` if the polygon is convex; `false` if concave, self-intersecting, or fewer than 3 vertices.

math::random.module_doc: Seedable random number generator for reproducible sequences.  Wraps `fastrand::Rng` with engine-compatible API including seeding, state save/restore, and normal distribution sampling.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem and provides the implementation details for random-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `RandomGenerator`. Primary functions: `new()`, `with_seed()`, `random()`, `random_int()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: RandomGenerator
items.description: Seedable random number generator exposed as a Lua object.  Wraps `fastrand::Rng` with engine-compatible API for deterministic random number generation, normal distribution, and state management.  # Fields - `rng` вҖ” `Rng`. - `seed` вҖ” `u64`.

math::rect.module_doc: Rect implementation for the `math` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `math` subsystem and provides the implementation details for rect-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Rect`. Primary functions: `new()`, `center()`, `area()`, `contains()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Rect
items.description: An axis-aligned rectangle defined by its top-left corner and dimensions.  Used for AABB collision detection, UI layout, and camera viewport clipping.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” X coordinate of the top-left corner. - `y` вҖ” Y coordinate of the top-left corner. - `width` вҖ” Width in pixels or world units. - `height` вҖ” Height in pixels or world units.

math::spatial_hash.module_doc: Spatial hash for efficient broad-phase AABB collision queries.  Items are keyed by a string ID and stored with axis-aligned bounding boxes. The grid partitions space into fixed-size cells for O(1) bucket lookup.

items.name: SpatialItem
items.description: Entry in the spatial hash. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `String`. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `w` вҖ” `f32`. - `h` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: SpatialHash
items.description: Spatial hash for AABB queries. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Divides the 2D plane into a uniform grid of square cells. Each item is inserted into every cell its bounding box overlaps, enabling fast broad-phase collision queries.  # Fields - `cell_size` вҖ” `f32`. - `items` вҖ” `HashMap<String`. - `buckets` вҖ” `HashMap<(i32`.

math::transform.module_doc: 2D affine transform wrapping Mat3 with chainable methods.  Compatible with standard 2D transform API for translate, rotate, scale, shear, and point transformation. Animates one or more values from start to target over a given duration, applying an easing curve to control the interpolation speed.  # Fields - `duration` вҖ” `f64`. - `easing_fn` вҖ” `fn(f32) -> f32`. - `easing_name` вҖ” `String`. - `clock` вҖ” `f64`. - `values` вҖ” `Vec<TweenValue>`.

network::constants.module_doc: Compile-time limits and defaults for the networking subsystem.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: MAX_PEERS
items.description: Maximum number of simultaneous peer connections a single host supports.  Luna2D targets small-scale multiplayer (LAN party, co-op). The engine hard-caps at 8 peers; the recommended default for new hosts is 4.

items.name: DEFAULT_PEERS
items.description: Default number of peers when no explicit value is provided.

items.name: MAX_CHANNELS
items.description: Maximum number of independent ENet channels per connection.  ENet supports up to 255 channels. Luna2D defaults to a more modest ceiling to keep memory usage predictable on integrated GPUs.

items.name: DEFAULT_CHANNELS
items.description: Default channel count for new connections when none is specified.

network::error.module_doc: Network-specific error types.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: NetworkError
items.description: Errors produced by the networking subsystem.  # Variants - `PeerLimitExceeded` вҖ” The requested peer count exceeds `MAX_PEERS`. - `Io` вҖ” A socket-level I/O error occurred. - `Enet` вҖ” An ENet-internal error surfaced from `rusty_enet`. - `HostDestroyed` вҖ” The host has already been destroyed; further calls are invalid. - `InvalidPeer` вҖ” The addressed peer index is out of range. - `InvalidAddress` вҖ” Failed to parse a bind address string.

network::host.module_doc: ENet host wrapper for the Luna2D networking subsystem.  [`NetworkHost`] owns a `rusty_enet::Host<UdpSocket>` and provides a safe Rust API that the Lua binding layers (`network_api`, `net_api`) consume.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: NetworkHost
items.description: Wraps a `rusty_enet::Host<UdpSocket>` with Luna2D-specific defaults and limit enforcement.  Created once per logical network endpoint (server or client). The caller must pump [`service`](Self::service) every frame to process I/O.

items.name: NetworkEvent
items.description: Result of a single [`NetworkHost::service`] call.  # Variants - `Connect` вҖ” A remote peer completed the connection handshake. - `Disconnect` вҖ” A remote peer disconnected (gracefully or timed out). - `Receive` вҖ” A data packet arrived from a remote peer.

items.name: PeerStats
items.description: Statistics snapshot for a single peer.  # Fields - `round_trip_time` вҖ” `u32`. - `round_trip_time_variance` вҖ” `u32`. - `packets_sent` вҖ” `u32`. - `packets_lost` вҖ” `u32`. - `packet_loss` вҖ” `u32`. - `incoming_bandwidth` вҖ” `u32`. - `outgoing_bandwidth` вҖ” `u32`. - `incoming_data_total` вҖ” `u32`. - `outgoing_data_total` вҖ” `u32`.

network.module_doc: UDP networking via ENet вҖ” reliable packet transport for multiplayer games.  This module wraps the [`rusty_enet`] crate to provide both a high-level `luna.network` API (options tables, event tables, camelCase) and a raw `luna.net` / `enet` API (direct ENet signatures, underscore naming, multi-value returns).  An ENet host acts as both server and client endpoint simultaneously: binding to a port enables incoming connections, while connecting to a remote address creates outgoing ones. The single `service(timeout)` event pump drives all I/O.  # Architecture  - **Tier 1** module вҖ” depends only on Baseline (`math`, `engine`). - [`host`] вҖ” `NetworkHost` wrapper around `rusty_enet::Host<UdpSocket>`. - [`error`] вҖ” `NetworkError` enum for Lua-friendly error messages. - [`constants`] вҖ” Compile-time limits (`MAX_PEERS`, `MAX_CHANNELS`).

items.name: constants
items.description: Compile-time limits and defaults for the networking subsystem.

items.name: error
items.description: Network-specific error types.

items.name: host
items.description: ENet host wrapper.

particle::config.module_doc: Particle emitter configuration types and enums.

items.name: AreaDistribution
items.description: Area distribution mode for particle emission.  # Variants - `None` вҖ” All particles spawn at the emitter center (no area spread). - `Uniform` вҖ” Uniform random distribution inside a rectangle. - `Normal` вҖ” Gaussian-approximated distribution inside a rectangle. - `Ellipse` вҖ” Uniform random distribution inside an ellipse. - `BorderEllipse` вҖ” Random distribution on the border of an ellipse. - `BorderRectangle` вҖ” Random distribution on the border of a rectangle.

items.name: InsertMode
items.description: Insert mode controlling where new particles are placed in the particle list.  # Variants - `Top` вҖ” New particles added to the end of the list (drawn on top, default). - `Bottom` вҖ” New particles added to the front of the list (drawn behind existing particles). - `Random` вҖ” New particles inserted at a random position in the list.

items.name: EmitterState
items.description: Emitter lifecycle state controlling whether the system emits and updates particles.  # Variants - `Active` вҖ” Emitting and updating particles each frame. - `Paused` вҖ” Particles freeze in place; no new emissions and no physics updates. - `Stopped` вҖ” No new emissions; existing particles continue ageing until dead.

items.name: EmissionShape
items.description: Emission shape controlling where new particles spawn relative to the emitter.  # Variants - `Point` вҖ” All particles spawn at the emitter center. - `Circle` вҖ” Particles spawn within or on the edge of a circle. - `Rectangle` вҖ” Particles spawn within a rectangle centered on the emitter. - `Ring` вҖ” Particles spawn within an annulus (ring between two radii). - `Line` вҖ” Particles spawn along a line segment. - `Cone` вҖ” Particles spawn within a cone sector. - `Star` вҖ” Particles spawn on the points or edges of a star polygon. - `Spiral` вҖ” Particles spawn along an Archimedean spiral.

items.name: RelativeMode
items.description: Relative mode controlling whether particles move with the emitter.  # Variants - `Detached` вҖ” Particles remain in world space after emission (default). - `Attached` вҖ” Particles move with the emitter position each frame.

items.name: ParticleConfig
items.description: Configuration for a particle emitter. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `max_particles` вҖ” `u32`. - `emission_rate` вҖ” `f32`. - `lifetime_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `lifetime_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `speed_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `speed_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `direction` вҖ” `f32`. - `spread` вҖ” `f32`. - `gravity_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `gravity_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `sizes` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `colors` вҖ” `Vec<[f32; 4]>`. - `spin_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `spin_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `rotation_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `rotation_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `spin_variation` вҖ” `f32`. - `size_variation` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_accel_x_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_accel_x_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_accel_y_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_accel_y_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `radial_accel_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `radial_accel_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `tangential_accel_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `tangential_accel_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_damping_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_damping_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `area_distribution` вҖ” `AreaDistribution`. - `area_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `area_height` вҖ” `f32`. - `area_angle` вҖ” `f32`. - `area_direction_relative` вҖ” `bool`. - `emitter_lifetime` вҖ” `f32`. - `insert_mode` вҖ” `InsertMode`. - `offset_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `relative_rotation` вҖ” `bool`. - `texture_id` вҖ” `Option<TextureKey>`. - `quads` вҖ” `Vec<[f32; 4]>`. - `alpha_keyframes` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `emission_shape` вҖ” `EmissionShape`. - `relative_mode` вҖ” `RelativeMode`. - `turbulence` вҖ” `f32`. - `drag` вҖ” `f32`. - `orbit_speed` вҖ” `f32`. - `animated_frames` вҖ” `u32`. - `frame_rate` вҖ” `f32`. - `color_by_speed` вҖ” `bool`. - `speed_color_min` вҖ” `f32`. - `speed_color_max` вҖ” `f32`. - `shape` вҖ” `ParticleShape`.  All ranges are sampled uniformly per-particle at spawn time.

particle::emission.module_doc: Particle spawn offset calculations for area distribution and emission shapes.

items.name: emission_offset
items.description: Compute an emission offset `(dx, dy)` based on the config's area distribution.  # Parameters - `config` вҖ” `&ParticleConfig`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.

items.name: emission_shape_offset
items.description: Compute an emission offset `(dx, dy)` based on the emission shape.  # Parameters - `shape` вҖ” `&EmissionShape`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.

particle::emitter.module_doc: Particle emitter struct and update/draw logic.

items.name: ParticleSystem
items.description: An emitter-based particle system. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `config` вҖ” `ParticleConfig`. - `particles` вҖ” `Vec<Particle>`. - `emitter_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `emitter_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `emit_accumulator` вҖ” `f32`. - `state` вҖ” `EmitterState`. - `emitter_age` вҖ” `f32`. - `prev_emitter_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `prev_emitter_y` вҖ” `f32`.  Call `update(dt)` each frame to advance physics and spawn new particles, then `draw_commands(ox, oy)` to obtain the `DrawCommand` list for rendering.

particle::math.module_doc: Math helper functions for particle interpolation and random sampling.

items.name: lerp
items.description: Linearly interpolate between `a` and `b` by factor `t`.  # Parameters - `a` вҖ” `f32`. - `b` вҖ” `f32`. - `t` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: interpolate_sizes
items.description: Interpolate a multi-stop size array at normalised time `t` (0 = birth, 1 = death).  # Parameters - `sizes` вҖ” `&[f32]`. - `t` вҖ” `f32`. - `variation` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.  The `variation` factor (0..1) scales down the interpolated size. With `variation = 0`, the full interpolated size is returned.  # Edge cases - Empty `sizes`: returns `1.0`. - Single value: returns `sizes[0] * (1 - variation)`. - `t` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

items.name: interpolate_colors
items.description: Interpolate a multi-stop color array at normalised time `t` (0 = birth, 1 = death).  # Parameters - `colors` вҖ” `&[[f32; 4]]`. - `t` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `[f32; 4]`.  # Edge cases - Empty `colors`: returns white `[1, 1, 1, 1]`. - Single value: returns that color. - `t` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

items.name: interpolate_alphas
items.description: Interpolate a multi-stop alpha array at normalised time `t` (0 = birth, 1 = death).  # Parameters - `alphas` вҖ” `&[f32]`. - `t` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.  # Edge cases - Empty `alphas`: returns `1.0`. - Single value: returns `alphas[0]`. - `t` is clamped to `[0, 1]`.

items.name: rand_range
items.description: Sample a uniform random value in `[min, max]`.  # Parameters - `min` вҖ” `f32`. - `max` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: rand_normal
items.description: Approximate a standard-normal random value using Box-Muller transform.  # Returns `f32`.

particle.module_doc: Particle system module providing emitter-based 2D particle effects.  **Tier**: Tier 2 вҖ” Engine Extensions.  A `ParticleSystem` spawns short-lived `Particle` entities each frame, advancing their position, velocity, and lifetime. Dead particles are recycled, keeping allocation bounded by `ParticleConfig::max_particles`.  Supports multi-stop size/color/alpha interpolation, emission shapes, radial/tangential acceleration, linear damping, relative mode, texture-based rendering, and five built-in geometric particle shapes (Square, Circle, Triangle, Spark, Diamond).  ## Sub-files  | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `config.rs`   | Enums (`AreaDistribution`, `InsertMode`, `EmitterState`, `EmissionShape`, `RelativeMode`) and `ParticleConfig` (~50 fields) | | `shapes.rs`   | `ParticleShape` enum вҖ” five geometric render primitives | | `particle.rs` | `Particle` per-particle live state (pos, vel, life, rotation, вҖҰ) | | `emitter.rs`  | `ParticleSystem` simulation loop, physics integration, and `draw_commands()` | | `math.rs`     | Math helpers: `lerp`, `interpolate_sizes`, `interpolate_colors`, `interpolate_alphas` | | `emission.rs` | Spawn-offset calculators for area distribution and emission shapes |

items.description: Emitter configuration enums and `ParticleConfig` struct.

items.description: Particle spawn-offset helpers for area distribution and emission shapes.

items.description: `ParticleSystem` simulation and draw-command generation.

items.description: Math helpers for particle interpolation and random sampling.

items.description: Time-fading ribbon effect (trail renderer).

items.name: Particle
items.description: A single particle managed by a `ParticleSystem`.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `vx` вҖ” `f32`. - `vy` вҖ” `f32`. - `life` вҖ” `f32`. - `max_life` вҖ” `f32`. - `rotation` вҖ” `f32`. - `spin` вҖ” `f32`. - `radial_accel` вҖ” `f32`. - `tangential_accel` вҖ” `f32`. - `linear_damping` вҖ” `f32`. - `size_variation` вҖ” `f32`. - `origin_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `origin_y` вҖ” `f32`.

particle::shapes.module_doc: Particle shape enum defining the geometric primitive used to render untextured particles.

items.name: ParticleShape
items.description: Geometric shape used when drawing untextured particles.  Defaults to `ParticleShape::Square` for backward compatibility with the original rectangle-only renderer. The shape is passed through to the GPU renderer as `ParticleRenderShape`; see `DrawParticleSystem` in `src/graphics/renderer.rs` for the matching render-side enum.  # Variants - `Square` вҖ” Square variant. - `Circle` вҖ” Circle variant. - `Triangle` вҖ” Triangle variant. - `Spark` вҖ” Spark variant. - `Diamond` вҖ” Diamond variant.

particle::trail.module_doc: Trail renderer for fading ribbon effects.  Stores a series of timestamped points that age out over a configurable lifetime, producing a tapered ribbon from head to tail.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for trail-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TrailPoint`, `Trail`. Primary functions: `new()`, `push_point()`, `update()`, `set_width()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TrailPoint
items.description: A point in a trail with age tracking.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `age` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: Trail
items.description: Fading textured ribbon renderer. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `points` вҖ” `Vec<TrailPoint>`. - `lifetime` вҖ” `f32`. - `start_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `end_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `head_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `tail_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `min_distance` вҖ” `f32`.  Points are pushed at the head and automatically removed once their age exceeds the configured lifetime. Width tapers linearly from `start_width` at the head to `end_width` at the tail.

pathfinding::ai_flow_field.module_doc: Dijkstra-based flow field for crowd pathfinding. Moved from `ai/flowfield`; used by the Lua `luna.ai.newFlowField` API.  Note: this type operates on [`SimpleGrid`].  The higher-level [`crate::pathfinding::FlowField`] operates on [`NavGrid`] instead.

items.name: FlowField
items.description: BFS flow field that stores normalized direction vectors toward a goal.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `usize`. - `height` вҖ” `usize`. - `goal` вҖ” `Option<(usize, usize)>`.  Built from a SimpleGrid. Each cell stores a direction vector and BFS distance.

pathfinding::astar.module_doc: Aвҳ… search with octile/Manhattan heuristic and Thetaвҳ… path smoothing.  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for astar-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `astar()`, `line_of_sight()`, `smooth_path()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: astar
items.description: Run Aвҳ… search on `grid` from `start` to `goal`.  # Parameters - `grid` вҖ” `&NavGrid`. - `start` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`. - `goal` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`. - `unit_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `max_nodes` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `(Option<Vec<(u32, u32)>>, bool)`.  - `unit_size`: NxN footprint (1 for single-cell units). - `max_nodes`: upper bound on nodes expanded; 0 = unlimited.  Returns `(path, complete)` where `path` is `Some(vec)` with the path from start to goal (or nearest reachable node) and `complete` indicates whether the goal was actually reached.

items.name: line_of_sight
items.description: Check line-of-sight between two cells using Bresenham's algorithm,  # Parameters - `grid` вҖ” `&NavGrid`. - `x1` вҖ” `u32`. - `y1` вҖ” `u32`. - `x2` вҖ” `u32`. - `y2` вҖ” `u32`. - `unit_size` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `bool`. respecting `unit_size`Г—`unit_size` footprint.

items.name: smooth_path
items.description: Smooth a path by removing unnecessary waypoints via line-of-sight checks  # Parameters - `grid` вҖ” `&NavGrid`. - `path` вҖ” `&[(u32, u32)]`. - `unit_size` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Vec<(u32, u32)>`. (Thetaвҳ…-style post-processing).

pathfinding::async_pool.module_doc: Thread pool for asynchronous path computation.  Pathfinding requests are submitted with an ID and processed on worker threads. Results are collected via non-blocking [`PathThreadPool::poll`].  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for async pool-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `PathThreadPool`. Primary functions: `new()`, `submit()`, `poll()`, `cancel()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: PathResult
items.description: A completed path result returned by [`PathThreadPool::poll`].

items.name: PathThreadPool
items.description: A pool of worker threads that process pathfinding requests asynchronously.  # Fields - `tx` вҖ” `Sender<PathRequest>`. - `rx` вҖ” `Receiver<PathResult>`. - `cancelled` вҖ” `Arc<Mutex<Vec<u64>>>`. - `thread_count` вҖ” `usize`. - `_handles` вҖ” `Vec<thread::JoinHandle<()>>`. - `pending` вҖ” `Arc<Mutex<u32>>`.

pathfinding::flow_field.module_doc: Flow field pathfinding for steering many units toward one or more targets.  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for flow field-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `FlowField`. Primary functions: `new()`, `calculate()`, `calculate_multi()`, `get_direction()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: FlowField
items.description: A pre-computed flow field that stores a direction vector and integrated cost for every cell, guiding any unit toward one or more target cells.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `directions` вҖ” `Vec<(f32`. - `f32` вҖ” `:INFINITY` = unreachable).`. - `costs` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `calculated` вҖ” `bool`. - `targets` вҖ” `Vec<(u32`. - `grid` вҖ” `Rc<RefCell<NavGrid>>`.

pathfinding::graph_path.module_doc: Adjacency-graph pathfinding using A* and Dijkstra.  Operates on abstract adjacency graphs: neighbors, centroids, and edge tags. Suitable for province maps, world-graph navigation, or any topology expressible as a sparse adjacency relation. Does not depend on grid or tile structures.

items.name: ProvincePath
items.description: A path through the province adjacency graph.  # Fields - `provinces` вҖ” `Vec<u32>`. - `total_cost` вҖ” `f64`.

items.name: ProvinceCostFn
items.description: Configurable cost function for province pathfinding.  `tag_costs` adds extra cost when crossing an edge with matching tags. `province_costs` adds per-province overrides on top of `default_cost`. `blocked` contains impassable province IDs.  # Fields - `t` вҖ” `1.0).`. - `default_cost` вҖ” `f64`. - `province_costs` вҖ” `HashMap<u32`. - `tag_costs` вҖ” `HashMap<String`. - `blocked` вҖ” `HashSet<u32>`.

items.name: find_province_path
items.description: Find a path between two provinces using A* with centroid distance heuristic.  # Arguments * `neighbors` вҖ” maps each province ID to its neighbor IDs * `centroids` вҖ” maps each province ID to its `(x, y)` centroid position * `edge_tags` вҖ” maps `(min(a,b), max(a,b))` edge keys to their tag sets * `from` вҖ” start province ID * `to` вҖ” goal province ID * `cost_fn` вҖ” cost function configuration  Returns `None` if no path exists.  # Parameters - `neighbors` вҖ” `&HashMap<u32`. - `centroids` вҖ” `&HashMap<u32`.  # Returns `Option<ProvincePath>`.

items.name: province_reachable
items.description: Find all provinces reachable from `start` within a cost budget using Dijkstra.  Returns a map of `province_id вҶ’ cost_to_reach` for all reachable provinces (including `start` at cost 0).  # Parameters - `neighbors` вҖ” `&HashMap<u32`. - `edge_tags` вҖ” `&HashMap<(u32`.  # Returns `HashMap<u32, f64>`.

pathfinding::grid.module_doc: 2D pathfinding grid with A*, Dijkstra, BFS, and flow field generation.  Cells are addressed with 0-based `(x, y)` coordinates. The Lua layer converts to/from 1-based indices.  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for grid-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Grid`. Primary functions: `new()`, `width()`, `height()`, `set_walkable()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Grid
items.description: 2D pathfinding grid with per-cell walkability and movement costs.  Supports A*, Dijkstra, and BFS pathfinding as well as flow field generation. All coordinates are 0-based.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `walkable` вҖ” `Vec<bool>`. - `costs` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`.

pathfinding::hpa.module_doc: Hierarchical Pathfinding Aвҳ… (HPAвҳ…) for fast long-range queries.  Divides the grid into chunks, finds boundary entrances, builds an abstract graph, and searches at the chunk level before refining to tile-level paths.

items.name: AbstractEdge
items.description: An edge in the abstract graph connecting two entrance nodes.  # Fields - `to` вҖ” `usize`. - `cost` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: AbstractNode
items.description: A node in the abstract graph, representing an entrance point on a chunk boundary.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `u32`. - `y` вҖ” `u32`. - `chunk` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`.

items.name: Chunk
items.description: A chunk region of the grid used during abstract graph construction.  # Fields - `col` вҖ” `u32`. - `row` вҖ” `u32`. - `x` вҖ” `u32`. - `y` вҖ” `u32`. - `w` вҖ” `u32`. - `h` вҖ” `u32`. - `entrance_indices` вҖ” `Vec<usize>`.

items.name: AbstractGraph
items.description: Pre-computed abstract graph for hierarchical Aвҳ… queries.  # Fields - `nodes` вҖ” `Vec<AbstractNode>`. - `edges` вҖ” `Vec<Vec<AbstractEdge>>`. - `chunks` вҖ” `HashMap<(u32, u32), Chunk>`. - `grid_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `grid_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `chunk_size` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: build_abstract
items.description: Build the abstract graph from a `NavGrid`.  # Parameters - `grid` вҖ” `&NavGrid`. - `chunk_size` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `AbstractGraph`.  Divides the grid into `chunk_size Г— chunk_size` regions, finds entrance pairs on every inter-chunk boundary, and connects entrances within the same chunk via local Aвҳ….

items.name: hpa_star
items.description: Run HPAвҳ… from `start` to `goal` on the abstract graph, then refine to tiles.  # Parameters - `grid` вҖ” `&NavGrid`. - `abstract_graph` вҖ” `&AbstractGraph`. - `start` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`. - `goal` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`. - `unit_size` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Option<Vec<(u32, u32)>>`.  Returns `None` if no path exists. The returned path is tile-level.

items.name: is_reachable
items.description: Check if `goal` is reachable from `start` using the abstract graph.  # Parameters - `abstract_graph` вҖ” `&AbstractGraph`. - `start` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`. - `goal` вҖ” `(u32, u32)`.  # Returns `bool`.  This is a fast connectivity check вҖ” it doesn't compute the actual path.

pathfinding::influence_map.module_doc: Multi-layer spatial float grid for strategic area analysis and influence mapping.  An [`InfluenceMap`] divides the game world into a uniform grid of cells, each storing one float value per named layer. Layers allow different types of influence to coexist (e.g., `"danger"`, `"resources"`, `"visibility"`) without interfering with each other.  ## Common Operations  - **Stamping**: `stamp_influence()` adds circular influence with linear falloff   at a world-space position (e.g., stamp enemy threat around each enemy unit). - **Propagation**: `propagate()` applies 3Г—3 averaging diffusion to spread values   across the grid, controlled by a `momentum` parameter. - **Decay**: `decay()` multiplies all cells by a factor each frame, causing old   influence to fade over time. - **Querying**: `max_position()`, `min_position()`, and `query_rect()` let AI   agents reason about which areas are most/least dangerous, resourceful, etc. - **Blending**: `blend()` combines two layers with weighted addition into a   destination layer (e.g., `combined = 0.7 * danger + 0.3 * distance`).  ## Coordinate System  The grid uses integer cell coordinates internally, but all public APIs accept and return world-space floating-point coordinates. The `cell_size` parameter controls the mapping between world space and grid space.

items.name: InfluenceMap
items.description: A multi-layer spatial float grid for influence mapping and strategic reasoning.  The grid has fixed dimensions (`width Г— height` cells) with a configurable `cell_size` in world units. Named float layers can be added dynamically. Each layer is a flat `Vec<f32>` of length `width * height`, indexed as `y * width + x`.  Designed for AI systems that need to reason about spatial properties: threat levels, resource density, visibility, territorial control, etc. Multiple layers can be queried and blended to produce composite scores for decision-making.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `usize`. - `height` вҖ” `usize`. - `cell_size` вҖ” `f32`. - `layers` вҖ” `HashMap<String, Vec<f32>>`.

pathfinding.module_doc: Grid-based pathfinding: Aвҳ…, HPAвҳ…, flow fields, and unit-size-aware navigation. Also includes adjacency-graph Aвҳ… for province and world-graph navigation.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ai_flow_field
items.description: AI flow field (simple walkability grid, moved from ai/flowfield).

items.name: astar
items.description: AStar, Dijkstra, and line-of-sight on NavGrid.

items.name: async_pool
items.description: Async pathfinding thread pool for off-thread NavGrid queries.

items.name: flow_field
items.description: Flow field pathfinding for crowd steering (NavGrid-based).

items.name: graph_path
items.description: Adjacency-graph pathfinding (A* and Dijkstra) over abstract neighbor maps. Suitable for province-level and world-graph navigation.

items.name: grid
items.description: 2D walkable grid with A*, Dijkstra, BFS, and flow field generation.

items.name: hpa
items.description: Hierarchical pathfinding A* (HPA*) with abstract graph and level-of-detail.

items.name: influence_map
items.description: Multi-layer spatial float grid for strategic area analysis and influence mapping.

items.name: nav_grid
items.description: Navigation grid with per-cell costs, diagonal modes, and HPA* support.

items.name: pathgrid
items.description: Weighted walkability grid with `Cell` type (moved from ai/pathgrid).

items.name: unit_pathfinder
items.description: Unit-radius-aware pathfinding wrapper over NavGrid.

pathfinding::nav_grid.module_doc: Navigation grid with per-cell traversal costs and diagonal movement modes.  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for nav grid-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `DiagonalMode`, `NavGrid`. Primary functions: `from_lua_str()`, `new()`, `from_costs()`, `get_width()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: DiagonalMode
items.description: Controls how diagonal movement is handled during pathfinding.  # Variants - `None` вҖ” None variant. - `Always` вҖ” Always variant. - `NoCornerCut` вҖ” NoCornerCut variant.

items.name: NavGrid
items.description: A 2D grid of traversal costs used by pathfinding algorithms.  Cells are addressed with 0-based `(x, y)` coordinates in row-major order. A cost of `0` marks a cell as blocked; values `1..=255` represent movement cost.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `costs` вҖ” `Vec<u8>`. - `chunk_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `diagonal_mode` вҖ” `DiagonalMode`. - `dirty_rects` вҖ” `Vec<(u32`.

pathfinding::pathgrid.module_doc: Weighted grid pathfinding (A*, Dijkstra) with obstacle support. Moved from `ai/pathgrid`; used by the Lua `luna.ai.newPathGrid` API.  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for pathgrid-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Cell`, `PathGrid`. Primary functions: `new()`, `in_bounds()`, `set_walkable()`, `is_walkable()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Cell
items.description: Single cell of the navigation grid. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `walkable` вҖ” `bool`. - `cost` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: PathGrid
items.description: Aвҳ… navigation grid. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `usize`. - `height` вҖ” `usize`. - `cell_size` вҖ” `f32`.  Cells are stored row-major, 0-based internally. Supports walkability toggling and per-cell cost multipliers.

pathfinding::unit_pathfinder.module_doc: Unit-aware pathfinder with result caching and convenience methods.  This module is part of Luna2D's `pathfinding` subsystem and provides the implementation details for unit pathfinder-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Waypoint`, `UnitPathfinder`. Primary functions: `new()`, `find_path()`, `find_path_smooth()`, `get_path_length()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Waypoint
items.description: A waypoint along a computed path. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `u32`. - `y` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: UnitPathfinder
items.description: A pathfinder that operates on a shared `NavGrid` with optional result caching.  # Fields - `grid` вҖ” `Rc<RefCell<NavGrid>>`. - `cache` вҖ” `HashMap<CacheKey`. - `cache_order` вҖ” `Vec<CacheKey>`. - `cache_enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `cache_max_size` вҖ” `usize`.

physics::body.module_doc: Body implementation for the `physics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `physics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for body-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `BodyType`, `BodyShape`, `Body`. Primary functions: `new()`, `new_circle()`, `new_polygon()`, `new_edge()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: BodyType
items.description: Determines whether a physics body is affected by forces and gravity.  # Variants - `Static` вҖ” Immovable body; does not respond to gravity or impulses. Used for terrain. - `Dynamic` вҖ” Movable body; subject to gravity, forces, and velocity integration. - `Sensor` вҖ” Trigger volume; detects overlaps but does not resolve collisions.

items.name: BodyShape
items.description: Describes the collision geometry of a body.  # Variants - `Rect { width, height }` вҖ” Axis-aligned rectangle (AABB). Default shape. - `Circle { radius }` вҖ” Circle; uses distance-based collision detection.

items.name: Body
items.description: A rigid body with position, velocity, mass, shape, and restitution.  Bodies live in a `World` and are identified by their index. Use `World::get_body_mut` to access and modify them each frame.  # Fields - `position` вҖ” World-space centre position. - `velocity` вҖ” Linear velocity in world units per second. - `mass` вҖ” Mass in kg. - `body_type` вҖ” Static, Dynamic, Kinematic, or Sensor. - `shape` вҖ” Collision geometry (Rect or Circle). - `restitution` вҖ” Bounciness coefficient. - `layer` / `mask` вҖ” Collision filter bitmasks. - `friction` вҖ” Friction coefficient. - `angle` вҖ” Angular orientation in radians. - `angular_velocity` вҖ” Spin rate in radians/s. - `shape_ext` вҖ” Extended shape override (polygon, edge, chain).

physics::collision.module_doc: Collision implementation for the `physics` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `physics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for collision-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `CollisionInfo`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: CollisionInfo
items.description: Collision contact data: penetration depth and separating normal.  Retained for backward compatibility; collision detection is now handled by rapier2d internally. See `World::get_collision_events()` for events.  # Fields - `penetration` вҖ” How much the two bodies overlap along the minimum separation axis. - `normal` вҖ” Unit vector pointing from body B towards body A.

physics.module_doc: Physics simulation module backed by rapier2d.  Provides rigid-body simulation with circles, rectangles, sensors, raycasting, joints, and collision event recording.  This module is part of Luna2D's `physics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: body
items.description: Body types, shapes, and the `Body` struct used by the physics world.

items.name: collision
items.description: Collision event structs returned by `World::get_collision_events`.

items.name: shape
items.description: Extended shape types: Polygon, Edge, and Chain colliders.

items.name: world
items.description: The `World` struct and all simulation management: bodies, joints, raycasting, contacts.

physics::shape.module_doc: Extended shape types for physics bodies.  Provides polygon, edge, and chain shapes beyond the basic rect/circle.  This module is part of Luna2D's `physics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for shape-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Shape`. Primary functions: `to_rapier_collider()`, `regular_polygon()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Shape
items.description: Extended collision shape for physics bodies.  Goes beyond `BodyShape` to support convex polygons, edges, and chains.  # Variants - `Rect` вҖ” Axis-aligned rectangle with width and height. - `Circle` вҖ” Circle with a radius. - `Polygon` вҖ” Convex polygon (max 8 vertices). - `Edge` вҖ” Line segment between two points. - `Chain` вҖ” Connected chain of edges, optionally closed.

items.name: StandaloneShape
items.description: A standalone shape value holding geometry and default fixture parameters.  Created via `luna.physics.newCircleShape` et al. and attached to bodies with `luna.physics.attachShape`. Can be reused across multiple bodies.  # Fields - `shape` вҖ” `Shape`. The underlying collision geometry. - `density` вҖ” `f32`. Mass density (default 1.0). - `friction` вҖ” `f32`. Surface friction coefficient (default 0.5). - `restitution` вҖ” `f32`. Bounciness: 0 = inelastic, 1 = fully elastic (default 0.0). - `sensor` вҖ” `bool`. If true, the shape detects overlaps without physical response.

physics::world.module_doc: Rapier2d-backed physics world with backward-compatible Luna2D API.  Wraps the rapier2d simulation pipeline while exposing the same `Body`-based interface. The `Body` struct is a sync buffer: mutations via `get_body_mut()` are flushed into rapier on the next `step()` call, and rapier's computed positions/velocities are read back after each step.

items.name: BodyContact
items.description: Collision event generated by `World::step` when two bodies start overlapping.  Contains stable integer body IDs for the two bodies involved.  # Fields - `body_a` вҖ” Index of the first body. - `body_b` вҖ” Index of the second body.

items.name: RaycastHit
items.description: Result of a `World::raycast` query. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `body_id` вҖ” Integer body ID that was hit. - `point` вҖ” World-space hit point `(x, y)`. - `normal` вҖ” World-space surface normal `(nx, ny)`. - `toi` вҖ” Distance along the ray to the hit point.

items.name: ContactInfo
items.description: Contact information between two bodies from the narrow phase.  # Fields - `body_a` вҖ” Index of the first body. - `body_b` вҖ” Index of the second body. - `normal_x` вҖ” Contact normal X component. - `normal_y` вҖ” Contact normal Y component. - `is_touching` вҖ” Whether the bodies are currently touching.

items.name: World
items.description: Simulates a 2D physics world using rapier2d.  Bodies are stored as a `Vec<Body>` for backward-API compatibility. Each `step()` syncs mutations into rapier, runs the pipeline, then reads back positions/velocities for Dynamic bodies.  # Fields - `bodies` вҖ” `Vec<Body>`. - `body_handles` вҖ” `Vec<RigidBodyHandle>`. - `collider_handles` вҖ” `Vec<ColliderHandle>`. - `extra_collider_handles` вҖ” `Vec<Vec<ColliderHandle>>`. - `collider_to_body` вҖ” `HashMap<ColliderHandle`. - `cached_shapes` вҖ” `Vec<BodyShape>`. - `cached_restitutions` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `cached_layers` вҖ” `Vec<(u32`. - `cached_frictions` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `pipeline` вҖ” `PhysicsPipeline`. - `gravity` вҖ” `Vector`. - `params` вҖ” `IntegrationParameters`. - `islands` вҖ” `IslandManager`. - `broad_phase` вҖ” `BroadPhaseBvh`. - `narrow_phase` вҖ” `NarrowPhase`. - `rbodies` вҖ” `RigidBodySet`. - `rcolliders` вҖ” `ColliderSet`. - `impulse_joints` вҖ” `ImpulseJointSet`. - `multibody_joints` вҖ” `MultibodyJointSet`. - `ccd_solver` вҖ” `CCDSolver`. - `joint_handles` вҖ” `Vec<ImpulseJointHandle>`. - `collision_events` вҖ” `Vec<BodyContact>`. - `begin_contact_events` вҖ” `Vec<(usize`. - `end_contact_events` вҖ” `Vec<(usize`. - `joint_types` вҖ” `Vec<&'static str>`. - `mouse_joint_anchors` вҖ” `HashMap<usize`. - `pixels_per_meter` вҖ” `f32`.

pipeline::dag.module_doc: DAG container for pipeline steps: topological sort, cycle detection, parallel grouping.  Key types: `Pipeline`, `ErrorMode`. Primary functions: `add_step`, `validate`, `get_execution_order`, `get_parallel_groups`. Part of the `pipeline` Tier 2 subsystem.

items.name: ErrorMode
items.description: Determines how the pipeline responds when a step fails.  # Variants - `Abort` вҖ” Abort variant. - `Continue` вҖ” Continue variant.

items.name: Pipeline
items.description: A directed acyclic graph (DAG) container that holds pipeline steps and their dependencies.  `Pipeline` owns the step definitions and provides validation and scheduling helpers. Runtime countdown state lives in `PipelineScheduler`.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `error_mode` вҖ” `ErrorMode`.

pipeline.module_doc: DAG-based pipeline orchestrator for composing multi-step workflows.  Provides `Pipeline`, `PipelineStep`, `PipelineResult`, and `PipelineScheduler` as pure-Rust types. The Lua binding lives in `crate::lua_api::pipeline_api`.  Architecture: Tier 2. Depends only on `crate::math` and `crate::engine`. Does not import `mlua` or any other Tier-2 crate module.

items.name: dag
items.description: DAG node and edge types; the `Pipeline` container and its `run` / `run_async` entry points.

items.name: result
items.description: `PipelineResult` and `PipelineStatus` returned after pipeline execution.

items.name: scheduler
items.description: `PipelineScheduler` вҖ” time-based dispatch that triggers pipelines on tick or interval.

items.name: step
items.description: `PipelineStep` definition, `StepStatus`, and `ErrorPolicy` for individual pipeline nodes.

pipeline::result.module_doc: Pipeline execution result: `PipelineResult` and `PipelineStatus`.  Key types: `PipelineResult`, `PipelineStatus`. Part of the `pipeline` Tier 2 subsystem.

items.name: PipelineStatus
items.description: Overall status of a pipeline execution.  # Variants - `Pending` вҖ” Pending variant. - `Running` вҖ” Running variant. - `Completed` вҖ” Completed variant. - `Failed` вҖ” Failed variant. - `Cancelled` вҖ” Cancelled variant.

items.name: PipelineResult
items.description: Aggregated outcome of a complete pipeline run.  # Fields - `status` вҖ” `PipelineStatus`. - `completed` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `failed` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `skipped` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `cancelled` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `total_duration` вҖ” `f32`. - `errors` вҖ” `Vec<(String, String)>`.

pipeline::scheduler.module_doc: Delay countdown and dispatch state for pipeline execution.  Key types: `PipelineScheduler`. Primary functions: `start`, `update`, `mark_step_waiting`. Part of the `pipeline` Tier 2 subsystem.

items.name: PipelineScheduler
items.description: Tracks per-step delay timers and overall wall-clock time for a pipeline run.  # Fields - `delay_timers` вҖ” `HashMap<String, f32>`. - `is_running` вҖ” `bool`. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32`.

pipeline::step.module_doc: Pipeline step definition: `PipelineStep`, `StepStatus`, and `ErrorPolicy`.  Key types: `PipelineStep`, `StepStatus`, `ErrorPolicy`. Part of the `pipeline` Tier 2 subsystem.

items.name: StepStatus
items.description: Execution status of a single pipeline step.  Steps transition through this lifecycle: `Pending` вҶ’ `Waiting` вҶ’ `Running` вҶ’ `Completed` (or `Failed` / `Skipped` / `Cancelled`).  # Variants - `Pending` вҖ” Pending variant. - `Waiting` вҖ” Waiting variant. - `Running` вҖ” Running variant. - `Completed` вҖ” Completed variant. - `Failed` вҖ” Failed variant. - `Skipped` вҖ” Skipped variant. - `Cancelled` вҖ” Cancelled variant.

items.name: ErrorPolicy
items.description: Determines how the pipeline reacts when this step fails.  # Variants - `Abort` вҖ” Abort variant. - `Continue` вҖ” Continue variant. - `Retry` вҖ” Retry variant.

items.name: PipelineStep
items.description: A single node in a pipeline DAG representing one unit of work.  Steps are identified by a unique `name` within their parent `Pipeline`. Runtime fields (`status`, `attempt`, `duration`, `error_msg`) are reset by `PipelineStep::reset()` or `Pipeline::reset()`.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `deps` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `delay` вҖ” `f32`. - `optional` вҖ” `bool`. - `retry_count` вҖ” `u32`. - `retry_delay` вҖ” `f32`. - `on_error` вҖ” `ErrorPolicy`. - `tag` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `metadata` вҖ” `HashMap<String, String>`. - `status` вҖ” `StepStatus`. - `attempt` вҖ” `u32`. - `duration` вҖ” `f32`. - `error_msg` вҖ” `Option<String>`.

procgen::cellular.module_doc: Cellular automata cave and dungeon generation.  Produces a flat grid of wall/open cells via random fill followed by iterative neighbor-count smoothing.

items.name: CellularOpts
items.description: Options for cellular automata generation.  # Fields - `fill` вҖ” `f32`. - `iterations` вҖ” `u32`. - `birth` вҖ” `u32`. - `survive` вҖ” `u32`. - `seed` вҖ” `u64`.

items.name: cellular_automata
items.description: Generates a cave/dungeon map using cellular automata.  # Parameters - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `opts` вҖ” `&CellularOpts`.  # Returns `Vec<u8>`.  Returns a flat `Vec<u8>` of size `width * height` where 1 = wall, 0 = open.

procgen::flood_fill.module_doc: BFS flood fill on a flat grid.  Fills connected cells that match a threshold condition starting from a seed coordinate, returning a binary mask.

items.name: flood_fill
items.description: BFS flood fill on a grid. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `data` вҖ” `&[u8]`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `sx` вҖ” `u32`. - `sy` вҖ” `u32`. - `threshold` вҖ” `u8`. - `above` вҖ” `bool`.  # Returns `Vec<u8>`.  - `data`: flat grid values - `threshold`: fill boundary value - `above`: if true, fill cells >= threshold; if false, fill cells <= threshold  Returns a `Vec<u8>` mask of same size (1 = filled, 0 = not).

procgen::lcg.module_doc: Linear congruential generator for deterministic procgen.  Internal helper used by cellular automata, Voronoi, and Poisson disk modules.

items.name: Lcg
items.description: Simple LCG (Linear Congruential Generator) for deterministic random numbers.

procgen.module_doc: Procedural world generation utilities.  Cave maps, Voronoi diagrams, flood fill, Poisson disk sampling, and periodic Perlin noise for tileable textures.

items.name: cellular
items.description: Cellular automata cave/dungeon generation.

items.name: flood_fill
items.description: BFS flood fill on a flat grid.

items.name: lcg
items.description: Linear congruential generator for deterministic procgen.

items.name: noise_ext
items.description: Periodic Perlin noise for tileable textures.

items.name: poisson
items.description: Poisson disk sampling for point distribution.

items.name: voronoi
items.description: Voronoi diagram generation with optional warp.

procgen::noise_ext.module_doc: Periodic Perlin noise for tileable textures.  Produces smooth noise that wraps seamlessly over a given period in both axes.

items.name: perlin_noise_periodic
items.description: Periodic Perlin noise that tiles over period (px, py).  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” `f64`. - `y` вҖ” `f64`. - `px` вҖ” `f64`. - `py` вҖ” `f64`.  # Returns `f64`.  Returns a value roughly in [-1, 1].

procgen::poisson.module_doc: Poisson disk sampling for point distribution.  Implements Bridson's algorithm to place points with a guaranteed minimum distance between each pair.

items.name: poisson_disk
items.description: Generates Poisson disk sample points using Bridson's algorithm.  # Parameters - `width` вҖ” `f32`. - `height` вҖ” `f32`. - `min_dist` вҖ” `f32`. - `max_attempts` вҖ” `u32`. - `seed` вҖ” `u64`.  # Returns `Vec<(f32, f32)>`.  Returns a list of (x, y) points with minimum distance `min_dist` between them.

procgen::voronoi.module_doc: Voronoi diagram generation with optional warp.  Assigns each pixel in a grid to its nearest seed point, returning region IDs, distances, and second-closest distances.

items.name: VoronoiOpts
items.description: Options for Voronoi diagram generation. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `warp_scale` вҖ” `f32`. - `warp_strength` вҖ” `f32`. - `seed` вҖ” `u64`.

items.name: voronoi_diagram
items.description: Generates a Voronoi diagram. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `points` вҖ” `&[(f32, f32)]`. - `opts` вҖ” `&VoronoiOpts`.  # Returns `(Vec<u32>, Vec<f32>, Vec<f32>)`.  Returns `(regions, distances, second_distances)`: - `regions`: flat `Vec<u32>` mapping each pixel to its closest point index - `distances`: distance to closest point - `second_distances`: distance to second-closest point

raycaster::column_batch.module_doc: Wolfenstein-style raycasting column batch renderer.  Stores per-column wall data produced by a raycaster and provides helpers for updating columns from raw ray data and querying depth.  This module is part of Luna2D's `raycaster` subsystem and provides the implementation details for column batch-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `ColumnData`, `ColumnBatch`. Primary functions: `new()`, `set_column()`, `get_column()`, `update_from_ray_data()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: ColumnData
items.description: Per-column rendering state produced by a raycaster.  # Fields - `tex_u` вҖ” `f32`. - `start` вҖ” `f32`. - `end` вҖ” `f32`. - `shade` вҖ” `f32`. - `cell_val` вҖ” `u32`. - `depth` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: ColumnBatch
items.description: Wolfenstein-style raycasting column batch renderer.  # Fields - `columns` вҖ” `Vec<ColumnData>`. - `screen_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `screen_height` вҖ” `f32`. - `floor_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `ceiling_color` вҖ” `Color`.  Holds an array of column slices sized to the screen width together with floor/ceiling colors. Columns can be set individually or bulk-updated from raw ray data.

raycaster::dda.module_doc: Grid-based raycaster using DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) traversal.  Provides the [`Raycaster2D`] struct for 2D grid-based raycasting used in retro FPS and dungeon-crawler style games. Supports single and multi-ray casting, line-of-sight checks, flat data output, and sprite projection.

items.name: Raycaster2D
items.description: 2D grid-based raycaster using DDA traversal.  The grid stores wall types as `u32` values: 0 = empty, >0 = wall. Coordinates are 0-based with (0,0) at top-left.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `cells` вҖ” `Vec<u32>`.

raycaster::depth_buffer.module_doc: Column-based depth buffer for sprite occlusion.  Provides a simple 1D depth buffer (one depth per screen column) used for correct sprite-vs-wall ordering in raycaster rendering.

items.name: DepthBuffer
items.description: Column-based depth buffer for sprite occlusion.  Stores one depth value per screen column. After rendering walls, each column's depth is recorded so that sprites can be correctly occluded.

raycaster::doors.module_doc: Sliding door support for grid-based raycaster levels.  Provides [`Door`], [`DoorManager`], and related types for implementing Wolfenstein 3D-style sliding doors that open and close over time.

items.name: DoorDirection
items.description: Sliding direction of a door.  # Variants - `Horizontal` вҖ” Horizontal variant. - `Vertical` вҖ” Vertical variant.

items.name: DoorState
items.description: Current animation state of a door.  # Variants - `Closed` вҖ” Closed variant. - `Opening` вҖ” Opening variant. - `Open` вҖ” Open variant. - `Closing` вҖ” Closing variant.

items.name: Door
items.description: Door state in a raycaster level.  Tracks position, animation progress, speed, and direction for a single door.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `u32`. - `y` вҖ” `u32`. - `open_amount` вҖ” `f32`. - `speed` вҖ” `f32`. - `direction` вҖ” `DoorDirection`. - `state` вҖ” `DoorState`.

items.name: DoorManager
items.description: Manages all doors in the level.  Stores a list of [`Door`] objects and drives their open/close animations each frame via [`DoorManager::update()`].

raycaster::heightmap.module_doc: Floor and ceiling height variations for stepped or multi-level environments.  Provides a [`HeightMap`] that stores per-cell floor and ceiling heights, enabling variable-height corridors in grid-based raycaster levels.

items.name: HeightMap
items.description: Per-cell floor and ceiling heights for variable-height levels.  Each cell has a floor height (default 0.0) and ceiling height (default 1.0). These can be used to render stepped floors, raised platforms, and lowered ceilings in dungeon-crawler style environments.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `floor_heights` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `ceiling_heights` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`.

raycaster::lighting.module_doc: Static and dynamic point lighting for raycaster worlds.  Provides simple ambient + point-light illumination suitable for retro FPS and dungeon-crawler style environments.

items.name: PointLight
items.description: Point light source in the raycaster world.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `radius` вҖ” `f32`. - `intensity` вҖ” `f32`. - `color` вҖ” `[f32; 3]`.

items.name: compute_lighting
items.description: Computes ambient + point-light illumination at a world position.  Each light contributes based on inverse-distance falloff within its radius. The result is the sum of ambient light and all point-light contributions, clamped per-channel to [0, 1].  # Parameters - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `ambient` вҖ” `f32`. - `lights` вҖ” `&[PointLight]`.  # Returns `[f32; 3]`.

items.name: apply_lit_shade
items.description: Applies lighting to a distance-shaded base brightness.  Multiplies the base shade value by each channel of the light color, producing a final lit RGB value.  # Parameters - `base_shade` вҖ” `f32`. - `light_color` вҖ” `[f32; 3]`.  # Returns `[f32; 3]`.

raycaster::minimap_overlay.module_doc: Top-down minimap extraction from a raycaster grid.  Provides functions to extract a pixel-based minimap from a [`super::Raycaster2D`] grid, including player position and directional arrow rendering.

items.name: extract_minimap
items.description: Extracts a top-down minimap from a Raycaster2D grid.  Returns flat RGBA pixel data (4 bytes per pixel, row-major) centered on the player position, with a configurable view radius and cell size.  # Parameters - `raycaster` вҖ” `&Raycaster2D`. - `player_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `player_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `player_angle` вҖ” `f32`. - `view_radius` вҖ” `u32`. - `cell_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `wall_color` вҖ” `[u8; 4]`. - `floor_color` вҖ” `[u8; 4]`. - `player_color` вҖ” `[u8; 4]`.  # Returns `(Vec<u8>, u32, u32)`.  Returns `(pixels, pixel_width, pixel_height)`.

items.name: draw_player_arrow
items.description: Renders a simple directional arrow for the player on the minimap.  Draws a small triangle pointing in the player's facing direction, centered at `(center_x, center_y)` in the pixel buffer.  # Parameters - `pixels` вҖ” `&mut [u8]`. - `img_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `center_x` вҖ” `u32`. - `center_y` вҖ” `u32`. - `angle` вҖ” `f32`. - `size` вҖ” `u32`. - `color` вҖ” `[u8; 4]`.

raycaster.module_doc: 2D grid-based raycaster engine for retro FPS and dungeon-crawler games.  Provides DDA ray traversal, segment-based raycasting, visibility polygons, wall column projection, sprite projection, lighting, doors, heightmaps, depth buffering, and minimap extraction.

items.name: column_batch
items.description: Wolfenstein-style raycasting column batch renderer.

items.name: dda
items.description: Grid-based raycaster using DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) traversal.

items.name: depth_buffer
items.description: Column-based depth buffer for sprite occlusion.

items.name: doors
