items.description: Sliding door support for grid-based raycaster levels.

items.name: heightmap
items.description: Floor and ceiling height variations for stepped or multi-level environments.

items.name: lighting
items.description: Static and dynamic point lighting for raycaster worlds.

items.name: minimap_overlay
items.description: Top-down minimap extraction from a raycaster grid.

items.name: projection
items.description: Wall column projection and distance-based shading.

items.name: ray_hit
items.description: Ray hit result from DDA grid traversal.

items.name: segment
items.description: Line segment definition and ray-segment intersection testing.

items.name: sprite_projection
items.description: Sprite screen-space projection for billboard rendering.

items.name: visibility
items.description: Visibility polygon via endpoint raycasting.

raycaster::projection.module_doc: Wall column projection and distance-based shading.  Provides functions for projecting wall distances to screen-space drawing parameters and computing distance-based brightness attenuation.

items.name: project_column
items.description: Projects a wall column distance to screen-space drawing parameters.  # Parameters - `distance` вҖ” `f32`. - `fov` вҖ” `f32`. - `screen_height` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(f32, f32, f32)`.  Returns (wall_height, draw_start, draw_end).

items.name: distance_shade
items.description: Distance-based shading. Returns brightness in [0, 1].  # Parameters - `distance` вҖ” `f32`. - `max_distance` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `f32`.  `(1 - distance / max_distance)` clamped to [0, 1].

raycaster::ray_hit.module_doc: Ray hit result from DDA grid traversal.  Contains the [`RayHit`] struct returned by [`super::Raycaster2D::cast_ray()`] and [`super::Raycaster2D::cast_rays()`].

items.name: RayHit
items.description: Result of a single ray cast.  Returned by the DDA raycaster when a ray hits a wall or reaches its maximum distance without hitting anything (`hit = false`).  # Fields - `distance` вҖ” `f32`. - `raw_distance` вҖ” `f32`. - `cell_value` вҖ” `u32`. - `side` вҖ” `u8`. - `tex_u` вҖ” `f32`. - `hit_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `hit_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `hit` вҖ” `bool`.

raycaster::segment.module_doc: Line segment definition and ray-segment intersection testing.  Provides the [`Segment`] type and [`cast_ray_2d()`] free function for casting rays against a list of line segments in 2D space.

items.name: Segment
items.description: A line segment for raycasting.  Defines a line from `(x1, y1)` to `(x2, y2)` in world space.  # Fields - `x1` вҖ” `f32`. - `y1` вҖ” `f32`. - `x2` вҖ” `f32`. - `y2` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: cast_ray_2d
items.description: Casts a ray from (ox, oy) in direction (dx, dy) against a list of segments.  # Parameters - `ox` вҖ” `f32`. - `oy` вҖ” `f32`. - `dx` вҖ” `f32`. - `dy` вҖ” `f32`. - `max_dist` вҖ” `f32`. - `segments` вҖ” `&[Segment]`.  # Returns `Option<(f32, f32, usize)>`.  Returns `Some((hit_x, hit_y, segment_index))` for the nearest hit within `max_dist`, or `None` if no segment is hit.

raycaster::sprite_projection.module_doc: Sprite screen-space projection for billboard rendering.  Contains the [`SpriteProjection`] struct returned by [`super::Raycaster2D::project_sprite()`].

items.name: SpriteProjection
items.description: Sprite projection result.  Describes where and how large to draw a billboard sprite on screen, as computed by the raycaster's camera transform.  # Fields - `screen_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `scale` вҖ” `f32`. - `distance` вҖ” `f32`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`.

raycaster::visibility.module_doc: Visibility polygon via endpoint raycasting.  Computes a visibility polygon from a point by casting rays at segment endpoints with small angular offsets, producing a sorted polygon outline.

items.name: field_of_view
items.description: Computes a visibility polygon by casting rays at segment endpoints.  # Parameters - `ox` вҖ” `f32`. - `oy` вҖ” `f32`. - `segments` вҖ” `&[Segment]`. - `radius` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `Vec<f32>`.  Casts rays towards each segment endpoint (plus small angular offsets) within `radius`, sorts the hit points by angle, and returns a flat polygon `[x0, y0, x1, y1, ...]`.

savegame.module_doc: Save/load slot system with collectors, schema versioning, dirty tracking, and auto-save.

items.name: SlotMeta
items.description: Metadata extracted from a save slot.  # Fields - `slot` вҖ” `String`. Slot name. - `timestamp` вҖ” `f64`. Unix epoch timestamp. - `version` вҖ” `i32`. Schema version. - `summary` вҖ” `String`. Optional summary string.

items.name: SaveManager
items.description: Pure-data save manager providing registration of named collectors, schema versioning, dirty-state tracking, and auto-save timer.  Actual serialisation and filesystem calls happen on the Lua side; this struct tracks the bookkeeping.

items.name: serialize_table
items.description: Serialize a simple Lua-compatible value hierarchy into a `return { ... }` string.  Supports nil, bool, number (f64), string, and nested tables (HashMap). Does not handle userdata, functions, or circular references.  # Parameters - `data` вҖ” `&HashMap<String, SaveValue>`. The table data to serialize. - `depth` вҖ” `u32`. Current nesting depth (internal; call with `0`).  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

items.name: serialize_value
items.description: Serialize a single value.  # Parameters - `value` вҖ” `&SaveValue`. The value to serialize. - `depth` вҖ” `u32`. Current nesting depth.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

items.name: SaveValue
items.description: A simple value type matching the Lua subset we can serialize.  # Variants - `Nil` вҖ” Lua nil. - `Bool` вҖ” Lua boolean. - `Number` вҖ” Lua number. - `Str` вҖ” Lua string. - `Table` вҖ” Lua table (string keys only for save data).

savegame::save_data.module_doc: Save/load slot system with collectors, schema versioning, dirty tracking, and auto-save.  This module is part of Luna2D's `savegame` subsystem and provides the implementation details for save data-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SlotMeta`, `SaveManager`, `SaveValue`. Primary functions: `new()`, `register()`, `unregister()`, `registered_names()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SlotMeta
items.description: Metadata extracted from a save slot. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `slot` вҖ” `String`. - `timestamp` вҖ” `f64`. - `version` вҖ” `i32`. - `summary` вҖ” `String`.

items.name: SaveManager
items.description: Pure-data save manager providing registration of named collectors, schema versioning, dirty-state tracking, and auto-save timer.  Actual serialisation and filesystem calls happen on the Lua side; this struct tracks the bookkeeping.  # Fields - `schema_version` вҖ” `i32`. - `registered` вҖ” `Vec<String>`. - `dirty` вҖ” `bool`. - `auto_save` вҖ” `Option<(f64`. - `auto_save_elapsed` вҖ” `f64`. - `migration_versions` вҖ” `Vec<i32>`.

items.name: serialize_table
items.description: Serialize a simple Lua-compatible value hierarchy into a `return { ... }` string.  # Parameters - `data` вҖ” `&HashMap<String, SaveValue>`. - `depth` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.  Supports nil, bool, number (f64), string, and nested tables (HashMap). Does not handle userdata, functions, or circular references.

items.name: serialize_value
items.description: Serialize a single value. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Parameters - `value` вҖ” `&SaveValue`. - `depth` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

items.name: SaveValue
items.description: A simple value type matching the Lua subset we can serialize.  # Variants - `Nil` вҖ” Nil variant. - `Bool` вҖ” Bool variant. - `Number` вҖ” Number variant. - `Str` вҖ” Str variant. - `Table` вҖ” Table variant.

scene::depth_sorter.module_doc: Per-frame depth-sorted draw batcher.  This module is part of Luna2D's `scene` subsystem and provides the implementation details for depth sorter-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `DepthEntry`, `DepthSorter`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add()`, `add_object()`, `sort()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: DepthEntry
items.description: Entry in the depth-sorted draw queue. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `depth` вҖ” `f32`. - `callback_index` вҖ” `usize`. - `is_object` вҖ” `bool`.

items.name: DepthSorter
items.description: Per-frame depth-sorted draw batcher. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Collects draw callbacks with depth values, sorts them, and flushes them in ascending depth order each frame.  # Fields - `entries` вҖ” `Vec<DepthEntry>`.

scene.module_doc: Scene stack module for managing game scene lifecycle, transitions, and depth-sorted rendering.  Provides a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with lifecycle callbacks (`enter`, `leave`, `pause`, `resume`, `update`, `draw`), animated transitions (fade, slide), a named scene registry, inter-scene data passing, and a `DepthSorter` for z-ordered draw batching.

items.name: depth_sorter
items.description: Z-order depth sorter for draw-call batching; groups scene objects by depth layer before rendering.

items.name: stack
items.description: LIFO scene stack with lifecycle callbacks (`enter`, `leave`, `pause`, `resume`, `update`, `draw`) and a named scene registry.

items.name: transition
items.description: Animated scene transitions with configurable types (fade, slide) and easing, plus inter-scene data passing.

scene::stack.module_doc: LIFO scene stack with registry and inter-scene data store.  This module is part of Luna2D's `scene` subsystem and provides the implementation details for stack-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `SceneStack`. Primary functions: `new()`, `next_scene_id()`, `push()`, `pop()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: SceneId
items.description: Unique identifier for a scene in the stack.

items.name: SceneStack
items.description: The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of scene references.  Scenes are identified by `SceneId` values. The Lua API layer maps these to `mlua::RegistryKey` references for actual Lua table access.  # Fields - `stack` вҖ” `Vec<SceneId>`. - `registry` вҖ” `HashMap<String`. - `data_keys` вҖ” `HashMap<String`. - `transition` вҖ” `Option<ActiveTransition>`. - `next_id` вҖ” `u64`.

scene::transition.module_doc: Visual transition types and active transition state for scene changes.  This module is part of Luna2D's `scene` subsystem and provides the implementation details for transition-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TransitionType`, `ActiveTransition`. Primary functions: `from_lua_str()`, `new()`, `progress()`, `is_complete()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TransitionType
items.description: Visual transition types between scenes. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `None` вҖ” None variant. - `Fade` вҖ” Fade variant. - `SlideLeft` вҖ” SlideLeft variant. - `SlideRight` вҖ” SlideRight variant. - `SlideUp` вҖ” SlideUp variant. - `SlideDown` вҖ” SlideDown variant.

items.name: ActiveTransition
items.description: Active transition state tracking progress between two scenes.  # Fields - `transition_type` вҖ” `TransitionType`. - `duration` вҖ” `f32`. - `elapsed` вҖ” `f32`.

serial::csv.module_doc: CSV parsing and serialization for Luna2D.  Converts between CSV strings and `SerialValue` using the `csv` crate. Parses CSV rows into ordered maps keyed by header columns.

items.name: CsvOptions
items.description: Options for CSV parsing and serialization.  # Fields - `delimiter` вҖ” `u8`. Field separator byte (default `b','`). - `has_headers` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the first row is a header row (default `true`).

items.name: from_csv
items.description: Parse a CSV string into a `SerialValue`.  Returns a `SerialValue::Seq` of rows. Each row is a `SerialValue::Map` keyed by column headers when `has_headers` is true, or a `SerialValue::Seq` of string values otherwise.  # Parameters - `s` вҖ” `&str`. CSV text. - `opts` вҖ” `CsvOptions`. Parse options.  # Returns `Result<SerialValue, String>`.

items.name: to_csv
items.description: Serialize a `SerialValue` to a CSV string.  Converts a `SerialValue::Seq` of `SerialValue::Map` rows to CSV.  # Parameters - `val` вҖ” `&SerialValue`. A sequence of maps (rows). - `opts` вҖ” `CsvOptions`. Serialization options.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

serial::json.module_doc: JSON parsing and serialization for Luna2D.  Converts between JSON strings and `SerialValue` using `serde_json`.

items.name: from_json
items.description: Parse a JSON string into a `SerialValue`.  # Parameters - `s` вҖ” `&str`. JSON text.  # Returns `Result<SerialValue, String>`.

items.name: to_json
items.description: Serialize a `SerialValue` to a JSON string.  # Parameters - `val` вҖ” `&SerialValue`. Value to serialize. - `pretty` вҖ” `bool`. Use pretty-printed output if true.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

serial::lua_table.module_doc: `SerialValue`: shared intermediate representation for all serial format modules.  All format modules (json, toml, csv, yaml) convert to/from `SerialValue`. `Lua-table` is the canonical name because the primary use case is bridging between Lua tables and text serialization formats.

items.name: SerialValue
items.description: A Luna2D serializable value вҖ” the common intermediate representation shared by all serial format modules.  # Variants - `Null` вҖ” Absent or nil value. - `Bool` вҖ” Boolean. - `Int` вҖ” 64-bit signed integer. - `Float` вҖ” 64-bit floating-point. - `Str` вҖ” UTF-8 string. - `Seq` вҖ” Ordered sequence of values. - `Map` вҖ” Ordered string-keyed map of values.

items.name: to_lua
items.description: Converts a `SerialValue` tree into a Lua value tree.  # Parameters - `lua` вҖ” `&Lua`. The active Lua state. - `val` вҖ” `&SerialValue`. The value to convert.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaValue<'lua>>`.

items.name: from_lua
items.description: Converts a Lua value tree into a `SerialValue` tree.  # Parameters - `val` вҖ” `&LuaValue`. The Lua value to convert.  # Returns `LuaResult<SerialValue>`.

serial.module_doc: Format-agnostic serialization: JSON, TOML, CSV, and Lua tables.  Provides `SerialValue` as a common intermediate representation and per-format modules that parse/serialize strings to/from `SerialValue`. No file I/O вҖ” callers supply strings and receive strings back. YAML has been removed вҖ” use TOML for human-authored config (design-assumption B-05).

items.name: csv
items.description: CSV parsing and serialization via the csv crate.

items.name: json
items.description: JSON parsing and serialization via serde_json.

items.name: lua_table
items.description: Shared intermediate representation for all serial formats.

items.name: toml
items.description: TOML parsing and serialization via the toml crate.

serial::toml.module_doc: TOML parsing and serialization for Luna2D.  Converts between TOML strings and `SerialValue` using the `toml` crate. Provides the functionality previously in `data::toml_convert`.

items.name: from_toml
items.description: Parse a TOML string into a `SerialValue`.  # Parameters - `s` вҖ” `&str`. TOML text.  # Returns `Result<SerialValue, String>`.

items.name: to_toml
items.description: Serialize a `SerialValue` to a TOML string.  # Parameters - `val` вҖ” `&SerialValue`. Value to serialize (must be a `Map` at root).  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

serial::yaml.module_doc: YAML parsing and serialization for Luna2D.  Converts between YAML strings and `SerialValue` using `serde_yml`.

items.name: from_yaml
items.description: Parse a YAML string into a `SerialValue`.  # Parameters - `s` вҖ” `&str`. YAML text.  # Returns `Result<SerialValue, String>`.

items.name: to_yaml
items.description: Serialize a `SerialValue` to a YAML string.  # Parameters - `val` вҖ” `&SerialValue`. Value to serialize.  # Returns `Result<String, String>`.

items.name: Bone
items.description: A single bone in a skeletal hierarchy.  Each bone stores a local transform relative to its parent (or to the skeleton root if `parent_index` is `None`). After `Skeleton::update_world_transforms()`, the `world_*` fields contain the final world-space transform.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. Unique bone name. - `parent_index` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Index of parent bone, or `None` for root. - `local_x` вҖ” `f32`. Local X offset from parent. - `local_y` вҖ” `f32`. Local Y offset from parent. - `local_rotation` вҖ” `f32`. Local rotation in radians. - `local_scale_x` вҖ” `f32`. Local X scale factor. - `local_scale_y` вҖ” `f32`. Local Y scale factor. - `world_x` вҖ” `f32`. Computed world-space X (updated by `update_world_transforms`). - `world_y` вҖ” `f32`. Computed world-space Y. - `world_rotation` вҖ” `f32`. Computed world-space rotation. - `world_scale_x` вҖ” `f32`. Computed world-space X scale. - `world_scale_y` вҖ” `f32`. Computed world-space Y scale.

spine.module_doc: Skeletal animation вҖ” bone hierarchies, slots, and world-transform propagation.  The spine module provides a hierarchical bone system for 2D skeletal animation. Bones form a parent-child tree; calling `Skeleton::update_world_transforms()` propagates local transforms down the hierarchy to produce world-space positions for rendering.

items.name: bone
items.description: Bone hierarchy node: local transform (translation, rotation, scale), parent pointer, and child list.

items.name: skeleton
items.description: `Skeleton` root type: manages the bone tree and calls `update_world_transforms()` to compute world-space positions.

items.name: slot
items.description: Attachment slot that links a bone to a displayable resource (sprite, mesh, or point attachment).

items.name: BoneParams
items.description: Parameters for creating and adding a bone in one call.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. Bone name. - `parent_index` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Parent bone index, or `None` for root. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. Local X offset. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. Local Y offset. - `rotation` вҖ” `f32`. Local rotation in radians. - `scale_x` вҖ” `f32`. Local X scale. - `scale_y` вҖ” `f32`. Local Y scale.

items.name: Skeleton
items.description: A skeletal animation rig composed of a bone hierarchy and render slots.  The skeleton owns a flat array of `Bone`s where parent indices point into earlier entries (bones must be added in topological order вҖ” parent before child). Calling `update_world_transforms()` propagates local transforms down the hierarchy to produce world-space positions.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. Skeleton name. - `bones` вҖ” `Vec<Bone>`. Bone array (parent indices index into this vec). - `slots` вҖ” `Vec<Slot>`. Render slots bound to bones. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. World-space root X position. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. World-space root Y position. - `scale_x` вҖ” `f32`. Root X scale. - `scale_y` вҖ” `f32`. Root Y scale.

items.name: Slot
items.description: A slot binding a visual attachment to a bone in the skeleton.  Slots determine which bone drives an attachment's position and define the draw order and tint colour for that attachment.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. Unique slot name. - `bone_index` вҖ” `usize`. Index of the bone this slot is bound to. - `color_r` вҖ” `f32`. Red tint (0вҖ“1). - `color_g` вҖ” `f32`. Green tint (0вҖ“1). - `color_b` вҖ” `f32`. Blue tint (0вҖ“1). - `color_a` вҖ” `f32`. Alpha (0вҖ“1). - `attachment_name` вҖ” `Option<String>`. Name of the current attachment, or `None`. - `draw_order` вҖ” `i32`. Z-sort key for rendering.

terminal::cell.module_doc: Cell data types for the terminal grid.

items.name: DEFAULT_FG
items.description: Default foreground colour (white, fully opaque).

items.name: DEFAULT_BG
items.description: Default background colour (black, fully transparent).

items.name: DEFAULT_CH
items.description: Default character (space).

items.name: TCell
items.description: A single character cell in the terminal grid.  Each cell stores a Unicode codepoint and two RGBA float colours вҖ” one for the foreground glyph and one for the background fill.  # Fields - `ch` вҖ” `u32`. Unicode codepoint (default: U+0020, space). - `fg` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Foreground RGBA colour (default: white). - `bg` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. Background RGBA colour (default: transparent black).

terminal.module_doc: Grid-based character-cell terminal emulator and widget toolkit.  The `terminal` module provides a 2D grid of [`TCell`] records together with a lightweight widget system for text-mode UIs such as roguelikes, debug consoles, and retro adventure interfaces.  Grid dimensions are capped at 512 columns by 256 rows. All public coordinate parameters are 1-based while internal storage is 0-based.  ## Tier  `terminal` is a Tier 2 engine extension. It may depend on `math`, `engine`, and Tier 1 modules. It must not import other Tier 2 modules.

terminal::terminal_state.module_doc: Terminal grid state and input handling.

items.name: MAX_COLS
items.description: Maximum number of columns a terminal grid may have.

items.name: MAX_ROWS
items.description: Maximum number of rows a terminal grid may have.

items.name: TerminalEvent
items.description: Internal terminal event emitted by input routing.  # Variants - `ButtonClicked` вҖ” Button activation event with the widget index. - `TextChanged` вҖ” Text input change event with the widget index. - `SelectionChanged` вҖ” List selection change event with the widget index.

items.name: Terminal
items.description: A grid-based character-cell terminal emulator with widget support.  The terminal maintains a 2D grid of [`TCell`] records and a flat list of [`Widget`] values that overlay interactive controls on top of the grid.  # Fields - `cols` вҖ” `usize`. Number of columns. - `rows` вҖ” `usize`. Number of rows. - `grid` вҖ” `Vec<TCell>`. Flat grid storage (`cols * rows`). - `cursor_col` вҖ” `usize`. Cursor column (0-based internal). - `cursor_row` вҖ” `usize`. Cursor row (0-based internal). - `widgets` вҖ” `Vec<Widget>`. Attached widget list. - `focused` вҖ” `Option<usize>`. Index of the focused widget.

terminal::widget.module_doc: Widget types for the terminal UI system.

items.name: BorderStyle
items.description: Line-drawing style for [`WidgetKind::Border`] widgets.  # Variants - `Single` вҖ” Single-line box-drawing characters (default). - `Double` вҖ” Double-line box-drawing characters. - `Ascii` вҖ” Plain ASCII characters (`+`, `-`, `|`).

items.name: WidgetBase
items.description: Shared base fields for all terminal widgets.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `usize`. Column position (0-based internal). - `y` вҖ” `usize`. Row position (0-based internal). - `width` вҖ” `usize`. Width in cells. - `height` вҖ” `usize`. Height in cells. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the widget is drawn. - `enabled` вҖ” `bool`. Whether the widget accepts input. - `tag` вҖ” `String`. Free-form identification tag.

items.name: WidgetKind
items.description: Concrete widget type discriminant.  # Variants - `Label` вҖ” Static or dynamic text label. - `Button` вҖ” Clickable button. - `TextBox` вҖ” Editable single-line text input. - `List` вҖ” Scrollable list of selectable items. - `Border` вҖ” Decorative border frame with optional title. - `Panel` вҖ” Container that groups child widgets.

items.name: Widget
items.description: A terminal widget combining shared [`WidgetBase`] fields with a concrete [`WidgetKind`] variant.  # Fields - `base` вҖ” `WidgetBase`. Shared position, size, visibility, and tag. - `kind` вҖ” `WidgetKind`. Type-specific data.

thread::channel.module_doc: Thread-safe channel for Lua inter-thread communication.  Provides an MPMC (multi-producer, multi-consumer) queue that can be shared between the main game thread and background Lua threads via `Arc<Channel>`. Only primitive Lua values (nil, bool, number, string) can cross thread boundaries.

items.name: ChannelValue
items.description: Serializable values that can be sent between threads.  # Variants - `Nil` вҖ” Nil variant. - `Bool` вҖ” Bool variant. - `Number` вҖ” Number variant. - `String` вҖ” String variant.  Only Lua-native primitive types are supported; UserData, tables, and functions cannot cross thread boundaries.

items.name: Channel
items.description: Thread-safe MPMC channel for Lua inter-thread communication.  Internally uses a `Mutex<VecDeque>` protected queue with a `Condvar` for blocking `demand()` calls. Safe to wrap in `Arc` and share across OS threads.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `queue` вҖ” `Mutex<VecDeque<ChannelValue>>`. - `condvar` вҖ” `Condvar`. - `push_count` вҖ” `Mutex<u64>`.

items.name: LuaChannel
items.description: Lua UserData wrapper for a thread-safe channel.  # Fields - `inner` вҖ” `Arc<Channel>`.  Holds an `Arc<Channel>` so the underlying channel can be shared across threads while each Lua VM holds its own `LuaChannel` handle.

items.name: lua_to_channel_value
items.description: Convert a Lua value into a `ChannelValue` for cross-thread transfer.  # Parameters - `value` вҖ” `LuaValue`.  # Returns `LuaResult<ChannelValue>`.  Only nil, boolean, number (integer or float), and string are supported. Returns a Lua runtime error for unsupported types.

items.name: channel_value_to_lua
items.description: Convert a `ChannelValue` back into a Lua value.  # Parameters - `lua` вҖ” `&Lua`. - `value` вҖ” `ChannelValue`.  # Returns `LuaResult<LuaValue<'_>>`.

thread.module_doc: Thread infrastructure: inter-thread channel and background Lua worker.  This module is part of Luna2D's `thread` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: channel
items.description: Channel sub-module.

items.name: worker
items.description: Worker sub-module.

thread::worker.module_doc: Background Lua thread with independent VM.  Each `LuaThread` spawns an OS thread running its own `mlua::Lua` instance. Communication with the main thread happens exclusively through `Channel` objects вҖ” no Lua state is shared across threads.

items.name: ThreadState
items.description: Execution state of a background Lua thread.  # Variants - `Pending` вҖ” Pending variant. - `Running` вҖ” Running variant. - `Completed` вҖ” Completed variant. - `Error` вҖ” Error variant.

items.name: LuaThread
items.description: A background Lua thread running its own VM.  Created via `luna.thread.newThread(code)`. Call `start()` to spawn the OS thread and `wait()` to block until completion. Errors are captured in `ThreadState::Error` and retrievable via `get_error()`.  # Fields - `code` вҖ” `String`. - `state` вҖ” `Arc<Mutex<ThreadState>>`. - `handle` вҖ” `Option<thread::JoinHandle<()>>`. - `channels` вҖ” `Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String`.

tilemap::autotile_sheet.module_doc: AutoTileSheet вҖ” loads a pre-arranged autotile image and generates TileSet tiles + bitmask rules.  ## 48-tile quarter-tile format  For high-quality composite autotile rendering, [`AutoTileSheet`] supports a 48-tile sub-tile format where each full tile is assembled from **four quarter-tile pieces** (TL, TR, BL, BR).  The source image must be **`3*tile_w` Г— `4*tile_h` pixels** вҖ” a 6-column Г— 8-row grid of `(tile_w/2 Г— tile_h/2)` quarter pieces.  Call [`AutoTileSheet::get_quarter_rects`] with the raw 8-bit neighbour bitmask to obtain the four [`Rect`]s to blit.  Use [`AutoTileSheet::get_composite48_grid_quad`] when the source atlas stores the 48 pre-composed tile variants in a 6 Г— 8 full-tile grid instead.  ### Bitmask bit layout (raw 8-bit)  ```text bit 0 = N   bit 4 = NE bit 1 = E   bit 5 = SE bit 2 = S   bit 6 = SW bit 3 = W   bit 7 = NW ```

items.name: AutoTileLayout
items.description: Predefined autotile sheet layout variants.  # Variants - `Blob47` вҖ” Blob47 variant. - `Composite48` вҖ” 48-tile composite variant. - `Minimal16` вҖ” Minimal16 variant.

items.name: AutoTileSheet
items.description: An autotile sheet that maps layout conventions to bitmask-based tile selection.  Stores the bitmaskвҶ”tile-index mapping tables for a given [`AutoTileLayout`] and can apply the resulting rules to a [`TileSet`].

tilemap::chunk.module_doc: Chunk-based tilemap storage for large and infinite maps.  [`ChunkMap`] stores tiles in fixed-size square chunks using a [`HashMap`]. Only chunks that have been written to are allocated, allowing sparse, large, or infinite maps without up-front memory commitment.  Tile coordinates use `i32` to support negative positions (e.g., a map centred at the origin where negative X/Y are valid map space).

items.name: ChunkMap
items.description: A chunk-based tilemap that supports large and infinite maps through sparse storage.  Tiles are stored in square chunks of `chunk_size Г— chunk_size` tiles.  Chunks are allocated on first write and can be individually unloaded to reclaim memory. Reads from unloaded chunks return [`ChunkMap::DEFAULT_GID`] (0).  # Fields - `chunk_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `chunks` вҖ” `HashMap<(i32`.

tilemap::coords.module_doc: Coordinate conversion functions for isometric and hexagonal tile grids.  This module is part of Luna2D's `tilemap` subsystem and provides the implementation details for coords-related operations and data management. Primary functions: `to_screen_iso()`, `from_screen_iso()`, `iso_rotate()`, `iso_direction_name()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: to_screen_iso
items.description: Converts tile coordinates to screen position using diamond isometric projection.  # Parameters - `tx` вҖ” `f32`. - `ty` вҖ” `f32`. - `tile_w` вҖ” `f32`. - `tile_h` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `Vec2`.  - `screen.x = (tx - ty) * tile_w / 2` - `screen.y = (tx + ty) * tile_h / 2`

items.name: from_screen_iso
items.description: Converts screen position back to tile coordinates for diamond isometric projection.  # Parameters - `sx` вҖ” `f32`. - `sy` вҖ” `f32`. - `tile_w` вҖ” `f32`. - `tile_h` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `Vec2`.

items.name: iso_rotate
items.description: Rotates an isometric direction (1вҖ“4) clockwise by `steps`.  # Parameters - `direction` вҖ” `i32`. - `steps` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `i32`.  Directions: 1=south, 2=west, 3=north, 4=east.

items.name: iso_direction_name
items.description: Returns the name of an isometric direction (1вҖ“4).  # Parameters - `direction` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `&'static str`.  Returns `"unknown"` for out-of-range values.

items.name: iso_direction_from_angle
items.description: Snaps an angle (in radians) to the nearest isometric direction (1вҖ“4).  # Parameters - `angle` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `i32`.  - south (down, ПҖ/2) вҶ’ 1 - west (left, ПҖ) вҶ’ 2 - north (up, -ПҖ/2) вҶ’ 3 - east (right, 0) вҶ’ 4

items.name: to_screen_hex
items.description: Converts axial hex coordinates to screen position (pointy-top layout).  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`. - `size` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `Vec2`.  - `x = size * вҲҡ3 * (q + r/2)` - `y = size * 3/2 * r`

items.name: from_screen_hex
items.description: Converts screen position back to axial hex coordinates (pointy-top layout).  # Parameters - `sx` вҖ” `f32`. - `sy` вҖ” `f32`. - `size` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.  Uses fractional axial reverse, then rounds with [`hex_round`].

items.name: hex_neighbors
items.description: Returns the six axial neighbor offsets for pointy-top hexagonal grids.  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `[(i32, i32); 6]`.

items.name: hex_distance
items.description: Returns the hex distance between two axial coordinates using cube distance.  # Parameters - `q1` вҖ” `i32`. - `r1` вҖ” `i32`. - `q2` вҖ” `i32`. - `r2` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `i32`.

items.name: hex_round
items.description: Rounds fractional axial coordinates to the nearest hex cell using cube rounding.  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `f32`. - `r` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.

items.name: hex_line
items.description: Returns all hex cells along a line between two axial coordinates.  # Parameters - `q1` вҖ” `i32`. - `r1` вҖ” `i32`. - `q2` вҖ” `i32`. - `r2` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.  Uses linear interpolation in cube space with [`hex_round`] at each step.

items.name: hex_ring
items.description: Returns all cells at exactly `radius` distance from `(q, r)`.  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`. - `radius` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.  For radius 0, returns just the center cell.

items.name: hex_spiral
items.description: Returns all hex cells from center outward to `radius`, ring by ring.  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`. - `radius` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.

items.name: hex_area
items.description: Returns all hex cells within `radius` distance (filled hex circle).  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`. - `radius` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `Vec<(i32, i32)>`.

items.name: hex_rotate
items.description: Rotates hex coordinates `(q, r)` around `(center_q, center_r)` by `steps Г— 60В°` clockwise.  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`. - `center_q` вҖ” `i32`. - `center_r` вҖ” `i32`. - `steps` вҖ” `i32`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.  Uses cube coordinate rotation.

items.name: hex_reflect
items.description: Reflects hex coordinates across an axis through the center.  # Parameters - `q` вҖ” `i32`. - `r` вҖ” `i32`. - `center_q` вҖ” `i32`. - `center_r` вҖ” `i32`. - `axis` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.  Axis must be `"q"`, `"r"`, or `"s"`. Uses cube coordinate reflection.

tilemap::isomap.module_doc: Multi-level isometric tilemap for OpenXcom-style rendering.  Each map cell holds four tile parts rendered in painter's algorithm order: `Floor`, `NorthWall`, `WestWall`, `Object`. Z-levels stack vertically with a configurable pixel offset so higher floors appear above lower ones.

items.name: IsoTilePart
items.description: The four sub-slots within each isometric map cell, rendered in this order.  # Variants - `Floor` вҖ” Floor variant. - `NorthWall` вҖ” NorthWall variant. - `WestWall` вҖ” WestWall variant. - `Object` вҖ” Object variant.  Matching OpenXcom's FLOOR вҶ’ NORTH_WALL вҶ’ WEST_WALL вҶ’ OBJECT sequence ensures correct painter's algorithm occlusion within a single cell.

items.name: IsoTile
items.description: One map cell containing four GIDs, one per [`IsoTilePart`].  # Fields - `parts` вҖ” `[u32; 4]`.  A GID of `0` means the slot is empty вҖ” `draw_iter` still yields the item so callers can decide whether to skip it.

items.name: IsoLevel
items.description: One Z-level of the isometric map вҖ” a 2-D grid of [`IsoTile`]s.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`.

items.name: IsoDrawItem
items.description: One renderable item produced by [`IsoMap::draw_iter`] in painter's order.  # Fields - `level` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_x` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_y` вҖ” `u32`. - `part` вҖ” `u32`. - `gid` вҖ” `u32`. - `screen_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `screen_y` вҖ” `f32`.  `level`, `tile_x`, `tile_y`, and `part` are all **0-based** Rust values. The Lua API converts them to 1-based before passing them to scripts.

items.name: IsoMap
items.description: Multi-level isometric tilemap with painter's-algorithm draw iteration.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_w` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_h` вҖ” `u32`. - `level_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `origin_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `origin_y` вҖ” `f32`.  # Coordinate system  ```text screen_x = origin_x + (tx - ty) * tile_w / 2 screen_y = origin_y + (tx + ty) * tile_h / 2 - tz * level_height ```  # Draw order  [`draw_iter`](IsoMap::draw_iter) yields items in the correct painter's algorithm order for a standard isometric view with the camera positioned at the top-left corner:  1. Diagonal `d = tx + ty` increases from `0` to `(W-1) + (H-1)`. 2. For each diagonal, tiles iterate in ascending `tx` order. 3. For each tile, Z-levels iterate from bottom (`0`) to `active_z`. 4. For each Z-level, parts iterate Floor вҶ’ NorthWall вҶ’ WestWall вҶ’ Object.

tilemap::large_map_renderer.module_doc: Optimized renderer for large tile-based maps with chunking and LOD.  [`LargeMapRenderer`] stores a flat tile array and divides it into [`MapChunk`]s for efficient culling. Dirty flags allow incremental rebuilds when individual tiles change. The renderer tracks camera position, zoom, and viewport to compute which chunks are visible.

items.name: MapChunk
items.description: A chunk of tiles pre-batched for fast culling and rendering.  # Fields - `cx` вҖ” `i32`. - `cy` вҖ” `i32`. - `dirty` вҖ” `bool`. - `tile_ids` вҖ” `Vec<u32>`.  Each chunk covers a `chunk_size Г— chunk_size` region of tiles.

items.name: LargeMapRenderer
items.description: Optimized large tile-map renderer with chunked culling.  # Fields - `tile_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `map_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `map_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `tileset_columns` вҖ” `u32`. - `chunk_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `camera_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `camera_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `camera_zoom` вҖ” `f32`. - `viewport_w` вҖ” `f32`. - `viewport_h` вҖ” `f32`. - `lod_enabled` вҖ” `bool`. - `lod_thresholds` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`.  Tiles are stored in a flat `Vec<u32>` (row-major). The renderer splits them into square chunks and exposes helpers to determine which chunks are visible for a given camera position, zoom, and viewport.

tilemap::mapgen.module_doc: Procedural map generation вҖ” MapBlock, MapGroup, MapScript, MapGen.  This module is part of Luna2D's `tilemap` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mapgen-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Edge`, `MapBlock`, `MapGroup`, `StepType`, `ScriptStep`. Primary functions: `from_str()`, `as_str()`, `new()`, `set_tile()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Edge
items.description: Cardinal edge direction for block-segment connectivity.  # Variants - `North` вҖ” North variant. - `East` вҖ” East variant. - `South` вҖ” South variant. - `West` вҖ” West variant.

items.name: MapBlock
items.description: A prefab grid of tiles that can be stamped into a generated map.  Contains multi-layer tile data and per-segment edge connection IDs.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `layers` вҖ” `u32`. - `segment_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `weight` вҖ” `f32`. - `tile_data` вҖ” `Vec<Vec<u32>>`. - `sides` вҖ” `HashMap<(Edge`.

items.name: MapGroup
items.description: A biome-like container holding [`MapBlock`] prefabs and [`MapScript`] generators.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `blocks` вҖ” `Vec<MapBlock>`. - `scripts` вҖ” `Vec<MapScript>`.

items.name: StepType
items.description: The type of operation a [`ScriptStep`] performs.  # Variants - `FillRandom` вҖ” FillRandom variant. - `PlaceBlock` вҖ” PlaceBlock variant. - `PlaceRandom` вҖ” PlaceRandom variant. - `PlaceLine` вҖ” PlaceLine variant. - `FloodFill` вҖ” FloodFill variant. - `FillArea` вҖ” FillArea variant. - `DrawPath` вҖ” DrawPath variant. - `FillRect` вҖ” FillRect variant.

items.name: ScriptStep
items.description: A single step in a [`MapScript`] with rich configuration.  # Fields - `step_type` вҖ” `StepType`. - `group_index` вҖ” `i32`. - `block_index` вҖ” `i32`. - `x` вҖ” `u32`. - `y` вҖ” `u32`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `count` вҖ” `u32`. - `rotation` вҖ” `u32`. - `mirror` вҖ” `bool`. - `random_rotation` вҖ” `bool`. - `random_mirror` вҖ” `bool`. - `direction` вҖ” `u32`. - `match_sides` вҖ” `bool`. - `condition_step` вҖ” `i32`. - `condition_success` вҖ” `bool`. - `chance` вҖ” `f32`. - `repeat_count` вҖ” `u32`. - `min_count` вҖ” `i32`. - `max_count` вҖ” `i32`. - `size_filter_w` вҖ” `i32`. - `size_filter_h` вҖ” `i32`. - `tile_id` вҖ” `u32`. - `path_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_layer` вҖ” `u32`. - `zone_start_y` вҖ” `i32`. - `zone_end_y` вҖ” `i32`.

items.name: MapScript
items.description: A named sequence of [`ScriptStep`]s that drives procedural generation.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `steps` вҖ” `Vec<ScriptStep>`.

items.name: MapOrientation
items.description: Map orientation for visual layout hints.  # Variants - `TopDown` вҖ” TopDown variant. - `SideView` вҖ” SideView variant.

items.name: LayerMode
items.description: How layers are managed during generation.  # Variants - `Unified` вҖ” Unified variant. - `Independent` вҖ” Independent variant.

items.name: MapSize
items.description: Predefined map size presets expressed in segment-grid units.  # Variants - `Small` вҖ” Small variant. - `Medium` вҖ” Medium variant. - `Large` вҖ” Large variant. - `Custom` вҖ” Custom variant.

items.name: MapZone
items.description: A named horizontal zone within a generated map.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `start_row` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: MapGen
items.description: Top-level procedural map generator. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  Produces a [`TileMap`] by stamping [`MapBlock`] prefabs according to [`MapScript`] steps, using deterministic randomness.  # Fields - `grid_w` вҖ” `u32`. - `grid_h` вҖ” `u32`. - `segment_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_pixel_w` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_pixel_h` вҖ” `u32`. - `orientation` вҖ” `MapOrientation`. - `layer_mode` вҖ” `LayerMode`. - `zones` вҖ” `Vec<MapZone>`. - `last_placement_count` вҖ” `u32`. - `seed` вҖ” `u64`.

See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: autotile_sheet
items.description: Auto-tile atlas with bitmask-based tile selection.

items.name: chunk
items.description: Sparse chunk-based tilemap storage for large and infinite maps.

items.name: coords
items.description: Coordinate helpers for tile вҶ” world conversions.

items.name: isomap
items.description: Multi-level isometric tilemap with painter's-algorithm draw order.

items.name: mapgen
items.description: Procedural map generation scripts and room placement.

items.name: tilemap
items.description: Core tilemap: layers, tile CRUD, sweep queries, and serialization.

items.name: tileset
items.description: Tile set definition with per-tile properties and animation frames.

items.name: tmx
items.description: Tiled TMX/TSX map format parser.

items.name: large_map_renderer

tilemap::polygon_map.module_doc: Polygon map renderer with region management and hit detection.  Manages a collection of named polygon regions with colors, labels, highlighting, and point-in-polygon queries.  This module is part of Luna2D's `graphics` subsystem and provides the implementation details for polygon map-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `PolygonRegion`, `PolygonMap`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add_region()`, `remove_region()`, `set_region_color()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: PolygonRegion
items.description: A named polygon region. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `vertices` вҖ” `Vec<f32>`. - `color` вҖ” `Color`. - `label` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `font_size` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: PolygonMap
items.description: Polygon map renderer with region management and hit detection.  # Fields - `outline_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `outline_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `highlight_color` вҖ” `Color`. - `highlighted` вҖ” `Option<String>`.  Stores named polygon regions and supports point-in-polygon queries, highlighting, and bounding-box computation. Regions are keyed by name.

tilemap::tile_walker.module_doc: Tile-based first-person movement controller with cardinal directions.  This module is part of Luna2D's `tilemap` subsystem and provides the implementation details for tile walker-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Facing`, `TileWalker`. Primary functions: `parse()`, `to_str()`, `angle()`, `dx()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Facing
items.description: Cardinal facing direction. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `North` вҖ” North variant. - `East` вҖ” East variant. - `South` вҖ” South variant. - `West` вҖ” West variant.

items.name: TileWalker
items.description: Tile-based movement controller for first-person grid navigation.  Operates on integer grid coordinates with cardinal facing directions.  # Fields - `x` вҖ” `i32`. - `y` вҖ” `i32`. - `facing` вҖ” `Facing`. - `prev_x` вҖ” `i32`. - `prev_y` вҖ” `i32`. - `prev_facing` вҖ” `Facing`.

tilemap::tilemap.module_doc: Main tile map container with layers, tiles, viewport, collision, and autotile support.  This module is part of Luna2D's `tilemap` subsystem and provides the implementation details for tilemap-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TileLayer`, `SweepResult`, `TileMap`. Primary functions: `new()`, `add_tileset()`, `get_tileset()`, `get_tileset_count()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TileLayer
items.description: A single layer of tiles in a [`TileMap`].  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. - `tint` вҖ” `[f32; 4]`. - `offset` вҖ” `Vec2`. - `parallax` вҖ” `Vec2`.  Tiles are stored in row-major order. GID 0 means "empty / no tile".

items.name: SweepResult
items.description: Result of a swept-AABB collision test against solid tiles.  # Fields - `contact_point` вҖ” `Vec2`. - `normal` вҖ” `Vec2`. - `tile_x` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_y` вҖ” `u32`. - `t` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: TileMap
items.description: A 2D tile map composed of layers, tilesets, and viewport-clipped rendering state.  All tile coordinates are **0-based**. Lua helpers add 1-based conversion externally.  # Fields - `tile_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `chunk_size` вҖ” `u32`. - `orientation` вҖ” `MapOrientation`. - `tilesets` вҖ” `Vec<TileSet>`. - `layers` вҖ” `Vec<TileLayer>`. - `viewport` вҖ” `Option<Rect>`. - `anim_timers` вҖ” `HashMap<u32`.

tilemap::tileset.module_doc: Tile set backed by a texture atlas with animation, solid flags, and autotile rules.  This module is part of Luna2D's `tilemap` subsystem and provides the implementation details for tileset-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `TileAnimFrame`, `TileSet`. Primary functions: `new()`, `get_first_gid()`, `get_tile_count()`, `get_columns()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: TileAnimFrame
items.description: A single frame in a tile animation sequence.  # Fields - `tile_id` вҖ” `u32`. - `duration_ms` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: TileSet
items.description: A tile set that maps local tile IDs to atlas regions, animations, and collision flags.  Tile IDs are 0-based within the set. The `first_gid` maps this set into the global GID space used by [`super::TileMap`].  # Fields - `first_gid` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_count` вҖ” `u32`. - `columns` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `spacing` вҖ” `u32`. - `margin` вҖ” `u32`. - `solids` вҖ” `Vec<bool>`. - `animations` вҖ” `HashMap<u32`. - `auto_rules_4` вҖ” `HashMap<(String`. - `auto_rules_8` вҖ” `HashMap<(String`.

tilemap::tmx.module_doc: Tiled TMX map format loader.  Parses `.tmx` files produced by the [Tiled Map Editor](https://www.mapeditor.org/). TMX is an XML-based format; this module uses [`roxmltree`] for parsing.  Supports: - Orthogonal, isometric, staggered, and hexagonal map orientations. - Tile data encoding: XML elements, CSV, and Base64 (with optional zlib/gzip compression). - Inline and external tileset references (TSX source returned as a path string for caller resolution). - Object layers and image layers (parsed to metadata; not converted to [`TileMap`] data).  Does NOT support: - Infinite maps (chunk-based TMX format): treat as a warning; use [`ChunkMap`] directly. - Embedded TSX tileset files вҖ” the caller must load them separately.  [`ChunkMap`]: super::chunk::ChunkMap

items.name: TmxOrientation
items.description: Rendering orientation of the map, as specified in the TMX `orientation` attribute.  # Variants - `Orthogonal` вҖ” Orthogonal variant. - `Isometric` вҖ” Isometric variant. - `Staggered` вҖ” Staggered variant. - `Hexagonal` вҖ” Hexagonal variant.

items.name: TmxStaggerAxis
items.description: The axis along which isometric / hexagonal tiles are staggered.  # Variants - `X` вҖ” X variant. - `Y` вҖ” Y variant.

items.name: TmxTileset
items.description: A tileset reference embedded in a TMX map.  # Fields - `first_gid` вҖ” `u32`. - `source` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `tile_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `spacing` вҖ” `u32`. - `margin` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_count` вҖ” `u32`. - `columns` вҖ” `u32`. - `image_source` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `image_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `image_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `solid_tiles` вҖ” `Vec<u32>`.  If `source` is `Some`, the tileset data lives in an external `.tsx` file and only `first_gid` is available here.  If `source` is `None` the tileset is inline and all other fields are populated.

items.name: TmxTileLayer
items.description: A standard tile layer from a TMX map.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. - `opacity` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `tiles` вҖ” `Vec<u32>`.

items.name: TmxObjectLayer
items.description: An object layer (object group) from a TMX map.  # Fields - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `visible` вҖ” `bool`. - `objects` вҖ” `Vec<TmxObject>`.

items.name: TmxObject
items.description: A single Tiled object within an object layer.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `u32`. - `name` вҖ” `String`. - `obj_type` вҖ” `String`. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`. - `width` вҖ” `f32`. - `height` вҖ” `f32`. - `gid` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: TmxLayer
items.description: Variant tag for TMX map layers. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Variants - `Tile` вҖ” Tile variant. - `Object` вҖ” Object variant.

items.name: TmxMap
items.description: A fully-parsed TMX map. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.  # Fields - `width` вҖ” `u32`. - `height` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_width` вҖ” `u32`. - `tile_height` вҖ” `u32`. - `orientation` вҖ” `TmxOrientation`. - `stagger_axis` вҖ” `Option<TmxStaggerAxis>`. - `hex_side_length` вҖ” `u32`. - `tilesets` вҖ” `Vec<TmxTileset>`. - `layers` вҖ” `Vec<TmxLayer>`. - `background_color` вҖ” `Option<[u8; 4]>`.

items.name: load_tmx
items.description: Parses a TMX file given its XML content as a string.  # Parameters - `xml` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `Result<TmxMap, String>`.  Returns a fully-populated [`TmxMap`], or an error string describing the problem.  ### Tile data encoding - **XML**: `<tile gid="вҖҰ"/>` child elements вҖ” always supported. - **CSV**: Comma-separated list of GIDs вҖ” supported without extra dependencies. - **Base64**: Base64-encoded little-endian `u32` array вҖ” supported via the `base64` crate. - **Base64 + zlib**: Further decompressed with `flate2` вҖ” supported. - **Base64 + gzip**: Gzip-decompressed with `flate2` вҖ” supported. - **Base64 + zstd**: Not supported (returns an error).

timer::clock.module_doc: Clock implementation for the `timer` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `timer` subsystem and provides the implementation details for clock-related operations and data management. Key types exported from this module: `Clock`. Primary functions: `new()`, `tick()`, `delta()`, `total()`.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: Clock
items.description: Tracks per-frame delta time, accumulated total time, and a rolling FPS measurement.  # Fields - `start_time` вҖ” `Instant`. - `last_frame` вҖ” `Instant`. - `delta` вҖ” `f64`. - `total` вҖ” `f64`. - `frame_count` вҖ” `u64`. - `fps` вҖ” `f64`. - `fps_timer` вҖ” `f64`. - `fps_frame_count` вҖ” `u64`. - `delta_buffer` вҖ” `[f64; AVERAGE_DELTA_WINDOW]`. - `delta_buffer_index` вҖ” `usize`. - `delta_buffer_filled` вҖ” `bool`.

timer.module_doc: Mod implementation for the `timer` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `timer` subsystem and provides the implementation details for mod-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

items.name: clock
items.description: Frame-based clock providing delta time, total time, and FPS.

items.name: scheduler
items.description: Scheduled event manager for delayed and repeating timed callbacks.

items.name: sleep
items.description: Suspends the current thread for the given number of seconds.  Values вүӨ 0 are ignored. This is a simple convenience wrapper around [`std::thread::sleep`].  # Parameters - `seconds` вҖ” `f64`.

timer::scheduler.module_doc: Scheduled event manager for delayed and repeating timed callbacks.  Provides [`Scheduler`] for one-shot delays, repeating timers, named events, individual pause/resume, global time-scale, and on-the-fly interval changes.  ## Usage ```rust,no_run # use luna2d::timer::Scheduler; let mut sched = Scheduler::new(); let id = sched.after(2.0);          // fires once after 2 seconds let rid = sched.every(0.5, -1);     // fires every 0.5 s indefinitely sched.set_time_scale(0.5);          // run at half speed let fired = sched.update(0.1);      // advance; returns IDs that fired sched.cancel(rid); ```

items.name: ScheduledEvent
items.description: A single scheduled event with optional name and pause state.  # Fields - `id` вҖ” `u32`. - `name` вҖ” `Option<String>`. - `remaining` вҖ” `f64`. - `interval` вҖ” `f64`. - `count` вҖ” `i32`. - `one_shot` вҖ” `bool`. - `paused` вҖ” `bool`.

items.name: Scheduler
items.description: Manages a collection of timed events (one-shot and repeating).  Each event has an integer ID (returned on creation) that can be used for cancellation, pause/resume, and property reads. Named events can also be cancelled by their string name.  The global `time_scale` multiplier compresses or stretches all timers. A scale of `0.5` makes everything run at half speed; `2.0` doubles speed. Individual events can be paused with [`Scheduler::pause`].

window::event_loop.module_doc: Event Loop implementation for the `window` subsystem.  This module is part of Luna2D's `window` subsystem and provides the implementation details for event loop-related operations and data management.  All public items are documented. See the parent module for architectural context and the `luna.*` Lua API for the scripting interface.

window::management.module_doc: Window management commands вҖ” title, fullscreen, vsync, position, size, minimize, maximize, close, icon, focus query, and DPI scale.  Every function takes `&WindowState` or `&mut WindowState` directly.  No winit calls are made here.  Deferred operations are stored in `pending_*` fields and executed by `engine::app::App` at the start of the next frame.

items.name: ModeInfo
items.description: Information about the current window mode.  # Fields - `fullscreen` вҖ” `bool`. - `fullscreen_type` вҖ” `&'static str`. - `vsync` вҖ” `i32`.

items.name: set_title
items.description: Schedules a window title change for the next frame.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `title` вҖ” `&str`.

items.name: set_fullscreen
items.description: Schedules a fullscreen mode change.  `flag` = `true` to enter fullscreen, `false` to exit. `mode` = `"exclusive"` for exclusive fullscreen; anything else uses borderless desktop mode.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `flag` вҖ” `bool`. - `mode` вҖ” `&str`.

items.name: is_fullscreen
items.description: Returns whether the window is currently in fullscreen mode.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: set_vsync
items.description: Schedules a VSync mode change.  `1` = Fifo (vsync on), `0` = Immediate (vsync off), `-1` = Mailbox (adaptive vsync).  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `mode` вҖ” `i32`.

items.name: get_vsync
items.description: Returns the current VSync mode integer.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `i32`.

items.name: get_dpi_scale
items.description: Returns the DPI scale factor of the display the window is on.  `1.0` on standard displays; `2.0` or higher on HiDPI / Retina displays.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `f64`.

items.name: get_position
items.description: Returns the current window position in screen coordinates as `(x, y)`.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `(i32, i32)`.

items.name: set_position
items.description: Schedules a window position change to `(x, y)` in screen coordinates.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `x` вҖ” `i32`. - `y` вҖ” `i32`.

items.name: minimize
items.description: Schedules a window minimize (iconify) operation.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`.

items.name: maximize
items.description: Schedules a window maximize operation.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`.

items.name: restore
items.description: Schedules a window restore from minimized or maximized state.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`.

items.name: is_minimized
items.description: Returns whether the window is currently minimized.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: is_maximized
items.description: Returns whether the window is currently maximized.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: has_focus
items.description: Returns whether the window currently has keyboard focus.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: request_attention
items.description: Schedules a user-attention request (taskbar flash on Windows / dock bounce on macOS).  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`.

items.name: close
items.description: Schedules window closure on the next frame, exiting the game loop.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`.

items.name: set_icon
items.description: Schedules a window icon change from the given file path.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `path` вҖ” `&str`.

items.name: set_size
items.description: Schedules a window resize to `(w, h)` logical pixels.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `w` вҖ” `u32`. - `h` вҖ” `u32`.

items.name: get_fullscreen_type_str
items.description: Returns the fullscreen type as a lowercase string.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

items.name: get_fullscreen
items.description: Returns the fullscreen state and type as a `(bool, &str)` pair.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `(bool, &'static str)`.

items.name: is_visible
items.description: Returns whether the window is currently visible.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: has_mouse_focus
items.description: Returns whether the mouse cursor is inside the window.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `bool`.

items.name: to_dpi_pixels
items.description: Converts a device-independent value to physical pixels using the DPI scale.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`. - `value` вҖ” `f64`.  # Returns `f64`.

items.name: from_dpi_pixels
items.description: Converts a physical pixel value to device-independent coordinates.  Returns the input unchanged if the DPI scale is zero.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`. - `value` вҖ” `f64`.  # Returns `f64`.

items.name: get_pixel_dimensions
items.description: Returns the window dimensions in physical pixels (logical size Г— DPI scale).  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`. - `win_w` вҖ” `u32`. - `win_h` вҖ” `u32`.  # Returns `(u32, u32)`.

items.name: set_mode
items.description: Schedules a combined window mode change (size + optional fullscreen + optional vsync).  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `w` вҖ” `u32`. - `h` вҖ” `u32`. - `fullscreen` вҖ” `Option<bool>`. - `fstype` вҖ” `Option<&str>`. - `vsync` вҖ” `Option<i32>`.

items.name: get_mode
items.description: Returns the current window mode settings.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `ModeInfo`.

items.name: show_message_box
items.description: Shows a platform-native message box dialog.  `box_type`: `"info"`, `"warning"`, or `"error"` (default `"info"`). `btn_type`: `"ok"`, `"okcancel"`, or `"yesno"` (default `"ok"`).  Returns the button the user pressed: `"ok"`, `"yes"`, `"no"`, or `"cancel"`.  # Parameters - `title` вҖ” `&str`. - `message` вҖ” `&str`. - `box_type` вҖ” `&str`. - `btn_type` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `&'static str`.

window.module_doc: Provides pure Rust functions for reading and writing [`crate::engine::shared_state::WindowState`] fields.  **No winit calls are made in this module.**  Deferred window operations are placed in `pending_*` fields and consumed by `engine::app::App` at the start of the next frame.  ## Files  | File | Scope | |------|-------| | `management` | Title, fullscreen, vsync, position, size, minimize, maximize, close, icon | | `viewport` | Logical dimensions, scale mode, pixel вҶ” game-space coordinate conversion | | `event_loop` | Reserved for future platform-specific event loop integration |

items.name: management
items.description: Window management commands: title, fullscreen, position, size, minimize, maximize, close, icon.

items.name: viewport
items.description: Viewport utilities: logical dimensions, scale mode, pixel вҶ” game-space conversion.

items.name: event_loop
items.description: Reserved for future platform-specific event loop integration.

window::viewport.module_doc: Viewport and coordinate-space utilities вҖ” logical game dimensions, scale mode, and pixel вҶ” game-space coordinate conversion.  `viewport_scale_x / _y` and `viewport_offset_x / _y` are computed by `engine::app` whenever the window is resized or the scale mode changes.  These functions treat the pre-computed values as read-only; callers should use [`set_scale_mode`] to request a change.

items.name: get_width
items.description: Returns the logical game width in virtual pixels.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: get_height
items.description: Returns the logical game height in virtual pixels.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `f32`.

items.name: get_scale_mode
items.description: Returns the current viewport scale mode string.  One of `"none"`, `"letterbox"`, `"stretch"`, or `"pixel"`.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `&str`.

items.name: set_scale_mode
items.description: Schedules a viewport scale mode change.  Accepted values: `"none"`, `"letterbox"`, `"stretch"`, `"pixel"`.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `mode` вҖ” `&str`.

items.name: to_pixels
items.description: Converts game-space coordinates `(x, y)` to window pixel coordinates.  Applies the pre-computed viewport scale and offset produced by `engine::app` during the last resize event.  Returns `(0.0, 0.0)` if no viewport transform has been set.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.

items.name: from_pixels
items.description: Converts window pixel coordinates `(x, y)` back to game-space coordinates.  Returns `(0.0, 0.0)` on either axis where the viewport scale is zero to avoid division by zero during early frames before the first resize event.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`. - `x` вҖ” `f32`. - `y` вҖ” `f32`.  # Returns `(f32, f32)`.

items.name: ScaleInfo
items.description: Viewport scale and offset information.  # Fields - `scale_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `scale_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_x` вҖ” `f32`. - `offset_y` вҖ” `f32`. - `game_width` вҖ” `f32`. - `game_height` вҖ” `f32`.

items.name: get_scale_info
items.description: Returns the current viewport scale and offset information.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&WindowState`.  # Returns `ScaleInfo`.

items.name: set_scale_mode_validated
items.description: Validates and schedules a viewport scale mode change.  Returns `true` if the mode was valid and set, `false` otherwise. Logs a warning on invalid mode.  # Parameters - `ws` вҖ” `&mut WindowState`. - `mode` вҖ” `&str`.  # Returns `bool`.
